title: Abba Father
firstLine: Abba, Father, send your Spirit
verses: I will give you living water
If you seek me, you will find me...
If you listen, you will hear me!
Come, my children, I will teach you...
I am your shepherd, I will lead you...
Peace I leave you, peace I give you...
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Abide With Me
firstLine: Abide with me; fast falls the eventide:
verses: Abide with me; fast falls the eventide:
The darkness deepens, Lord, with me abide;
When other helpers fail, and comforts flee,
Help of the helpless, O abide with me.
Swift to its close ebbs out life's little day;
Earth's joys grow dim, its glories pass away;
Change and decay in all around I see;
O thou who changest not, abide with me.
I need thy presence every passing hour;
What but thy grace can fool the tempter's power?
Who like thyself my guide and stay can be?
Through cold and sunshine, Lord, abide with me.
I fear no foe with thee at hand to bless;
Ills have no weight and tears no bitterness,
Where is death's sting? Where grave thy victory?
I triumph still, if thou abide with me.
Hold thou thy Cross before my closing eyes;
Shine through the gloom and point me to the skies;
Heaven's morning breaks, and earth's vain shadows flee;
In life, in death, O Lord abide with me.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

At hic latet simul et humanitas:
Ambo tamen credens, atque confitensPeto quod
Fac me tibi semper magis credere,
In te spem habere, te diligere.
Praesta meae menti de te vivere

title: All Glory Praise And Honour
firstLine: All glory, praise, and honour to thee,
verses: All glory, praise, and honour to thee,
To whom the lips of children
All glory, praise, and honour To thee,
True King art thou, Lord Jesus,
The saints and angels praise thee, While men on earth proclaim
Thy rule of love and mercy,
In hearts that bless thy name!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: All Hail The Power Of Jesus' Name
firstLine: All hail the power of Jesus' name,
verses: All hail the power of Jesus' name,
And crown Him Lord of all.
Ye chosen seed of Israel's race,
Hail Him who saves you by His grace,
And crown Him Lord of all.
Sinners whose love can never forget
His feet And crown Him Lord of all.
sacred throng We at His feet may fall;
And crown Him Lord of all.
O that with yonder sacred throng
We at His feet may fall;
And crown Him Lord of all.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: All Man's Labour
firstLine: All man's labour can produce,
verses: You have given for our use,
May this lowly, bread and wine
On this gift of bread and wine, cont'd
May this food which strength imparts
And a taste of joy to come.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: All Of My Life
firstLine: All of my life, I will praise the living God,
verses: All of my life, I will praise the living God,
My heart full of joy, I will bless His holy name.
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
He saves us from all harm.
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
He fills our life with good.
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
He died to bring us life.
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
He is the Bread of life.
Bless the Lord, O my soul!
In him we find all love.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: All People That On Earth (Psalm 100)
firstLine: All people that on earth do dwell,
verses: All people that on earth do dwell,
Sing to the Lord with cheerful voice;
Him serve with fear, his praise forth tell.
Come ye before him, and rejoice.
Know that the Lord is God indeed;
Without our aid he did us make;
We are his flock, he doth us feed,
And for his sheep he doth us take.
O enter then his gates with praise;
Approach with joy his courts so true;
Laud, and bless His name always.
For it is seemly so to do.
For why, the Lord our God is good;
His truth at all times firmly stood,
And shall from age to age endure.
To Father, Son and Holy Ghost,
The God whom heaven and earth adore.
From men and from the angel host
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: All That I Am
firstLine: All that I am, all that I do,
verses: All that I am, all that I do,
All that I'll will ever have I offer now to you.
All that i dream, all that I pray,
All that I'll ever make I give to you today.
Take and sanctify these gifts for your honour lord.
Knowing that i Love and serve You is enough reward.
All that I am, all that I do,
All that I'll will ever have I offer now to you.
All that i dream, all that I pray,
All that I'll ever make I give to you today.
Take and sanctify these gifts for your honour lord.
Knowing that i Love and serve You is enough reward.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: All The Earth Proclaim The Lord(psalm 100)
firstLine: All the earth proclaim the Lord,
verses: All the earth proclaim the Lord,
Serve you the Lord, heart filled with gladness,
Come into His Presence singing for joy!
Know that the Lord is our Creator.
Yes, He is our Father; we are His sons.
We are the sheep of His green pasture,
For we are His people; He is our God.
O enter His courts while singing His praise.
Our Lord is good, His love enduring,
His word is abiding now with all people.
Honour and praise be to the Father,
The Son, and the Spirit, world without end.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: All To Jesus I Surrender
firstLine: All to Jesus I surrender,
verses: All to Him I freely give;
I will ever love and trust Him,
I surrender all, I surrender all.
All to Thee, my blessed Saviour,
Humbly at His feet I bow;
Take me Jesus, take me now.
Lord, I give myself to Thee;
Fill me with Thy love and power,
Let Thy blessing fall on me.
Now I feel the sacred flame;
Oh, the joy of full salvation!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: All You Who Seek A Comfort Sure
firstLine: All you who seek a comfort sure
verses: All you who seek a comfort sure
Jesus, who gave Himself for you
Opens to you His Sacred Heart;
Oh, to that heart draw nigh.
You hear how kindly He invites;
You hear His words so blest:
" All you that labour, come to Me,
And I will give you rest."
What meeker than the Saviour's heart
As on the Cross He lay?
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

firstLine: Alleluia, Alleluia, Alleluia,
verses: Praise the Lord for it's good.
Praise the Lord for it's good.
For He is the Lord, Lord God of all;
Alleluia; praise the Lord for it's good.
Alleluia; praise the Lord for it's good.
Alleluia; praise the Lord for it's good.
Alleluia; praise the Lord for it's good.
For his mercy is without end.
Alleluia; praise the Lord for it's good.
Alleluia; praise the Lord for it's good.
For all His favours to all mankind.
Alleluia; praise the Lord for it's good.
Alleluia; praise the Lord for it's good.
Praise the Lord, for it's good.
Glory be to the Father and to the Son,
and to the Spirit, now for ever more,

porta manes, et stella maris, succúrre cadénti
súrgere qui curat pópulo: tu quae genuísti,
Virgo prius ac postérius, Gabriélis ab ore

title: Almighty Father Take This Bread
firstLine: Almighty Father take this bread
verses: Where sins divide us take instead,
The wine we offer soon will be Christ's blood,
Receive it Holy Trinity, this holy sacrifice
O God, by angels' choir adored,
your name be praised on earth;
On all men be that peace outpoured
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Although The Way Be Lonely
firstLine: Although the way be lonely,.
verses: I sing through the shade and the sunshine,
I sing, for I cannot be silent,
It may be the way He chooses,
For well He knows the pathway
A day of light and gladness,
On which no shade will fall;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Amazing Grace (Psalm 100)
firstLine: Amazing grace! how sweet the sound!
verses: Amazing grace! how sweet the sound!
That saved a wretch like me;
I once was lost, but now am found;
Was blind, but now I see.
'Twas grace that taught my heart to fear,
How precious did that grace appear,
Through many dangers, toils and snares,
'Tis grace that brought me safe thus far,
And grace will lead me home.
Yes, when this heart and flesh shall fail,
I shall possess within the veil,
A life of joy and peace.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Angels From The Realms Of Glory
firstLine: Angels, from the reams of glory,
verses: Angels, from the reams of glory,
Wing your flight o'r all the earth;
Come and worship, come and worship,
Watching o'r your flocks by night.
God in man is now residing:
Come and worship, come and worship,
Seek the great Desire of nations;
Ye have seen his natal star,
Come and worship, come and worship,
Watching long in hope and fear,
Come and worship, come and worship,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Angels We Have Heard On High
firstLine: Angels we have heard on high,
verses: Angels we have heard on high,
Him whose birth the angles sing;
Christ the Lord, the new-born king.
See Him in a manger laid
Whom the choirs of angels praise
While our hearts in love we raise.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Anima Christi
firstLine: Anima Christi, sanctifica me,
verses: In hora mortis meae voca me,
Et jube me venire ad te,
Et cum Sanctis tuis laudem te
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

verses: Anyi abiawo ikele gi gozie anyi Chukwu (2x)
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Are Ye Able? Said The Master
firstLine: Are ye able'' said the Master,
verses: Are ye able'' said the Master,
" To the death, we follow Thee."
Lord, we are able, our Spirits are Thine,
Remould them, make us like Thee, divine:
Thy guiding radiance above us shall be,
A beacon to God, to love and loyalty.
O heart! thou joy of saints on high!
Attracted by those loving words, To thee I lift my prayer.
Wash thou my wounds in that dear blood
Which forth from thee doth flow;
New grace, new hope inspire, a new
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Arise And Shine
firstLine: Arise and shine for the Lord is coming,
verses: Arise and shine for the Lord is coming,
Arise and shine for the Lord is coming,
Arise and shine for the Lord is coming,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Arise Come To Your God (Psalm 100)
firstLine: Arise, come to your God,
verses: Sing Him your songs of rejoicing.
Cry out with joy to the Lord all the earth,
Come before Him singing for joy.
Know that He the Lord is God,
He made us we belong to him,
We are His people, the sheep of His flock.
Go within his gates giving thanks,
Enter His courts with songs of praise,
Give thanks to Him and bless His name
Indeed how good is the Lord
He is faithful from age to age
Give glory to the Father Almighty,
To his Son, Jesus Christ, the Lord,
To the Spirit who dwells in our hearts.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Around The Altar
firstLine: Around the altar, we sing and
verses: Around the altar, we sing and
Our joy is full in Christ.
Take His body, singing, praying altogether,
Drink the blood, singing, praying altogether,
This meal that is given by Christ Jesus
Whoever shares it will never die,
Help us, Jesus to fully understand
Very grateful, for your death for the world.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: As I Kneel Before You
firstLine: As I kneel before you,
verses: As I bow my head in prayer,
Take this day, make it yours
And fill me with your love.
All I have I give you,
Every dream and wish are yours;
Mother of Christ, mother of mine,
And I see your smiling face,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: As The De Wy Shades Of Even
firstLine: As the dewy shades of even,
verses: As the dewy shades of even,
Free my thoughts from aught defiled;
With thy wings of mercy cover,
Keep from sin thy helpless child.
Thine own sinless heart was broken,
Sorrow's sword had pierced its care
Queen of sorrows, guard and guide me,
Let me to thine arms repair,
In thy tender bosom hide me;
Take me, Mother to thy care.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: As With Gladness Men Of Old
firstLine: As with gladness men of old
verses: As with gladness men of old
As with joy they hailed its light,
So, most gracious Lord, may we
Ever more be Led to Thee.
As with joyful steps they sped
There to bend the knee before Thee,
So many we with willing feet
Keep us in the narrow way;
And, when earthly things are past,
Bring our ransomed souls at last
Where they need no star to guide,
Where no clouds Thy glory hide.
Thou its Light, its Joy, its Crown,
Thou its Sun which goes not down,
There for ever may we sing
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: As Your Family Lord
firstLine: As your family Lord, see us here, (3X)
verses: As your family Lord, see us here, (3X)
At your altar Lord, we are fed (3X)
Fill our hearts Lord, with your love (3X)
Make us friends Lord, with everyone (3X)
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Asperges Me
firstLine: Asperges me, Domine, hyssopo,
verses: Et mundabor; Lavabis me, et super nivem dealbabor,
Miserere mei, Deus, Secundum magnam misericordiam tuam.
Gloria Patri, et Filio, et Spiritui Sancto,
Sicut erat in principio, et nunc, et semper,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: At The Lamb's High Feast We Sing
firstLine: At the Lamb's high feast we sing,
verses: At the Lamb's high feast we sing,
Who hath washed us in the tide,
Praise we him whose love divine,
Gives the guest his blood for wine,
Gives his body for the feast,
Love the victim, love the priest.
Where the Paschal blood is poured,
Death's dark angel sheathes his sword;
Through the wave that drowns the foe,
Christ, the Lamb whose blood was shed,
Powers of hell beneath thee lie;
Death is conquered in the fight;
Thou halt brought us life and light;
Now thy banner thou dost wave;
Angels join his praise to tell -
See overthrown the prince of hell.
From the death of sin set free,
Souls re-born, dear Lord, in thee;
Hymns of glory, songs of praise,
Risen Lord, all praise to thee,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Attende Domine
firstLine: Attende Domine, et miserere,
verses: Ad te Rex summe, omnium redemptor.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ave Maria (As I Kneel ...)
firstLine: As I kneel before you,
verses: As I bow my head in prayer,
Take this day, make it yours
And fill me with your love.
All I have I give you,
Every dream and wish are yours;
Mother of Christ, mother of mine,
And I see your smiling face,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ave Maria O Maiden, O Mother
firstLine: Ave Maria!
verses: Proudly thy children are calling on thee;
Thine are the graces unclaimed by another,
Sinless and beautiful, star of the sea!
Pray for thy children who call upon thee;
Sinless and beautiful, star of the sea.
Ave Maria! the night shades are falling,
Softly our voices arise unto thee;
Earth's lonely exiles for succour are calling,
Sinless and beautiful, star of the sea!
Ave Maria! thou portal of heaven,
Harbour of refuge, to thee do we flee;
Lost in the darkness, by stormy winds driven,
Shine on our pathway, fair star of the sea!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ave Maris Stella
firstLine: Ave maris stella,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

V. Dignare me laudare te, Virgo sacrata.
R. Da mihi virtutem contra hostes tuos.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ave Verum Corpus Natum
firstLine: Ave verum Corpus natum
verses: O Jesu, dulcis, O Jesu Pie,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Away In A Manger
firstLine: Away in a manger, no crib for a bed,
verses: Away in a manger, no crib for a bed,
The little Lord Jesus laid down His sweet head.
The stars in the bright sky looked down
The little Lord Jesus asleep on the hay.
The cattle are lowing, the Baby awakes,
But little Lord Jesus no crying He makes.
And stay by my side until morning is nigh.
Be near me, Lord Jesus: I ask Thee to stay
Close by me forever and love me, I pray.
Bless all the dear children in Thy tender care;
And fit us to heaven, to live with Thee there.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Beautiful Saviour
firstLine: Beautiful Saviour, King of creation,
verses: Son of God and son of man!
Truly I'd love Thee, Truly I'd serve Thee,
Light of my soul, my joy, my crown!
Fair are the meadows, fairer the woodlands,
Robed in flow'rs of blooming spring.
Jesus is fairer, Jesus is purer;
He makes our sorrowing spirit sing.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Behold Among Men
firstLine: Behold among men, the dwelling place of God
verses: Behold among men, the dwelling place of God
They shall be His people, and He shall be their Lord and God.
People called by God through the words of the prophets
People brought together in the house of the Lord,
People who attend to their Lord,
The church of Christ the Lord.
Temple built by saints, by the martyrs and
Holy temple built upon Christ the cornerstone;
Holy dwelling place of our God;
The Church of Christ the Lord.
People who are born of the water and the Spirit;
People who are marked by the seal of the Spirit;
People in the Spirit made one;
The church of Christ the Lord.
People who are grafted on Jesus the vine,
Branches bearing fruits in the grace of the Lord;
Branches rooted in His great love;
The church of Christ the Lord.
People justified by the grace of the Spirit,
People who are heirs of an everlasting life;
The church of Christ the Lord.
People nourished now by the body of the Lord.
People who sit down to the new paschal supper;
Who shall pass from death into life;
The church of Christ the Lord.
People called by faith to be quickened by His life;
People called by God from the darkness into light;
Holy people called to be royal; The Church of Christ the Lord.
People whom the Lord has acquired for His praise;
People called by God for His own;
People who put trust in the Lord Jesus Christ;
The church of Christ the Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Benedictus Qui Venit
firstLine: Benedictus qui venit,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bethlehem Of Noblest Cities
firstLine: Bethlehem! Of noblest cities,
verses: None can once with thee compare;
Thou alone, the Lord from heaven,
Fairer than the sun at morning,
Was the star that told His birth;
To the lands their God announcing,
Hid beneath a form of earth.
See them bend, their gifts to offer,
Gifts of incense, gold, and myrrh.
With the Father and the Spirit,
Endless praise to thee be paid.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Beto Tunga
firstLine: Key C: 3/4
verses: Beto tunga, Beto tunga betotunga -ye Beto tunga dibundu, beto tunga ye DC
Kuisa mpagi nata maka bu na nge ye beto tunga DC Dibundu yanzadi kele ya moto yon so-o betotunga (DC) dibundu, iyeye A yee
Kuisa mpagi nata maka bu na ngeyee Beto tunga DC Batata na beto nata maka bo-o, beto tunga (DC) Dibundu, iyeye A yee.
Kuisa mpagi nata maka bu na ngeye betotunga Ba mama na beto nata maka bo-o betotunga DC.
Kuisa mpagi nata maka bu na ngeye betotunga 2x Bilenge nabeto naka maka bo-o betotunga 2x
Kuisa mpagi nata maka bu na nge yee betotunga Choro lee nabeto ikele ya moto yon so etc
Kuisa mpagi nata mak bu na ngeye Etc paroisse na beto ikele ya moto yo nso etc.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bianu Umu Chineke
verses: Bia nu Bia nu umu chineke
Ya kere- I gwe, n'ihe dum no na ya (2x)
ihe nile ekereke kpo isiala nye onyenwanyi
Obu Eze di egwu, Eze di ebube
Umu madu nile, kpo isi ala nye osebruwa
Bata n'ul nso ya, n'obi anuri n'ekele
Otosiri inyekele n'ulo nso nke ya
Maka ihu n'anya ya, n'ebe madu no.
Ozitere nwa ya, ka ogbaputa anyi na njo
Nwa ya nwuru n'obe, maka njo nke anyi.
Unu madu nile tuo nu Chukwu egwu
Ike nile siya n'aka bia nuwa.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Blessed Are The Poor In Spirit
firstLine: "Blessed are the poor in spirit;
verses: Theirs is the kingdom of God.
Sing out all you children of the Lord.
Blessed is the man who is humble;(3X)
he'll win possession of the land.
Blessed are the ones now in mourning;(3X)
they'll have their comfort one day.
Blessed all who thirst for justice;(3X)
for they will soon be satisfied.
Blessed is the man of mercy;(3X)
he will have mercy in his turn.
Blessed are the clean of heart;(3X)
they will see the face of the lord.
they are the children of their God.
Blessed are the sons of justice;(3X)
theirs is the kingdom of the Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Blessed Assurance
firstLine: "Blessed assurance, Jesus is Mine!
verses: Oh, what a foretaste of glory Divine!
Heir of salvation, purchase of God,
Born of His Spirit, washed in His Blood!
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour, all the day long.
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour, all the day long.
Visions of rapture now burst at my sight!
Echoes of mercy, whispers of love.
Perfect submission, all is at rest.
And in my Saviour, I'm happy and blessed;
Filled with His goodness, lost in His love.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Blessed Be God
firstLine: "Blessed be God. Amen.
verses: Blessed be His Holy name. Amen
Blessed be His Holy angels. Amen.
May the power of His name be upon us. Amen.
Blessed be His angels forever and ever.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Blessed Lamb! On Calvary's
firstLine: Blessed Lamb! on Calvary's mountain
verses: Slain to take our sins away:
Let the drops of that rich fountain,
Lowly at thy feet we pray.
In thy love our souls confide:
By thy groans within the garden,
By the death which thou hast died,
Let thy Passion - Let thy Passion,
So shall peace, sweet peace be given,
So shall earth but lead to heaven,
Since for us the Lamb was slain:
Thou canst not have died in vain.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Blessed Saviour Thee I Love
firstLine: Blessed Saviour, Thee I love,
verses: All my hopes in thee abide,
Thou my hope, and naught beside;
All my gain I count but loss;
Clouds they are that hide my day,
Let me see Jesus crucified for me.
Thine to live, and Thine to die;
Height or depth, or creature power,
Ne'er shall hide my Saviour more;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Blessed Saviour
firstLine: Blessed Saviour, we adore Thee,
verses: We thy love and grace proclaim,
Thou art might, thou art Holy,
Glorious, Glorious, Glorious is thy Name, O Lord,
Glorious, Glorious, Glorious is thy Name, O Lord.
Let the Saints of every nation,
Sing thy just and endless praise.
From thy throne of Heaven's glory,
To the cross of sin and shame,
Thou didst come to die a ransom,
Come and take thy royal Throne,
Come and reign and reign forever,
Be the Kingdom all thine own.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bow Down And Hear Our Cry
firstLine: Bow down and hear our cry,
verses: Bow down and hear our cry,
The walls of gold entomb us,
Take not thy thunder from us,
From lies of tongue and pen,
The prince and priest and thrall,
Smite us and save us all;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bread You Have Given Us
firstLine: Bread you have given us, Lord,
verses: Bread you have given us, Lord,
Wine you have shared with us,
We come to sing at the table of love, (2X)
We come to sing all the songs we know,
To bring our love, to bring our love.
We come to laugh at the table of love, (2X)
We come to laugh at the joy we know,
To bring our love, to bring our love.
We come to die at the table of love, (2X)
We come to die all the death we know,
To bring our love, to bring our love.
We come to be at the table of love, (2X)
We come to be all the life we know,
To bring our love, to bring our love.
I come with you to the table of love, (2X)
I come with you whom I long to know,
To bring our love, to bring our love.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bring An Offering And Enter His Courts(psalm 96)
firstLine: Bring an offering and enter His courts;
verses: Bring an offering and enter His courts;
in his temple worship the Lord.
O sing a new song to the Lord,
Sing to the Lord all the earth,
O sing to the Lord, bless His name.
Proclaim His help day by day,
Tell among the nations His glory
And His wonders among all the peoples.
The Lord is great and worthy of praise,
To be feared above all gods,
The gods of the heathens are naught.
It was the Lord, who made the heavens,
His are majesty and the state and power,
And splendour in His holy place.
Give the Lord, you families of peoples,
Give the Lord glory and power,
Give the Lord the glory of His name.
Bring an offering and enter His courts,
Worship the Lord in His temple,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bring Flowers Of The Rarest
firstLine: Bring flowers of the fairest, bring flowers of the rarest,
verses: Bring flowers of the fairest, bring flowers of the rarest,
From garden and woodland and hillside and vale.
Our full hearts are swelling, our glad voices telling
The praise of the loveliest, Rose of the vale.
O Mary! we crown thee with blossoms today!
Queen of the Angels, Queen of the May.
O Mary! we crown thee with blossoms today,
Queen of the Angels, Queen of the May.
Our voices ascending, in harmony blending.
Oh! Thus may our hearts turn dear Mother , to thee.
Oh! Thus shall we prove thee, how truly we love thee,
How dark without Mary, life's journey would be.
O Virgin most tender, our homage we render.
Thy love and protection, sweet Mother , to win.
In danger defend us, in sorrow befriend us,
And shield our hearts from contagion and sin.
Of mothers the dearest, Oh, wilt thou be nearest,
When life with temptation is darkly replete?
Forsake us, o never! Our hearts be thine ever
As pure as the lilies we lay at thy feet.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bring To The Lord Your Offerings (Psalm 29)
firstLine: Bring to the Lord your offerings;
verses: Bring to the Lord your offerings;
adore Him in His holy place.
O give the Lord you children of God,
give the Lord glory and power;
give the Lord the glory of His name;
adore the Lord in His holy court.
The Lord's voice resounding on the waters
the Lord on the immensity of waters;
the voice of the Lord full of power,
the voice of the Lord, full of splendour.
The Lord's voice shattering the cedars,
the Lord shatters the cedars of Lebanon;
He makes Lebanon leap like a calf
and Sirion like a young wild-ox.
The Lord's voice shaking the wilderness,
the Lord shakes the wilderness of Kadesh;
the Lord's voice rending the oak tree
In his temple they all cry: "Glory!
" The Lord sat enthroned over the flood;
the Lord sits as king forever.
Give glory to the Father Almighty,
to his Son, Jesus Christ, the Lord,
to the Spirit who dwells in our hearts,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: (4/4) Cheta I Hie Nile Chineke Nna N'emere Gi Ee
firstLine: Chet' i hie nile Chineke nna n'emere gi ee Bia jaa ya mma, inugo kele ya Bia kele ya doo, cheta olu nile Chineke nna n'alulu gi ee bia jaa ya mma inugo kele ya Bia kele ya n'oge erugo,
verses: Chet' i hie nile Chineke nna n'emere gi ee Bia jaa ya mma, inugo kele ya Bia kele ya doo, cheta olu nile Chineke nna n'alulu gi ee bia jaa ya mma inugo kele ya Bia kele ya n'oge erugo,

(But' onyinye gi ga na Alter k'kele chukwu) Ahuike nile Chineke nna jia gozi gi ee bia jaa ya mma inugo kele ya, bia kele ya doo, cheta oria nile Chineke nna n'agwonu gi ee bia jaa ya mma inugo-kele ya bia kele ya n'oge rugo.
Maka aku n'uba Chineke nyere gi bia jaa ya mma, (kunite bia jaa ya mma) Mgbe igukosiri ngozi Chineke Ibiara tuo ro ya mma mma?
Onyinye obi ocha ka Chineke n'anara O nwa nnem were onyinye gi tuoro ya mma mma.
Onyinye chineke n'enye umu ya O bu ka, a mara chineke bara uba n'ebe umu ya no, ngozi chineke bu ya n'edu gi, n'imendu gi ezi nwannem kele ya, bia nye-chineke onyinye.
Bia nye Chineke onyinye, onyinye din mma, K'anyi bu nye Chineke nna onyinye obi ocha

Alt: Ihioma nile si n'aka Chi-neke nna Ngozi nile si n'a-ka Chukwu nna Omumu nile si n'aka Chineke, aku n'uba nile si n'aka ya, bia nu nye Chi-ne-ke onyinye di mma, k'anyi bu nye Chineke nna onyinye obi ocha n'odi mma.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Children In Thy Presence Met
firstLine: Children in thy presence met,
verses: Fill our hearts with holy fear,
Though we do not yet by sight,
God most high, behold thy face,
Pour into our minds the light
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Children Of Mary
firstLine: Children of Mary, high your voices raise,
verses: Children of Mary, high your voices raise,
Ye on whom she casts her tender eyes,
Children of God, sing her immortal praise,
And all exalt her glory to the skies,
Children of Mary, high your voices raise,
Children of God, sing her immortal praise.
I see ascending to her glorious throne
The fervent prayers of every faithful child,
Each heart erects an altar to her name
Where Mary lives in everlasting fame,
Children of Mary, high your voices raise,
Children of God, sing her immortal praise.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Chineke Mo Onye Nwemo, Abiara M N'iru Gi Inye Gi Ekele
firstLine: Chineke mo, onye nwemo, abiara m n'iru gi inye gi ekele, chukwu A nam aja gi mma, nna nkigwe ihiosoro gi eh n'imendu mo mebe, K'osiso gi chukwu, Okaka eh ihiosoro gi eh n'imendu mo mebe
verses: Chineke mo, onye nwemo, abiara m n'iru gi inye gi ekele, chukwu A nam aja gi mma, nna nkigwe ihiosoro gi eh n'imendu mo mebe, K'osiso gi chukwu, Okaka eh ihiosoro gi eh n'imendu mo mebe
K'o si so nna, k'o si so, Chi-neke nna mo (odim nma)
K'o siso, k'osi so gi- chineke nna mo- odiri m mma n'eluwa, odiri m njo- aga m aturu gi mma mma n'obi ocha Ngozi gi chineke nna o rugo m'aka ebe omasiri gi chineke nna mo O ma siri mo.

O bughi n'igbaka mbo n'ezie bu ya bu n'ego ekwe mkpata, Chetakwa nine uwa si nakara, ka ufodu n'atahuhu n'uwa echere Chi ha, Emekata o'di ka adighi eme A ga m amara egwu bur'onyi nyem wee bia niru gi o ma si gi chineke nna mo, o ma siri mo.
Ekwere mo, oge chukwu ka cha mma n'uwa, ekwere mo, ukwa ru-o-ge ya ya-ada, Ngozi chineke, erusagom Ka mmiri eh lee a mara ya n'ime ndu mo.
A gam agbara egwu buru onyi nye m wee bia niru gi-oma si gi chineke nna mo-omasiri mo

ukwe kam ji bia (Ref) Egwu oyori ma, kam ji bia n tru gi nna ikele gi n'ihin'eme nandu mo Ahuhu bia na ndu mo, agam atabanu ya Anuri bia nandu mo, agam anuriba n'oko si so gi, k'osi so chukwu kosi So, k'osi, k'osi so chineke nna mo o masirimo.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Christ Be With Me
firstLine: "Christ be with me, Christ within me,
verses: "Christ be with me, Christ within me,
Christ behide me, Christ before me,
Christ beside me, Christ to win me,
Christ to comfort and restore me,
Christ beneath me, Christ above me,
Christ in quiet, Christ in danger,
Christ in hearts of all that love me,
Christ in mouth of friend and stranger.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Christ Is King Of Earth And Heaven
firstLine: Christ is King of earth and heaven;
verses: Christ is King of earth and heaven;
In the splendour of His temple,
Can refuse to bend the knee,
To the God made Man who reigneth,
For the lack of faithful service,
To the Master whom we own.
With the praise of faithful subjects,
To His love in honour bound.
Till we breathe our latest breath,
Till we greet in highest heaven,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Christ Is My Rock
firstLine: Christ is my Rock, my Refuge, my Stronghold,
verses: Christ is my Rock, my Refuge, my Stronghold,
Firm as the tree's root that clutches the land.
He who has faith builds without worry,
Not like the man who builds upon sand.
I set my house on a solid foundation:
Christ is my rock, the root of my soul's recreation.
I know a man who love to live free,
He pitched his tent by the side of the sea.
It stood near the surf and
Till one day a wave came and washed it away.
I know a man who loved to live high,
He built his castle near up to the sky.
Through summer and spring, it stood pretty well
When winter wind whistled, it toppled and fell.
I know a man, a cool one to know,
He built his house out of ice and of snow.
In chill isolation, his night work was done,
But it melted away with the warm morning sun.
I know a man who built out of straw,
The flimsiest fellow that I ever saw.
One day a spark set it to flame,
And nothing was left to remember his name.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Christ Our Pasch
firstLine: REF:
verses: Christ our Pasch has been slain, alleluia Christ himself;
Pasch of the New Law, the Spirit's holy feast;
O Pasch of Christ the Lord who for us has come to earth.
Pasch of the New Law, O joy of all mankind;
the doors of life are wide, giving life to us once more.
Pasch of the New Law, the banquet hall is full
of guests the Lord has called, that all may share his feast.
Pasch of the New Law, behold your baptized saints in robes
of purest white for the marriage of the Lamb.
Pasch of the New Law, our soul's immortal
flame shines forth in splendor bright, never more to cease its light.
Pasch of the New Law, O Christ who live
again, the power of death you crushed, you have given us your life.
Pasch of the New Law, we pray to you, O Lord;
stretch forth your blessed hands on the people you have saved.
Pasch of the New Law, O Christ receive
our songs; to you be glory Lord, with all joy and praise. Amen!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Christ The Lord Is Risen Today
firstLine: Christ the Lord is risen today!
verses: Christ the Lord is risen today!
Christians, haste your vows to pay;
For the sheep, the Lamb has bled,
Christ, the Lord, is risen on high,
Now he lives no more to die.
When in strange and awful strife
Haste with joy your vows to pay
Christ, the Lord, is risen on high!
Now he lives no more to die.
Christ, who once for sinners bled,
Now the first born from the dead,
Throned in endless might and power,
Hail , our Prince of life adored!
Help and save us, gracious Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Christmas Comes To Bless
firstLine: Christmas comes to bless the earth,
verses: Christmas comes to bless the earth,
Bright and high the dawning light,
Burning through the gloom of night.
God hath come with men to dwell,
In this message hard to tell,
God the Son, the one Divine,
Sing the angels all to thee,
All in one our praises most.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Down, Lord
firstLine: Come down Lord, my son is ill,
verses: Come down Lord, my son is ill,
Wracked with fever the livelong day
He is life to me, if you will, Drive death away,
Drive death away, Lord, do not come to my house, I'm unworthy,
Speak and the promise is sealed,
For when Your Word, O God, is spoken,
He shall be healed, he shall be healed.
Come down, Lord, my soul is ill,
Wracked with anguish the livelong day,
All my sorrowing will be still,
If You but say, if You but say,
Lord, do not come to my house, I'm unworthy,
Speak and the promise is sealed,
For when Your Word, O God, is spoken,
I shall be healed, I shall be healed.
Come down, Lord the world is ill,
Wracked with bloodshed the livelong day,
Man must struggle for peace until
You show the way, You show the way,
Lord, do not come to our house, we're unworthy,
Speak and the promise is sealed,
For when Your Word, O God, is spoken,
We shall be healed, we shall be healed.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come God's Children
firstLine: Come God's children let us sing, alleluia.
verses: Come God's children let us sing, alleluia.
Bringing gifts to praise our King, alleluia.
Drawing close in heart and mind, alleluia.
That by his aid the truth we 'll find, alleluia.
We offer gifts of bread and wine, alleluia.
In Christ our Lord, the Son Divine, alleluia.
In joyful song our voices raise, alleluia.
To God above we offer praise, alleluia.
We must love him with all our heart, alleluia.
As a whole and not a part, alleluia.
Come God's children let us sing, alleluia.
Bringing gifts to praise our King, alleluia.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Holy Spirit Creator Come
firstLine: Come Holy Spirit, Creator, come,
verses: Come, take possession of our souls,
And make them all thy own.
Thou who art called the Paraclete,
The living spring, the living fire,
Thou who are sev'n fold in thy grace,
O guide our minds with thy blest light,
And with thy strength which never decays,
Far from us drive our deadly foe;
And through all perils lead us safe,
Through thee may we the Father know,
And thee the Spirit of them both,
All glory to the Father be,
The same to thee, great Paraclete,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Let Us Sing Songs Of Glory
firstLine: Come, let us sing songs of glory,
verses: Come, let us sing songs of glory,
Let us clap our hands and shout for joy.
From day to day God will show the way
Give the glory due and sing:
Give the glory due and sing:
God is our King and our Father;
He's a mighty God and King of Kings.
He filled the seas, and He made the trees.
Ours is the joy of His people;
We can walk in safety at his side
In stormy night, He will give us light.
Let us always know that He is near,
Light from above, teaching us to love.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Let's Share In The Banquet Of The Lord
firstLine: "Come, let's share in the banquet of the Lord,
verses: "Come, let's share in the banquet of the Lord,
Come, let's share in the supper of the Lord,
Eat the bread and drink the wine,
Flesh and blood of the Lord divine,
Share his life, it is yours and mine,
For we are one in his design.
Come, let's share in the passion of the Lord,
Come, let's share the resurrection of the Lord,
Come, let's share the ascension of the Lord,
Come, let's share our union with the Lord,
Come, let's share in the life of the Lord,
Come, let's share in the love of the Lord,
Come, let's share in the banquet of the Lord,
Come, let's share in the supper of the Lord,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Lord Jesus The Light Is Dying
firstLine: Come Lord Jesus, the light is dying,
verses: Come Lord Jesus, the light is dying,
The night keeps crying, come Lord Jesus.
Poverty a-plentyHearts gone wild with war,
The dreams of all are empty,
To set the lame man leaping,
To give the blind man sight,
We await the Prince of peace.
The clouds shall send a Saviour
To free us from our chains.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Praise The Lord
firstLine: Come, praise the Lord, you
verses: Come, sing His praise and let
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Rock The Cradle For Him
firstLine: Come, rock the cradle for Him,
verses: Come, rock the cradle for Him,
Come, in the crib adore Him,
Dull care, I pray you, bury:
And in the Lord make merry,
Sweet little Jesu, sweet little Jesu.
The throne of God all holy:
And kneel we down before Him,
Sweet little Jesu, Sweet little Jesu.
For sin by night or morrow,
Would cause Him pain and sorrow,
Sweet little Jesu, sweet little Jesu.
So at your hour of dying,
Sweet little Jesu, sweet little Jesu.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Sound His Praise Abroad (Psalm 95)
firstLine: Come sound His praise abroad
verses: He gave the seas their bounds:
The watery worlds are all His own
We are His work, and not our own,
He formed us by His word.
Come like the people of His choice,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Thou Almighty King
firstLine: Come, Thou almighty King,
verses: Help us your name to sing.
Come and rule over us, Ancient of Days.
And let your holiness, to us descend.
You who almighty art, enlighten every heart
From us do not depart, Spirit of pow'r.
To you, great One in Three,
Eternal praises be, Hence, ever more!
Your sovereign majesty, may we in glory see,
And to eternity love and adore.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come To Me Beloved
firstLine: Come to me, Beloved
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come Thou Long Expected Saviour
firstLine: Come thou long expected Saviour,
verses: Born to set thy people free;
From our fears and sins release us;
Let us find our rest in thee,
Hope of all the earth thou art;
Born a child and yet a King,
Born to reign in us forever, Now thy gracious Kingdom bring.
Rule in all our hearts alone;
Raise us to thy glorious throne.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Come To The Springs Of Living Water
firstLine: Come to the springs of living water
verses: Come to the springs of living water
Flowing from the heart of God.
Come from the distant valley to the mountain of the Lord
Come from the far-off islands to God
Cast the clouds of darkness from your eyes.
Come to the springs of living water
Flowing from the heart of God.
Come with your precious ointment to the mountain of the Lord.
Come with your humble offerings to God.
Hasten to pay him your vows.
Come with guitars and cymbals to the mountain of the Lord.
Come with your music singing to God.
Ours is the God of Jacob, rejoice.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Crown Him With Many Crowns
firstLine: Crown him with many crowns,
verses: Hark, how the heavenly anthem drowns
Of him who died for thee,
And hail him as thy matchless King,
Whose arm those crimson trophies won,
As of that rose the stem,
The root, whence mercy ever flows,
Crown him the Lord of love;
But downward bends his burning eye
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Daily Daily Sing To Mary
firstLine: Daily, Daily, sing to Mary,
verses: Sing, my soul, her praises due;
All her feasts, her actions worship,
Call her Mother , call her Virgin.
When the tempest rages round you,
She will calm the troubled sea.
Gifts of heaven she has given,
She, the Queen, who decks her subjects
With the light of God's own grace.
Sing, my tongue, the Virgin's trophies,
Who for us her Maker bore,
For the curse of old inflicted, Peace and blessing to restore.
Sing in songs of praise unending,
We ary not nor faint in telling
All the gifts she gives to men.
Strive to sound her glory forth:
Where the voice of music thrilling,
All our joys do flow from Mary,
All of them join her praise to sing:
Mother of our Lord and King.
While we sing her awful glory,
Let our hearts be quick to offer
Love the heart alone can teach.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Daughter Of A Mighty Father
firstLine: Daughter of a Mighty Father
verses: Mother of the Son and Savior
Of the Truth the Life the Way
Guide our footsteps calm our passion
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Day Is Dying In The West
firstLine: Day is dying in the west,
verses: Day is dying in the west,
Heaven is touching earth with rest.
Wait and worship while the night
Holy, holy, holy Lord God of host!
Heaven and earth are full of Thee!
Heaven and earth are praising Thee,
Lord of life, beneath the dome
Gather us, who seek Thy home.
To the fold of thy embrace,
Through the glory and the grace
Of the stars that veil Thy face,
When for ever from our sight,
Pass the stars, the day, the night,
Lord of angels, on our eyes
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Dear Angel Ever At My Side
firstLine: Dear angel, ever at my side,
verses: Dear angel, ever at my side,
To leave thy home in heaven to guard
For I have felt thee in my thoughts
And when my heart loves God,
I know the sweetness is from thee.
And when, dear spirit, I kneel down
Something there is within my heart
Which tells me thou art there.
when I pray thou prayest too,
Thy prayer is all for me;
But when I sleep thou sleepest not,
But I am weak, and when I fall,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Dear Children Praise The Lord
firstLine: Dear children, praise the Lord in all,
verses: Dear children, praise the Lord in all,
Early and late, in mirth and tears.
Lift up thy voice in earnest call,
With mingled love and holy fear.
Dear children, praise His holy name,
Adore His presence, fear His word,
And oh in all be this thy aim,
Still more and more to praise the Lord.
Praise Him for all His mercies given
In flowing streams with lavish hand,
Who made thee that thy soul in heav'n
Might ever in his presence stand.
Praise Him who in Baptismal rite
Call'd thee from sin to life of grace,
Who pour'd on thee His Holy light
To fit thee for the heav'nly place.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Dear Guardian Of Mary
firstLine: Dear Guardian of Mary! dear nurse of her Child!
verses: Dear Guardian of Mary! dear nurse of her Child!
Life's ways are full weary, the desert is wild.
Bleak sands are all round us, no home can we see.
Sweet Spouse of our Lady, we lean safe on thee.
For thou to the pilgrim art father and guide,
And Jesus and Mary felt safe at thy side.
O Glorious Patron, secure shall I be,
Sweet Spouse of our Lady, if thou stay with me!
God chose thee for Jesus and Mary, wilt thou
Forgive a poor exile for choosing thee now?
There's no saint in heaven, St. Joseph like thee,
Sweet Spouse of our Lady, do thou plead for me.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Dear Maker Of The Starry Skies
firstLine: Dear maker of starry skies,
verses: Light never lost by faithful eyes,
Christ, the salvation sent to all,
Be near thy servants when they call.
Thy love that pitied from on high,
A guilty race foredoomed to die,
Was pain that sentence to repeal,
So, at the eventide of earth,
From that bride chamber of thy birth,
Our mortal flesh thou didst assume,
Born of a spotless Virgin's womb.
O thou, at whose august decree,
Once heard, creation bends the knee,
While heaven and earth obey thy will,
Trembling before thee, and are still.
Holiest of holy ones, from whom
In this brief world of time, we pray,
Keep Satan's treacherous darts at bay.
To God the Father, God the Son,
And God the Spirit, Three in One,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Deliver Me O Lord (Líbera Me Dómine)
firstLine: Deliver me, O Lord, from death eternal on that fearful day,
verses: Deliver me, O Lord, from death eternal on that fearful day,
When the heavens and the earth shall be moved,
When Thou shalt come to judge the world by fire.
I am made to tremble, and I fear, till the judgement be upon us, and the coming wrath,
When the heavens and the earth shall be moved.
That day, day of wrath, calamity and misery, day of great and exceeding bitterness,
When Thou shalt come to judge the world by fire.
Rest eternal grant unto them, O Lord: and let light perpetual shine upon them.
Deliver me, O Lord, from death eternal on that fearful day, When the heavens and the earth shall be moved, when Thou shalt come to judge the world by fire.
And lead us not into temptation
May he (she, they) rest in peace.
And let my cry come unto Thee.
The Lord be with you. R. And with your spirit.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Dies Iræ Dies Illa
firstLine: Dies iræ, dies illa
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ding Dong Merrily On High
firstLine: Ding dong! Merrily on high
verses: In heaven the bells are ringing.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ecce Panis Angelorum
firstLine: Ecce panis angelorum,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ecce Sacerdos Magnus
firstLine: Ecce Sacerdos magnus
verses: Qui in diebus suis placuit Deo,
Qui in di ebus suis, qui in diebus suis, placuit Deo.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Enter Rejoice And Come In
firstLine: Enter rejoice and come in /2ce
verses: Today will be a joyful day
Lift up your hearts to the Lord /2ce
Today will be a joyful day
Lift up your hearts and rejoice.
Open your hearts to the Lord /2ce
Today will be a joyful day
Open your hearts to the Lord.
Open your hearts to all men /2ce
Today will be a joytful day
Open your hearts to all men.
Sing out in songs to the Lord/2ce
Today will be a joyful day
Sing out in songs to the Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Enwerem Anuri (2/4)
firstLine: Enwere m anuri, oge m nuru ka ha n'ekwu si,
verses: Enwere m anuri, oge m nuru ka ha n'ekwu si,
N'ulo Chineke nna mo N'ulo chukwu. D/S
E-nwerem anuri oge m nuru Ka ha n'ekwusi,
k'anyi jeenu n'uloo ngi bu Jerusalem anyi kwuzi,
Ewuru jerusalem dika oke obodo kwusirike,
dika oke obodo kwusiri ike Ebe ahu ka mba nile n'aga - Mba nile nke dinwenu.
K'anyi jee n'ulo chukwu n'ulo Chukwu D/S
N'ihi ihunanya nke umunne m, (Ref) Na ndi enyi mo ana m ekwu si (Ref) Udo udo, udo dirigi,
n'ihi ihunanya mnwere maka ulo nke dinwenu., (Ref)
Agam ario maka odi mma gi. Ref/CH.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Fade Fade Each Earthly Joy
firstLine: Fade, fade, each earthly joy, Jesus is mine!
verses: Fade, fade, each earthly joy, Jesus is mine!
Break every tender tie, Jesus is mine!
Jesus alone can bless, Jesus is mine!
Tempt not my soul away, Jesus is mine!
Here would I ever stay, Jesus is mine!
Born but for one brief day,
Farewell, ye dreams of night, Jesus is mine!
Lost in this dawning Light, Jesus is mine!
All that my soul has tried,
Jesus has satisfied, Jesus is mine!
Welcome, my saviour bless, Jesus is mine!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Fading Still Fading
firstLine: Fading still fading the last beam is shining,
verses: Fading still fading the last beam is shining,
Ave Maria, the day is declining,
Safety and innocence fly with the light,
Temptation and danger walk forth in the night,
From fall of the shade to the bright morning time,
Shield us from danger and save us from crime.
Ave Maria, Ave Maria, Ave Maria, audi nos.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Faith Of Our Fathers Living Still
firstLine: Faith of our fathers, living still,
verses: Faith of our fathers, living still,
In spite of dungeon, fire and sword:
Oh, how our hearts beat high with joy,
Whenever we hear that glorious word!
Faith of our fathers! holy faith!
We will be true to thee till death.
Our fathers, chained in prisons dark,
Were still in heart and conscience free.
How sweet would be their children's fate
If they, like them, could die for thee!
Faith of our fathers! holy faith!
We will be true to thee till death.
Faith of our fathers, we will strive,
To win all Nations unto thee;
And through the truth that comes from God,
Mankind shall then be truly free.
Faith of our fathers! holy faith!
We will be true to thee till death.
Faith of our fathers, we will love
Both friend and foe in all our strife,
And preach thee too, as love knows how,
By kindly words and virtuous life.
Faith of our fathers! holy faith!
We will be true to thee till death.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Father Within Thy House Today
firstLine: Father, within thy house today,
verses: We wait thy kindly love to see,
Since thou hast said in truth that they,
Who dwell in love are one with thee,
Bless those who for thy blessing wait;
Dear Lord of Love, whose heart of fire,
So full of pity for our sin,
Was once in that divine desire,
Broken, thy Bride to woo and win,
Look down and bless them from above,
And keep their hearts alight with love.
Blest Spirit, who with life and light,
Didst quicken chaos to thy praise,
Still lifts our nature up to grace,
Bless those who here in truth consent;
Great One in Three, of whom are named,
All families on earth and heaven,
Hear us, who have thy promise claimed.
And let a wealth of grace be given;
Grant them in life and death to be,
Each knit to each, and both to the
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Fields Full Of Flowers
firstLine: Fields full of flowers and
verses: And the waters clear and blue
Lift our hearts to thoughts of
They Lift our hearts to you.
All of heavens and all of the earth
Will praise the Holy of holies.
Who will go up to the
Who will see his holy face?
Every man who is pure of
Heart and set in the right
They will see the holy face of the Lord
Lift up your heads, O you
Open up ye gates and ring
For the Lord who is God of
All has come to your doors
Let us sing songs of glory to the Lord
O Lord of hosts our King.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Fight The Good Fight
firstLine: Fight the good fight with all thy might,
verses: Fight the good fight with all thy might,
Christ is thy strength, and Christ thy right,
Lay hold on life, and it shall beThy joy and crown eternally.
Run the straight race through God's good grace;
Lift up thine eyes, and seek His face;
Life with its way before us lies,
Christ is the path, and Christ the prize.
Cast care aside, upon thy guide,
Lean, and His mercy will provide,
Lean, and the trusting soul shall prove
Christ is its life, and Christ its love.
Faint not nor fear, His arms are near,
He changeth not, and thou art dear.
Only believe, and thou shalt see
That Christ is all in all to thee.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Fill My House Unto The Fullest
firstLine: Fill my house unto the fullest,
verses: Fill my house unto the fullest,
Eat my bread and drink my wine
The love I bear is held from no one.
All I have and all I do, I give to you.
Take my time unto the fullest,
Find in me the trust you seek
Take my hands to you outreaching.
All I have and all I do, I give to you.
Christ our Lord with love enormous,
From His cross this lesson taught.
Love all men as I have loved you.
All I have and all I do, I give to you.
Join with me as one in Christ's love.
May our hearts all beat as one.
All I have and all I do, I give to you.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Firmly I Believe And Truly
firstLine: Firmly I believe and truly,
verses: God is Three, and God is One,
And I trust and hope most fully,
And each thought and deed unruly,
Do to death, as he has died.
Simply to his grace and wholly,
Light and life and strength belong,
Him the holy, him the strong.
For the love of him alone Holy Church, as his creation,
And her teachings, as his own.
With and through the angelic host,
To the God of earth and heaven,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: For We Are One In The Love Of Christ
firstLine: For we are one in the love of Christ.
verses: For we are one in the love of Christ.
If we die with him we will rise up to life.
If in your life love abounds,
Seek no further for God is found.
Oh when your brother cries out tears,
Share with him his hopes and fears.
Oh when great joy fills your heart,
Open up a smile will start.
Share all your goods with your fellow man,
His Body we eat, His Blood we drink,
Of the Bread of love, we eat heartily.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: From Every Stormy Wind That Blows
firstLine: From every stormy wind that blows,
verses: From every stormy wind that blows,
From every swelling tide of woes,
There is a calm, a sure retreat;
There is a place where Jesus sheds
The oil of gladness on our heads -A place than all beside more sweet;
There is a scene where spirits blend,
Where friends hold fellowship with friends,
Though sundered far, by faith they meet,
There, there, on eagle wings we soar,
And sense and sin molest no more;
And heaven comes down our souls to greet,
Oh, let my hand be cold, and still,
My tongue be silent, cold and still,
This pounding heart forget to beat,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Full In The Panting Heart Of Rome
firstLine: Full in the panting heart of Rome,
verses: Full in the panting heart of Rome,
From pilgrims' lips that kiss the ground, Breathes in all tongues one only sound:
"God bless our Pope, the great, the good."
The golden roof, the marble walls,
The note redouble, till it fills
With echoes sweet the seven hills:
Then surging through each hallowed gate,
Where martyrs glory, in peace, await;
It sweeps beyond the solemn plain,
Peals over Alps, across the main:
From torrid south to frozen north,
Yet strikes no chord more true to Rome's
Than rings within our hearts and homes:
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Glorious God
firstLine: "Glorious God, King of creation,
verses: We praise you, we bless you,
Lord of hearts, Christ the King,
And come to you in prayer.
We sing your praise every where.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Glory Be To Jesus
firstLine: Glory be to Jesus, Who in bitter pains
verses: Glory be to Jesus, Who in bitter pains
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Glory Be To The Father
firstLine: Glory be to the Father, glory be to the Son,
verses: Glory be to the Father, glory be to the Son,
And to the Holy Spirit, now and forever. Amen.
His mercy lives forever, glory be to God,
His wisdom dulls the wisdom of men,
Oh, sing his praise o'r the land,
And with your voices sound his name,
His love endures forever, glory be to God,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Glory To God Glory
firstLine: Glory to God, glory,
verses: O praise the name of the Lord.
Praise Christ the Son of the living God.
Praise Christ the Word of the living God.
Praise the Light of the living God.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Glory To God Glory O Praise Him Alleluia!
firstLine: Glory to God, glory, O
verses: praise the name of the Lord.
Bless the Lord all you works of the Lord
Sun and moon bless the Lord
Bless the Lord you stars of heaven.
Light and darkness bless the Lord
Bless the Lord you nights and days
Now let the earth bless the Lord
Mountains and hills bless the Lord
Bless the Lord you seas and rivers.
Now let the Church bless the Lord
Let his people praise His name.
Bless the Lord you saints of the Lord
Peoples near and far, praise His name.
Praise to Father and to the Son,
To the Holy Spirit Three in One.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Glory To God On High
firstLine: Glory to God on high;
verses: Honour and praise Him all of our days
and love Him in all mankind.
Know for sure that in his love
Praise the Lord in all we do,
And we can't go far wrong.
Trust the Lord with all our might,
Who taught us all the way to love,
And the joy that 's to be won.
For in the love of all mankind
We love our God as well.
To the Father now give praise,
And praise be to the Son,
And by His Spirit's love in us
We know that God is One.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Go Prophet Go!
firstLine: Go, Prophet, go! Go Prophet, go!
verses: Go, Prophet, go! Go Prophet, go!
Go to the ends of the earth for me!
Go, Prophet, go! Go Prophet, go!
Tell them of Christ's new victory!
Go where the rhythm rocks and wails,
Tell them of him who dances in death,
Show them his beat of whips and nails.
Go to the gaudy city streets,
Tell them of Christ upon a beam,
Abandoned by God to die with men.
Go to the haunts where men are trapped,
Caught by their guilt and misery.
Tell them of Christ who lives again,
Offers the power to set them free.
Live with the people whom you know,
Love them in all their pains and fears
Tell them the healing word of Christ,
Give them your laughter and your tears.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Go Tell It On The Mountain
firstLine: Go tell it on the mountain,
verses: Go tell it on the mountain,
Go tell it on the mountain,
l sought both night and day;
I asked the Lord to help me,
And he showed me the way.
Lo, when they had seen it,
They all bowed down and prayed;
To where the babe was laid.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Be With You
firstLine: God be with you till we meet again!
verses: God be with you till we meet again!
By His counsels guide, uphold you;
With His sheep securely fold you;
God be with you till we meet again!
Till we meet at Jesus feet...
Till we meet!... Till we meet!
...God be with you till we meet again!
God be with you till we meet again!
God be with you till we meet again!
God be with you till we meet again!
When life's perils thick confound you;
Put his loving arms around you;
God be with you till we meet again!
God be with you till we meet again!
Keep love's banner floating o'er you;
Smite death's threatening wave before you;
God be with you till we meet again!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Gives His People Strength
firstLine: God gives His people strength,
verses: If we believe in His way,
All those who bear the burden of the day.
If we but trust in His word,
He warmly welcomes anyone who's erred.
If we but open wide our heart,
He's sure to do His part,
He's always the first to make a start.
When sorrow fills us to the brim,
He lays to rest our restlessness in Him.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Is Love
firstLine: God is love, and he who abides in love
verses: God is love, and he who abides in love
Abides in God and God in him.
The love of Christ has gather us together.
Let us rejoice in him and be glad.
By this shall all know that we are his disciples,
If we have love for one another.
Owe no man anything except to love one another,
For he who loves his neighbour will fulfil the whole law.
And so you will fulfil the law of Christ.
The cup of blessing, which we bless,
Is it not fellowship in the Blood of Christ?
Is it not fellowship in the Body of Christ?
We many are one bread, one body
For we all partake of the one Bread.
This is the bread that came down from heaven,
He who eats this bread shall live forever.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Loves A Cheerful Giver
firstLine: God loves a cheerful giver,
verses: He loves to hear you laughing.
When you're in an awkward spot.
When the odds add up against you, it's time to stop and sing:
"Praise God!" To Praise Him is a joyous thing.
Peter always made a fuss, Peter was impetuous,
He knew hard times when he denied his Lord,
But hardly had he fallen when he got right up began again,
Christ named him His successor as his reward.
Jonah was a gloomy sort, he always had a sad report.
He ran from God, he ran and he set sail,
His journey's end was quite abrupt,
A fish came by and swallowed him up,
He spent three dark and dreary days inside the whale.
He lost it all but stood the test,
For Job was steadfast in his misery,
"God gives to me, He takes away,
Blessed be the name of God this day, "
And he was doubly blessed for his fidelity.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Of Mercy And Compassion
firstLine: God of mercy and compassion,
verses: Father, let me call thee Father,
Tis thy child returns to thee.
Jesus Lord, I ask for mercy,
Let me not implore in vain;
All my sins I now detest them,
By my sins I have deserved,
Hell with all its pains and torments,
By my sins I have abandoned,
Right and claim to heaven above;
In a boundless sea of love.
To that Cross my sins have nailed him,
Yet he bleeds and dies for me.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Our Father Everlasting
firstLine: God our Father everlasting,
verses: please accept the gifts we offer;
Take our bread upon the altar,
and our wine that fill the chalice.
God our Father everlasting, please accept the gifts we offer;
Take our bread upon the altar, and our wine that fill the chalice.
Thankful hearts and joyful voices we approach your holy altar.
Bearing gifts of your creation, we return what you have given.
To you Father; now we offer with the host and with the chalice.
All we have and all our being in this sacrifice most holy.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Our Father Forgive Our Sins
firstLine: God our Father, forgive our sins,
verses: God our Father, forgive our sins,
God our Father, we hope in Thee
To do according to thy will.
To be ever worth love of thee.
We pray to love, to save thy children;
Christ our Redeemer to be our tongue,
Let us be righteous in Communion,
Let us be righteous to overcome,
Wash away our sins with thy tongue,
To save us from the death of hell.
Mary our Mother pray for us thy children,
Give us on earth a Mother 's care.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Rest You Merry Gentlemen
firstLine: God rest you merry, gentlemen,
verses: To save us all from Satan's power
O tidings of comfort and joy,
O tidings of comfort and joy.
How that in Bethlehem was born
The Son of God by name.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Rises His Foes Are Scattered
firstLine: God rises His foes are scattered
verses: God rises His foes are scattered
And all who hate Him must flee away
But all the just can dance and say.
They melt like wax in fire,
Sing out and get you ready,
He's now at hand Defender of lonely widows,
Father of orphans in all the land.
O God, You helped Your people,
When in the desert they went through hell.
The earth did shake and tremble,
When it was weary You sent some more,
Your flock hid in Your goodness,
You gave the land back to the poor.
He breaks the sinner and every foe
The Lord of Judah and Israel
Go clap your hands and ring a bell,
Go forth, O God most mighty
Accept as gifts their every gem.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: God Sent His Son They Called Him Jesus
firstLine: God sent His son, they called Him Jesus
verses: God sent His son, they called Him Jesus
He came to love, heal and forgive
He lived and died to buy my pardon
An empty grave is there to prove my Saviour lives.
Because He lives, I can face tomorrow
Because He lives, all fear is gone.
Because I know He holds the future
And life is worth the living just because He lives.
How sweet to hold a new born baby
And feel the pride and joy He brings
But greater still the calm assurance
This child can face uncertain days, because He lives.
And then one day, I'll cross the river
I'll fight life's final war with pain.
And then, as death gives way to victory
I'll see the lights of glory and I'll know He reigns.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Go Tell Everyone
firstLine: God's spirit is in my heart;
verses: God's spirit is in my heart;
He has called me and set me apart,
This is what I have to do,
He sent me to give the good news to the poor,
Tell prisoners that they are prisoners no more,
Tell blind people that they can see,
And go tell everyone the news that the Kingdom of God has come,
And go tell everyone the news that God's Kingdom has come.
Just as the Father sent me,
So I'm sending you out to be,
Don't carry a load in your pack,
You don't need two shirts to your back,
A workman can earn his own keep,
Don't worry what you have to say,
Don't worry because on that day,
God's Spirit will speak in your heart,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Good Christian Men Rejoice
firstLine: Good Christian men, rejoice
verses: With heart and soul and voice;
Give you heed to what we say: news, news.
Ox and ass before him bow,
And He's in the manger now.
With heart and soul and voice;
Now you hear of endless bliss: joy, joy.
Jesus Christ was born for this!
With heart and soul and voice;
Now you need not fear the grave: peace, peace.
Jesus Christ was born to save!
Calls you one and calls you all
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Gradual_tract
firstLine: Requiem aeternam dona eis, Domine,
verses: Et in gloria tua suscipe eos.
Per crucem tuam salva eos, Domine,
Et in regno tuo colloca eos.
Sanctorum chorus laudat te, Rex gloriae,
Cum angelis in caelo canunt victoriae.
Et vitam aeternam in sinu tuo.
Cum sanctis tuis gaudeant in aeternum. Amen.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Grant To Us
firstLine: Grant to us, O Lord, a heart renewed;
verses: Grant to us, O Lord, a heart renewed;
Recreate in us your own Spirit, Lord!
when I will make a new covenant
Deep within their being I will implant my law;
I will write it in their hearts.
I will be their God and they shall my people.
And for all their faults I will grant forgiveness;
never more will I remember their sins.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Great And Glorious Saint Patrick
firstLine: Great and glorious Saint Patrick,
verses: Hearken to the prayers of your children
The offspring of your ardent zeal,
This day your praise shall sound.
Borne on the wings of charity,
Bade Satan from her valleys flee,
Our sires did lose their way,
You cheered their hearts with heavenly light,
Oh what a harvest crown your toil,
The earth long cursed was blessed,
Each lovely virtue graced its soil,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Great Indeed Are Your Works O Lord
firstLine: Great indeed are Your works O Lord,
verses: Great indeed are Your works O Lord,
You our light and our life,
You are the path which we tread,
From every corner of the earth,
You lead them all by the hand,
Then at the end of all times,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Great Is Thy Faithfulness
firstLine: Great is Thy faithfulness, O God my Father;
verses: Great is Thy faithfulness, O God my Father;
There is no shadow of turning with Thee;
Thou changest not, Thy compassions, they fail not;
As Thou hast been, Thou forever will be.
Morning by morning new mercies I see.
All I have needed Thy hand hath provided;
Great is Thy faithfulness, Lord, unto me!
Summer and winter and spring time and harvest,
Sun, moon and stars in their courses above,
Join with all nature in manifold witness
To Thy great faithfulness, mercy and love.
Pardon for sin and a peace that endureth,
Thine own dear presence to cheer and to guide;
Strength for today and bright hope for tomorrow,
Blessings all mine, with ten thousand beside!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Great Things Happen When God Mixes With Men
firstLine: Great things happen when God mixes with us (x2)
verses: Great things happen when God mixes with us (x2)
Great things happen when God mixes with us.
Some find life, some find peace;
Some see things as they never could before
And some people find that they can now begin to trust.
Some find health, some find hope;
Some see themselves as they never could before
And some people find that they can now begin to live.
Some find peace, some are disturbed;
Some see their lives as they never could before
And some people find that they must now begin to change.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Greet We This Mystery
firstLine: Greet we this mystery yearly returning
verses: Greet we this mystery yearly returning
Still doth its history set our hearts burning:
Thoughts, word, actions be true again.
Christ, in the sight of his brethren reclining
On that last night of his, gave them for dining
Lamb that was slain for them.
So did Moses ordain for them.
After that offering made for a token
There stood he proffering his body broken,
Now in those hands of his,
Whole for all as for each of them.
His body fortified spirits that sickened;
Hearts sad and mortified his blood requickened;
Drink of this cup that is offered
Drink my blood that is shed for you."
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Guide Me O Thou Great Redeemer
firstLine: "Guide me O Thou great Redeemer,
verses: I am weak but thou art mighty,
Hold me with Thy powerful hand.
Bread of heaven, Bread of heaven,
We gather Lord around your table
Waiting for the Bread of life.
Make us worthy, make us holy,
Take away all sin and strife.
Living bread come down from heaven,
He who eats it shall not die.
Come and eat and live forever,
You shall live with God on High.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail Holy Joseph
firstLine: Hail , holy Joseph, hail!
verses: Prince of the house of God,
Cheer thou the hearts that faint,
And guide the steps that fail.
To thee the word made flesh
And bless, ye Saints on high,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail Holy Queen Enthroned Above
firstLine: Hail, holy Queen enthroned
verses: O Maria! above, O Maria! Hail, Queen of mercy and Heaven and earth resound the hymn
Triumph, all ye Cherubim, Sing with us, ye Seraphim,
The cause of joy to all below, O Maria!
The spring through which all graces flow, O Maria!
Angels, all your praises bring, Earth and heaven,
O gentle, loving, holy one, O Maria!
The God of light became your Son, O Maria!
Triumph, all ye Cherubim, Sing with us, ye Seraphim,
Heaven and earth resound the hymn:
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail Jesus Hail!
firstLine: Hail , Jesus, hail! who for my sake,
verses: Hail , Jesus, hail! who for my sake,
Sweet blood from Mary's veins didst take;
And shed it all for me:
Oh, blessed be my Saviour's blood,
My light, my life, my only good,
To endless ages let us praise,
The precious blood whose price could raise,
The world from wrath and sin;
Whose streams our inward thirst appease,
And heal the sinner's worst disease,
Oh, Sweetest blood, that can implore,
Pardon of God, and heaven restored,
The heaven which sin had lost;
While Abel's blood for vengeance pleads,
For those who wrong him most.
Oh, to be sprinkled from the wells
Of Christ's own sacred blood, excels,
Hurt not the happy hearts that shine,
With those red drops of his.
Ah, there is joy amid the Saints,
When this sweet song we raise,
Oh, louder then, and louder still,
Earth with one mighty chorus fill,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail O Mary Full Of Grace
firstLine: Hail O Mary full of grace,
verses: Hail O Mary full of grace,
Thou of women first and best,
Fear not, thou shalt bear a Son,
He Son of God most high shall be.
Lo! the handmaid of the Lord,
With His word may all accord.
Comes this promise of a Child,
Full of joy akin to tears,
Full of hope, yet full of tears.
Be it Lord at thy sweet will,
All I am to thee I owe,
All thy purpose thou dost know.
What thou givest to be mine,
May it ne'er be aught but mine,
Be it good or be it ill,
Still be all at thy sweet will.
God is giving fear must cease,
In His will is perfect peace.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail Queen Of Heaven
firstLine: Hail , Queen of heav'n the ocean star,
verses: Hail , Queen of heav'n the ocean star,
Guide of the wand'rer here below:
Thrown on life's surge, we claim your care -
Save us from peril and from woe
Mother of Christ, star of the sea,
Pray for the wanderer, pray for me.
O gentle, chaste, and spotless Maid,
We sinners make our prayers through you
Remind your Son that he has paid
Virgin most pure, star of the sea,
Pray for the sinner, pray for me.
Sojourners in this world of tears,
To you, blest advocate, we cry;
Pity our sorrows, calm our fears,
And soothe with hope our misery:
Refuge in grief, star of the sea,
Pray for the mourner, pray for me.
And while to him who reigns above
In Godhead One, in Persons Three,
The source of life, of grace, of love,
Homage we pay on bended knee;
Do you, bright Queen, star of the sea,
Pray for your children, pray for me.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail Queen Of The Heavens
firstLine: Hail Queen of the Heavens, hail Mistress of earth,
verses: Hail Queen of the Heavens, hail Mistress of earth,
Hail Virgin most pure of immaculate birth,
Clear star of the morning in beauty enshrined,
O Mary make speed to the help of mankind,
Clear star of the morning in beauty enshrined,
O Mary make speed to the help of mankind.
Hail Mother most pure, hail Virgin renown'd,
Hail Queen with the stars as diadem crown'd,
Above all the angels in glory untold,
Standing next to the King in a vesture of gold,
Above all the angels in glory untold,
Standing next to the King in a vesture of gold.
O Mother of mercy, O Star of the sea,
O Hope of the guilty, O light of the grave,
Thro' thee may we come to the Heaven of rest,
And see Heaven's King in the courts of the Blest,
Thro' thee may we come to the Heaven of rest,
And see Heaven's King. in the courts of the Blest.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail Redeemer King Divine
firstLine: Hail , Redeemer, King divine
verses: Priest and Lamb, the throne is thine;
King whose reign shall never cease,
" Praise be Jesus Christ, our King
Lord of life, earth, sky and sea;
Rule our minds, our hearts, our wills,
Till in peace each nation rings
With thy praises, King of Kings.
King most holy, King of truth,
Guide the lowly, guide the youth,
Christ, O King of glory bright,
States and Kingdoms new and old.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail This Joyful Day's Return
firstLine: Hail this joyful day's return,
verses: On His Church His Spirit shed!
Like the golden tongues of flame,
On the twelve the Spirit came -
Tongues, that earth may hear their call,
Fire that love may burn in all.
Seven times seven its course hath run;
Church of Christ from debt made free,
Lord, to thee thy people bend;
Grant us, dearest Lord we pray,
Thou who didst our fathers guide,
Grant us pardon, grant us peace,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hail Thou Star Of Ocean
firstLine: Hail , thou star of ocean,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Happy Indeed Is The Man (Psalm 1)
firstLine: Blessed is he who delights
verses: in the law of the Lord.
Happy indeed is the man who follows
Not the counsel of the wicked,
Not lingers in the way of sinners
Nor sits in the company of scorners,
But whose delight is the law of the Lord
And who ponders his law day and night.
He is like a tree that is planted
That yields its fruit in due season
And whose leaves shall never fade;
And all that he does shall proper.
Not so are the wicked, not so!
Shall be driven away by the wind.
When the wicked are judged, they shall not stand,
Nor find room among those who are just,
For the Lord guards the way of the just,
But the way of the wicked leads to doom.
Give praise to the Father Almighty,
To his Son, Jesus Christ, the Lord,
To the Spirit who dwells in our hearts
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hark Hark The Angels Singing
firstLine: Hark, hark, the Angels singing,
verses: And thou Lord God of Host.
Thus may we sing in glory,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hark My Soul How Everything
firstLine: Hark, my soul, how everything
verses: Strives to serve our bounteous King;
Sings its part, and then obeys.
While the grove their song applauds.
Night and day they warbling run,
Never pause, but still sing on.
All the flowers that gild the spring,
If heaven bless them, thankful, they
Smell more sweet, and look more gay.
This short office to our Lord;
We, on whom his bounty flows,
All things give, and nothing owes.
Wake, for shame, my sluggish heart,
Wake, and gladly sing your part;
Learn of birds, and springs, and flowers,
How to use your noblest powers.
Call whole nature to your aid;
Who to one God all belong.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hark A Herald Voice Is Sounding
firstLine: Hark! a herald voice is sounding,
verses: Hark! a herald voice is sounding,
O ye children of the day!"
Christ her sun, all sloth dispelling,
Lo! the Lamb so long expected,
Come with pardon down from heaven;
Let us haste, with tears of sorrow,
One and all to be forgiven.
So when next he comes with glory,
Shrouding all the earth in fear,
