May he then as our defender,
On the clouds of heaven appear.
To the Father and the Son,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hark Hark My Soul!
firstLine: Hark! hark, my soul!
verses: How sweet the truth those blessed strains are telling,
Of that new life when sin shall be no more.
Angels of Jesus, angels of light,
Singing to welcome the pilgrims of the night.
Onward we go, for still we hear them singing,
Come, weary soul, for Jesus bids you come,
And through the dark, its echoes sweetly ringing,
The music of the gospel leads us home.
Far, faraway, like bell at evening pealing,
The voice of Jesus sounds o'er land and sea,
And laden souls, by thousands meekly stealing
King shepherd, turn their weary steps to thee.
Though life be long and dreary,
The day must dawn and dark-some night be past;
All journeys end in welcome to the weary,
And Heaven, the heart's true home, Will come at last.
Angels sing on, your faithful watches keeping,
Sing us sweet fragments of the songs above,
Till life's long night shall break in endless love.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hark How The Banks Of Jordan Ring
firstLine: Hark! how the banks of Jordan ring,
verses: Hark! how the banks of Jordan ring,
The conscious earth, and sea and sky,
Welcomes his advent from on high,
Who did their groaning fabric build,
The coming of their King abide?
Strew all his path, and lodging meet,
Prepare a royal guest to greet.
Jesus, our souls with health endow;
Creatures of earth without your aid,
We languish like the flowers that fade.
Stretch out your hand, we faint no more;
The fallen to their feet restore,
Show but your face, and wintry earth,
Shall bring her dallying flowers to birth.
Let praise beyond all praise be yours;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hark The Hearld Angels Sing
firstLine: Hark! the herald angels sing:
verses: Peace on earth and mercy mild:
Join the triumph of the skies;
Hail the heaven-born Prince of Peace!
Light and life to all He brings,
Risen with healing in His wings.
Mild He lays His glory by,
Born that man no more may die;
Born to raise the sons of earth,
Born to give them second birth.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bring Them In
firstLine: Hark!
verses: Out in the desert dark and dreary,
Calling the sheep who've gone astray,
Far from the shepherd<'s fold away.
Bring them in, bring them in,
Bring them in from the fields of sin.
Bring them in, bring them in,
Bring the wandering ones to Jesus.
Who'll go and help this shepherd kind,
Help Him the wandering ones to find?
Who'll bring the lost ones to the fold
Where they'll be sheltered from the cold?
Out in the desert hear their cry,
Out on the mountains wild and high.
Hark! 'tis the Master speaks to thee,
"Go find my sheep where-e'r they be."
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Have You Been To Jesus For The Cleansing Power
firstLine: Have you been to Jesus
verses: Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
Are you fully trusting in His grace this hour?
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
Are you washed, are you washed,
In the blood, in the blood,
In the soul cleansing blood of the Lamb?
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
Are you walking daily by the Saviour's side?
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
Do you rest each moment in the Crucified?
Are you washed in the Blood of the Lamb?
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
Will your soul be ready for the mansions bright
And be washed in the blood of the Lamb?
Lay aside the garments that are stained with sin,
And be washed in the blood of the Lamb,
There's a fountain flowing for the soul unclean,
O be washed in the blood of the Lamb.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: He Bought The Whole Field
firstLine: He bought the whole field for joy,
verses: He bought the whole field for joy,
He bought the whole field for sheer joy.
A man found a treasure, it was hidden in a field,
He bought the whole field for joy.
Paul was a Hebrew by birth,
He left his land to inherit the earth.
And when the seed gave yield in the hearts of a few,
A rich young man searching for truth,
Who kept his commandments the whole of his youth,
If he gave all he had.
But he did not have the vision of Paul,
Who bought the whole field for joy.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: He Is Lord
firstLine: He is Lord /2ce
verses: He has risen from the dead and He is Lord;
Every knee shall bow, every tongue confess
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: He Leadeth Me
firstLine: He leadeth me! O blessed thought,
verses: He leadeth me! O blessed thought,
O words with heavenly comfort fraught;
Whate'er I do, where'er I be,
Still 'tis God's hand that leadeth me.
He leadeth me! He leadeth me!
By His own hand He leadeth me;
His faithful follower I would be,
For by His hand He leadeth me.
By waters still, o'er troubled sea,
Still 'tis His hand that leadeth me.
Lord, I would clasp Thy hand in mine,
Since 'tis my God that leadeth me.
And when my task on earth is done,
When, by Thy grace, the victory's won,
E'en death's cold wave I will not flee,
Since God through Jordan leadeth me.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: His Bountiful Care
firstLine: He sendeth the sunshine and rain...
verses: He sendeth the sunshine and rain...
He scattereth dew on the flowers...
He maketh the desert to bloom...
His blessings descend as the showers...
Boun...tiful, mer...ciful, won...derful love.Boun...tiful, mer...ciful, won...derful love.
He causeth the rivers to flow...
The fields now with verdure are clad...
He tempers the wind to his lamp...
The earth with His sunshine is glad...
For bounty so royal and free...
..Oh, help us to praise thee, dear Lord...
And serve thee in all that we do...
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hear O Thou Bounteous Maker
firstLine: Hear, O thou bounteous Maker, hear,
verses: Hear, O thou bounteous Maker, hear,
Our humble vows with gracious ear;
Turn not thy saving face away,
Whilst on this solemn feast we pray.
Great searcher of our hearts, to thee,
Behold, we to thy mercies fly,
Do thou thy healing grace apply.
Great are our sins, O Lord, but thou
Canst pardon more than we can do;
May our defects, like shadows, raise
The beauty and the life of grace.
May fasts extinguish in our will,
The fuel and desire of oil,
And thus our souls from fetters free
May only thirst and follow thee.
Our claim to reign with thee above.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hear Thy Children Gentle Jesus
firstLine: "Hear thy children, gentle Jesus,
verses: While we breathe our evening prayer,
Save us from all harm and danger,
Save us from the wiles of Satan,
Mid the lone and sleepful night,
From thy great white throne above,
All the night thy heart is wakeful,
Shades of heaven fast are falling,
When the shades of death fall round us,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hear Us O Lord
firstLine: Hear us, O Lord, and have mercy upon us,
verses: Hear us, O Lord, and have mercy upon us,
Hear us, O Lord, and have mercy upon us,
For we have sinned against thee.
For we have sinned against thee.
King high exalted, all the world's redeemer,
To thee thy children lift their eyes with weeping;
Christ, we implore thee, hear our supplications.
Right hand of Godhead, headstone of the corner,
Path of salvation, gate of heaven's kingdom,
Cleanse thou thy people, stained with their transgressions.
Make lamentation in thy holy hearing;
Gracious, grant thou to our sins indulgence.
Humbly confess we, who have sinned against thee,
All our misdoings, hidden now no longer,
May thy redeeming mercy find us pardon.
Brought by false witness unto death for sinners,
Christ do thou keep us whom thy blood hath ransomed.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Help Lord The Souls
firstLine: Help, Lord, the souls that thou hast made,
verses: Help, Lord, the souls that thou hast made,
The souls to thee so dear,
In prison for the debt unpaid,
These holy souls, they suffer on,
Until thy high behest is done,
For daily falls, for pardoned crime,
The shadow of thy cross sublime,
Oh, by their patience of delay,
Their sacred Zeal to burn away,
Oh, by their fire of love, not less
Oh, by thy own great Name.
The souls to thee most dear,
In prison for the debt unpaid,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Here We Are
firstLine: Here we are, all together;
verses: As we pray we'll always be.
Join we now as friends, and celebrate
The brotherhood we share, all as one.
Keep the fire burning, kindle it with care,
And we'll all join in and sing.
Let us make the world an alleluia,
Let us make the world a better place.
Keep a smile handy, have a helping hand,
Let us all join in and sing.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Herod Why Thrills Thy Heart
firstLine: Herod, why thrills thy heart with fear?
verses: Herod, why thrills thy heart with fear?
The royal Babe thou seekest here
Envies no earthly toys, for he
A heavenly crown doth offer thee.
In haste to Bethlehem that day
The wise men took their star-led way,
Their light to seek, where light doth shew,
Gifts on the Giver to bestow.
Killed at the tyrant's anxious call,
For Christ a thousand victims fall;
And mothers' hearts the piteous tale
Lo, dipped in Jordan's cleansing stream,
The Lamb of God would whiter seem;
Yet 'twas our sins, in foul array,
He bore, and bearing washed away.
That would his heavenly birth dispute;
The sick no more with fever burn,
And at his voice the dead return.
Swift its own nature to resign,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: His Yoke Is Easy
firstLine: " The Lord is my shepherd,
verses: He maketh me down to lie, in pastures green,
He leadeth me, the quiet waters by.
His yoke is easy, His burden is light,
I've found it so, I've found it so.
He leadeth me, by day and night,
And give me the strength to take
E'n for His own Name's, sake.
Yea, though I should walk in the valley of death
E'n yet will I fear no ill,
For thou art with me and thy rod and staff
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hold The Fort
firstLine: Ho! my comrades see the signal waving in the sky
verses: Ho! my comrades see the signal waving in the sky
Reinforcement now appearing, victory is nigh.
Hold the fort for I am coming,
Wave the answer back to heaven,
Hold the fort for I am coming,
Wave the answer back to heaven,
Mighty men around us falling courage almost gone.
In our leaders name we triumph, over every foe.
Fierce and long the battle rages,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Holy God We Praise Your Name
firstLine: Holy God, we praise your name;
verses: Holy God, we praise your name;
Lord of all, we bow before you!
All on earth your sceptre claim,
All in heaven above adore you,
Fill the heavens with sweet accord,
Holy Spirit, Three we name you,
Spare your people, Lord, we pray,
Lo, I put my trust in you -
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Holy Holy Holy! Lord God Almighty
firstLine: Chant:
verses: Early in the morning our song shall rise to thee;
God in three persons, blessed Trinity!
Early in the morning our song shall rise to thee;
God in three persons, blessed Trinity!
Cherubim and seraphim falling down before thee,
Which wert, and art and evermore shalt be.
All thy works shall praise thy name, on earth,
God in three persons, blessed Trinity.
Though the eye of sinful man thy glory
may not see, Only thou art holy, there is none beside thee
Perfect in power, in love, and purity.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Holy Holy Holy (Psalm 99)
firstLine: "Holy, holy is the Lord
verses: Holy, mighty, holy is the Lord.
Tremble, for the Lord is King
So bring Him glory, let us sing His glory ;
Blessings let us bring unto a very mighty, King;
Blessings let us bring unto a very mighty King.
Sisters, brothers, let us shout his praise
Praising His holy name all the day,
Justice is what our God loves
Justice that always comes from above,
Thank Him for His guiding hand,
Ask Him to bless our Father's land,
He came all in a pillar of flame,
Moses felt the Lord close by,
Giving a help from on high,
He answered every-one, just the same,
Hail Him who is King on high,
Love Him who hears our every cry,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Holy Mary Mother Mild
firstLine: Holy Mary, Mother mild,
verses: Hear, O hear thy feeble child,
Cast thy tender eyes on me,
Joy of angels, Queen of love,
Prove to me thy loving care,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Holy Queen We Bend Before Thee
firstLine: Holy Queen! we bend before thee,
verses: Holy Queen! we bend before thee,
Make us love thee, we implore thee;
Make us truly to be thine.
Teach, oh teach us Holy Mother ;
How to love and help each other;
How the prize of life to win.
Thou to whom a child was given,
Greater than the sons of men,
By the hope thy name inspires,
Be our doom reversed through thee,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Holy Spirit Lord Of Light (Ven I Sancte Spiritus)
firstLine: Holy Spirit, Lord of light,
verses: Come, thou Father of the poor,
Come, thou light of all that live!
Thou in toll art comfort sweet,
Solace in the midst of woe.
Visit thou these hearts of thine,
If thou take thy grace away,
Nothing pure in man will stay,
All his good is turned to ill.
Heal our wounds, our strength renew,
On our dryness pour thy dew,
Wash the stains of guilt away.
Bend the stubborn heart and will,
Melt the frozen, warm the chill,
Guide the steps that go astray.
Give them comfort when they die;
Give them life with thee on high;
Give them joys that never end.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Hosanna Hosanna Hosanna!
firstLine: Hosanna! Hosanna! Hosanna!
verses: Blessed is He that cometh, in the Name of the Lord
Blessed be the King of Israel, -
Blessed be the King of Israel, -
Blessed, Blessed, Blessed, Blessed, Blessed, Blessed.
Blessed, Is He that cometh in the Name of the Lord.
In the Name of the Lord,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: How Can I Repay The Lord (Psalm 155: 10 - 19)
firstLine: How can I repay the Lord
verses: How can I repay the Lord
I will raise the cup of salvation,
I will call on God*s name.(DC)D.S.
And when I said in my alarm:
How can I repay the Lord for his goodness to me
I will raise the cup of salvation,
I will call on God's name.
I trusted, even when I said:
And when I said in my alarm:
How can I repay the Lord
The cup of salvation I will raise
I will call on the Lord's name.
My vows to the Lord I will fulfil
O precious in the eyes of the Lord
Is the death of his faithful.
I will call on the Lord's name.
My vows to the Lord I will fulfil
In the courts of the house of the Lord.
Praise the Father, the Son, and Holy Spirit
The God who is, who was and is to come
At the end of the ages.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: How Excellent
firstLine: How excellent, how excellent,
verses: Is thy loving kindness O God,
Is your loving kindness O God.
Therefore the children of men put their trust,
Therefore the children of men, put their trust,
Therefore put their trust under the shadow of your wings.
With the fullness of your name,
And you shall make them drink
Of the river of your pleasure, .
And you shalt make them drink
Of the river of your pleasure,
With the fullness of your house,
And you shall make them drink,
Of the river of your pleasure.
Show your loving kindness unto them,
Your pleasures and you shall make them drink
Of the river of your pleasure,
That know you, unto them that know you and your righteousness,
Your pleasures, and you shall make them drink
Of the river of your pleasure to the upright in heart.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: How Great Is Your Name
firstLine: How great is your name for
verses: How great is your name for
Where it's written all over the sky
Where the stars sing their praise
Or the eagle that fly with
In the wide restless sea that
How clear is your voice for
To hear what you have to say
It is heard in the breeze
Or the rain drops that spray
But the heart hears it best
When the mind is at rest
Everywhere in the glance of a child
In a sinner's despair or a
Meet in a crowded city street
We can see where your face
Is in the whole human race.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: How Great Is Your Name
firstLine: How great is your name, O Lord our God
verses: How great is your name, O Lord our God
Your majesty is praised above the heaven;
on the lips of children and of babes
you have found praise to foil your enemy,
to silence the foe and the rebel.
When I see the heavens, the work of your hands,
the moon and the stars which you arranged,
what is man that you should keep him in
mind mortal man that you care for him?
Yet you have made him little less than a god;
gave him power over the works of your hand,
put all things under his feet.
All of them sheep and cattle,
birds of the air, and fish
that make their way through the waters.
Give glory to the Father Almighty,
to his Son, Jesus Christ, the Lord,
to the Spirit who dwells in our hearts,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: How Lovely Is Your Dwelling Place (Psalm 84)
firstLine: How Lovely is your dwelling place
verses: How Lovely is your dwelling place
My soul is longing and yearning,
Is yearning for the courts of the Lord.
My heart and my soul ring out their joy
The sparrow herself finds a home
And the swallow a nest for her brood;
She lays her young by your altars,
God of hosts, my King and my God.
They are happy who dwell in your house,
They are happy, whose strength is in you,
In whose hearts are the roads to Sion,
As they go through the bitter valley,
They make it a place of springs,
They will see the God of gods in Sion,
O Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer,
Give ear, O God of Jacob.
Turn your eyes, O God, our shield,
Look on the face of your anointed.
Is better than a thousand elsewhere,
The threshold of the house of God
I prefer to the dwellings of the wicked.
For the Lord God is a rampart, a shield;
He will give us His favour and glory .
The Lord will not refuse any good
To those who walk without blame.
Lord, God of hosts, happy the man
Give praise to the Father Almighty
To his Son, Jesus Christ, the Lord.
To the Spirit who dwells in our hearts,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: How Many Roads Must A Man Walk Down
firstLine: How many roads must a man walk down,
verses: How many roads must a man walk down,
How many seas must a white dove sail,
Before he sleeps in the sand,
How many times must the canon balls fly,
The answer my friend, is blowing in the wind,
The answer is blowing in the wind.
How many years can a mountain exist,
Before it's washed in the sea,
How many years can some people exist,
Before they're allowed to be free,
How many times can a man turn his head,
And pretend he just doesn't see.
How many times must a man look up,
Before he can see the sky,
How many ears must one have,
Before he can hear people cry,
How many deaths will it take till he knows
That too many people have died.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Howl My Soul
firstLine: Howl, you, for the day of the Lord is near,
verses: Howl, you, for the day of the Lord is near,
Howl, my soul, for the day of the Lord is here,
Let every knee be bent, every head be bowed,
For He will come like thunder tearing up the cloud.
Howl, my soul, tremble, my soul, in fear.
We ep, you, for he comes to judge the land,
We ep, my soul, for none but the just shall stand,
He will come in a whirl-wind to uproot the trees,
Yet He will rock my soul, for the judgment is at hand.
Sing, you, for the Lord was born of men,
Sing, my soul, for the Lord returns again,
He will breath new Spirit into old dry bones,
Trade a heart of flesh for a heart of stone,
Sing, my soul, sing my soul. Amen.
Peace, you, there's no need to be afraid,
Peace, my soul, for He knows of what you're made,
Though mountains crumble when He bares His breast,
In the crook of His arm I will lie to rest,
Peace, my soul, it is the moment for which you've prayed.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Humbly Let Us Voice Our Homage
firstLine: "Humbly let us voice our Homage
verses: What our senses fail to fathom
Let us grasp through faith's consent!
Let us sing with one accord!
Praised be Christ, His Son, our Lord;
Praised be God, the Holy Spirit;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Humbly We Adore Thee
firstLine: Christ, our God and brother
verses: God, the mighty, thou hast come,
Son of Adam, still thou art,
Thou who died to save us,
We in grief confess our guilt;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Am The Bread Of Life
firstLine: I am the bread of life,
verses: I am the bread of life,
He who comes to me shall not hunger
He who believes in me shall not thirst.
No one can come to me
And I will raise him up,
and I will raise him up.
I will raise him up on the last day.
The bread that I will give,
Is my flesh for the life of the world,
And he who eats of this bread,
Of the flesh of the Son of Man,
You shall not have life within you.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Am Thine O Lord
firstLine: I am Thine, O Lord,
verses: I have heard Thy voice, and it told Thy love to me;
But I long to rise in the arms of faith,
And be closer drawn to Thee.
Draw me nearer, nearer, blessed Lord,
To the cross where Thou hast died;
Draw me nearer, nearer, blessed Lord,
Thy service, LordBy the power of grace divine;
Let my soul look up with a steadfast hope,
And my will be lost in Thine.
O the pure delight of a open hour
That before Thy throne I spend,
When I kneel in prayer, and with Thee, my God,
I commune as friend with friend!
There are depths of love that I cannot know
Till I pass the narrow sea;
There are heights of joy that I may not reach
Till I rest in peace with Thee.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Yet I Believe
firstLine: I believe God lay sleeping in the tomb,
verses: I believe God lay sleeping in the tomb,
Waiting until the day should break
I believe my Father has care of me
And someday, He'll say, "My child awake
I believe man was born to die,
I believe that man was meant to cry,
I believe that the stars are mine,
Love lifts the stars from the night,
I believe there's always sun to shine,
Though sometimes it's hidden from sight.
I believe man must go alone,
To ssed like a wave on the sea,
Yet I believe that man must have a home,
God holds a mansion for me.
I believe man was born for joy,
Some men must find it in pain,
Some rivers run through caves beneath the ground,
Sometimes there's sun in the rain.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Believe That Jesus Died For Me
firstLine: Key Eb: 4/4
verses: I believe that Jesus died for me
A sacrifice beyond my understanding offering himself in place of me
O how can I repay the Lord.
Jesus to you this gift we bring
Holy Ghost accept this offering which we offer up to you D/C
I believe all that the scripture said about the love beyond my understanding
O how can I repay the Lord.
Teach me Lord to love you as I aught
O teach me Lord to love you all my life time spreading love where ever find my self,
O how can I repay I the Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Wedding Banquet
firstLine: I cannot come,
verses: I cannot come to the banquet,
I have bought me a cow,
A certain man held a feast,
On his fine estate in town,
He laid a festive table and wore a wedding gown,
To his neighbours far and wide,
But when the meal was ready,
The master rose up in anger,
Called his servants by name, said:
"Go into the town, fetch the blind and the lame,
Fetch the peasant and the pauper,
And my table must be filled
When all the poor had assembled,
There was still, room to spare,
And force them to come in,
Now God has written a lesson
For that great and glorious day,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Come To Thee
firstLine: I come to thee once more my God,
verses: I come to thee once more my God,
For I have sought thee wide world through
Though bright and many are the spots,
Where I have built a nest,
Yet in the brightest still I pined
Riches could bring me joy and power
And they were fair to see,
Yet Gold was but a sorry god
Then honour and world's good word
Yet could I rest on bliss that hung
For thou hast made this wondrous soul
Ah, send thy sweet transforming word
To make it more thine own.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Need Thee Every Hour
firstLine: " I need thee every hour,
verses: I need thee, O I need thee;
O bless me now, my Saviour,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Joy Is Like The Rain
firstLine: I saw rain drops on my window,
verses: I saw rain drops on my window,
I saw clouds upon a mountain,
I saw Christ in wind and thunder,
Whipped by wind yet still afloat,
I saw rain drops on the river,
Bit by bit the river grows,
Till all at once it overflows,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I The Lord Of Sea And Sky
firstLine: I the Lord of sea and sky,
verses: I the Lord of sea and sky,
I have heard my people cry
All who dwell in dark and sin
I who made the stars of night,
Here I am Lord, it is I, Lord,
I will go Lord, if You lead me,
I the Lord of snow and rain,
I have borne my people's pain,
I have wept for love of them,
I will break their hearts of stone,
Give them heart of love alone.
I will speak my words to them
I the Lord of wind and flame,
I will tend the poor and lame
I will set a feast for them
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Want To Sing (Psalm 108:1-5)
firstLine: I want to sing,
verses: I want to shout your praise, O Lord!
Awake and play, my harp and zither!
Let the dawning day join in praise.
My heart is ready to praise you, O Lord;
To bless you, God of my salvation, alleluia!
For great and wonderful your glory, my God;
Your name is great above the heavens, alleluia
Lord, your mercy overshadows the earth;
Your glory lights the firmament on high, Alleluia.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Was Glad (Psalm 122)
firstLine: I was glad, I was glad, when they said unto me;
verses: I was glad, I was glad, when they said unto me;
We will go into the house of the Lord,
I was glad, I was glad, when they said unto me,
We will go into the house of the Lord.
Our feet shall stand within your gates,
They shall proper, they shall prosper that love you
They shall prosper that love you. Amen,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Will Seek And Find Thee Now
firstLine: I will seek and find thee now,
verses: I will seek and find thee now,
I will lay my heart to rest,
Thou art pure and undefiled, undefiled.
I am a weak and helpless child,
Yet thy word hath said, to me:
Little ones may come to Thee,
Yet the little ones may come to Thee.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I Will Sing I Will Sing
firstLine: I will sing, I will sing a song
verses: I will sing, I will sing a song
Alleluia, alleluia, glory to the Lord (3X)
We will come, we will come
If the Son, if the Son shallMake you free (3X)
They that sow in tears shallReap in joy (3X)
In His name, in His name, We have the victory (3X)
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesu Dulcis Memoria
firstLine: Jesu dulcis memoria,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: If We Eat Of The Lord
firstLine: "If we eat of the Lord,
verses: And we drink of the Lord,
Like the Lord we shall all be.
Then we live with His life
And we share in His love,
And his truth will make us free.
The Lord tells us His plans,
" To the Father come through me;
Eat my flesh, drink my blood,
Love each man as I love you.
By this sign all will know
Through this love, man I renew."
Bring my love to the world,
Be a sing for all to see,
That I bring man my peace
That I come to make him free.
If we eat of the Lord
And we drink of the Lord,
Like the Lord we shall all be.
Then we will live with His life
And we share in His love,
And His truth will make us free.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: I'll Sing A Hymn To Mary
firstLine: I'll sing a hymn to Mary,
verses: I'll sing a hymn to Mary,
To love and bless thy name.
The ark of God's own promise,
The gate of Heaven to me,
To live and not to love thee,
Would fill my soul with shame;
I'll love and bless thy name.
The saints are high in glory,
Oh, that which God did give thee,
I'll love and bless thy name.
But in the crown of Mary,
As Queen of all the angels,
" No sin hath e'er defied thee, "
I'll love and bless thy name.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Immaculate Mary
firstLine: Immaculate Mary!
verses: Now to God be all glory,
To love and to serve Thee
In the hour of her mourning,
We pray for the Vicar of Christ
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: In Christ There Is No East Or West
firstLine: In Christ there is no East or West,
verses: In Christ there is no East or West,
In Him no South or North,
But one great fellowship of love
In Him shall true hearts everywhere
His service is the golden cord
Join hands then, brothers of the faith,
Who serves my Father as a son
In Christ now meet both East and We st.
In him meet South and North,
All Christly souls are one in Him,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: In Paradisum
firstLine: In paradisum dedúcant te ángeli:
verses: in tuo adventu suscipiant te mártyres,
et perdúcant te in civitátem sanctam Jerúsalem.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: In Your Body Lord
firstLine: In your Body, Lord, we are one (3X)
verses: In your Body, Lord, we are one (3X)
To all men, Lord, grant your peace (3X)
Give thy Spirit, Lord, to us all (3X)
Make our hearts, Lord, great like Thine (3X)
In this banquet, Lord, we find strength, (3X)
Draw us nearer, Lord, each to each (3X)
Fill our hearts, Lord, with your care (3X)
Bless us, Lord, with your love (3X)
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: 4/4 Into Your Sanctuary
firstLine: Into your sanctuary we've come to adore you Lord (2x)
verses: Into your sanctuary we've come to adore you Lord (2x)
In adoration and praise, we glorify your holy name,
Into you sanctuary we've come to adore you Lord.
We thank you Lord for the gift of life in us
We reverence your holy name oh our Lord.
With sweet melody of joy, our mouth is full of praise, for one day
In you house is better than a thousand else where
Hide us under the shadow of your wings, that we may over
Come the tribulations of the world.
We sing to you, messiah, for told by the prophets, we give you praise
O son of David and son of Abraham
We sing to you, messiah the hope of the people, we give you praise
O Christ, our Lord and king humble meek of heart.
We sing to you, the way to the father in heaven
We give you praise, the way of truth and way of all grace and light.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: It's A Long Road To Freedom
firstLine: It's a long road to freedom,
verses: It's a long road to freedom,
But when you walk in love,
With the wind of your wing,
And cover the earth with the songs you sing,
I walked one morning by the sea,
And all the waves reached out to me,
I took their tears, then let them be.
I walked one morning at the dawn,
When bits of night still lingered on,
I sought my star, but it was gone.
I walked one morning with a friend,
And prayed the day would never end,
I walked one morning with the king,
And all my winters turned to spring,
Yet every moment held its sting.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: 'Tis The Month Of Our Mother
firstLine: 'Tis the month of our Mother ,
verses: When our lips and our spirits
Are glowing with love and with praise.
Be the fairest of seasons, sweet May.
That the love of their Mother
Hath ever a solace for woe.
And what joy to the erring.
That a trust in her guidance,
Will lead to a glorious goal.
Then God hath so honoured our race,
As to clothe with our nature,
Sweet Mary, the Mother of Grace.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jerusalem My Happy Home
firstLine: Jerusalem, my happy home,
verses: When shall I come to thee?
When shall my sorrows have an end?
Thy joys when shall I see?
O happy harbour of the saints!
In thee no sorrow may be found,
No grief, no care, no toil.
In thee no sickness may be seen,
No hurt, no ache, no sore;
In thee there is no dread of death,
No dampish mist is seen in thee,
There every soul shines as the sun;
There lust and lucre cannot dwell;
There is no hunger, heat, nor cold,
God grant I once may see
Thy endless joys, and of the same
Thy walls are made of precious stones,
Thy gates are of right orient pearl,
Thy very streets are paved with gold,
Thy tiles are made of beaten gold -
O God that I were there.
Within thy gates no thing doth come
No spider's web, no dirt, no dust,
No filth may there be seen.
Would God I were in thee!
Would God my woes were at an end,
Thy joys that I might see!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus Eternal Truth Sublime
firstLine: Jesus eternal truth sublime,
verses: Thou crown of those who through all time,
Thy suppliant people through the prayers,
For his dear sake thy wrath forbear,
Who saw Him go upon his way,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus Gentlest Saviour
firstLine: Jesus, gentlest Saviour,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus Is God!
firstLine: Jesus is God!
verses: The countless stars, the golden dust,
That strew the skies at night,
The wheeling storm, the dreadful fire,
The summer's sun, the winter's frost,
Songs of adoring praise to Him,
He was true God in Bethlehem's crib,
He who in heaven eternal reigned,
All are worthwhile for all are means,
Worthwhile a thousand years of life,
If by our credo we might own,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus Keep Me Near The Cross
firstLine: Jesus, keep me near the cross,
verses: Jesus, keep me near the cross,
In the cross, in the cross,
Till my raptured soul shall find
Love and mercy found me; There the bright and morning
Star Sheds its beams around me.
Near the cross! O Lamb of God,
me walk from day to day,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus My Lord Behold At Length The Day
firstLine: Jesus, my Lord, behold at length the day,
verses: Jesus, my Lord, behold at length the day,
When I resolve from sin to turn away.
I will never more offend thee,
Since my poor soul thy precious Blood has cost;
Suffer it not to be forever lost.
Kneeling in tears, behold me at thy feet:
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus My Lord My God My All
firstLine: Jesus, my Lord, my God, my all,
verses: Jesus, my Lord, my God, my all,
How can l love you as l ought?
And how revere this wondrous gift,
So far surpassing hope or thought?
Oh, make us love you more and more.
Had l but Mary's sinless heart,
To love you with, my dearest King,
Oh, with what bursts of fervent praise,
Your goodness, Jesus would l sing!
Ah see! within a creature's hand,
The vast Creator deigns to be,
On Joseph's arm, or Mary's knee.
Your body, soul and Godhead, all,
I cannot compass all l have,
For all you have and are, are mine.
Sound, sound His praises higher still,
And come, ye angels, to our aid;
Whose power man and angels made.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus My Lord To Thee I Cry
firstLine: "Jesus, my Lord, to Thee I cry;
verses: "Jesus, my Lord, to Thee I cry;
Oh, bring Thy free salvation nigh,
And take me as I am.
And take me as I am,
and take me as I am,
Oh, take me as I am.
Helpless I am, and full of guilt;
But yet for me Thy blood was spilt,
And Thou canst make me what Thou wilt,
And take me as I am.
My best resolves I only break,
Yet save me for Thine own name's sake,
And take me as I am.
Behold me, Saviour at thy feet;
Deal with me as Thou see-est me;
And take me as I am.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus My Saviour Is All Things To Me
firstLine: Jesus my Saviour is all things to me,
verses: Jesus my Saviour is all things to me,
Oh what a wonderful Saviour is He,
Guiding protection o'er life troubled sea,
Jesus for me, Jesus for me,
All the time everywhere, Jesus for me.
Jesus in sickness, and Jesus in health,
Jesus in poverty, comfort or wealth,
unshine or tempest, whatever it be,
He is my safety, Jesus for me.
He is my refuge, my rock and my tower,
He is my treasure, my strength and my power,
Life everlasting, my daysman is he,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Jesus Thou Art Coming
firstLine: (Adoration and Faith) Jesus Thou art coming,
verses: (Adoration and Faith) Jesus Thou art coming,
Thou, the God who made me,
(Humility and Sorrow) Who am I, my Jesus,
I am very I have caused Thee pain,
Put Thy kind arms around me.
(Love and Desire) Dearest Lord, love Thee,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Joy To The World
firstLine: Joy to the world! the Lord is come;
verses: Joy to the world! the Lord is come;
Let every heart prepare Him room,
And heav'n and heav'n and nature sing.
Joy to the world! the Saviour reigns;
While fields and floods, rocks, hills and plains,
He rules the world, with truth and grace,
And wonders, and wonders of his love.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Joy-bells Ringing
firstLine: Joy-bells ringing, children singing,
verses: Fill the air with music sweet;
Make the chain of song complete.
List, list, the song that swells -
Hark their voices loud and clear,
Breaking o'er us, like a chorus
When the heart is free from care;
Skies are cheering, and we're hearing
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Just A Closer Walk With Thee
firstLine: Just a closer walk with Thee,
verses: Just a closer walk with Thee,
Grant it Jesus, is my plea,
Let it be dear Lord, let it be.
I am weak but Thou art strong,
Jesus, keep me from all wrongs,
I'll be satisfied as long as, I walk,
Let me walk close to Thee.
Through this world of toils and snares I'll falter,
Lord who cares, None but Thee my burden shares,
None but Thee dear Lord, none but Thee.
When my feeble life is o'er,
Times for me would be no more
To Thy kingdom's shore, to Thy shore.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Just As I Am
firstLine: Just as I am, without one plea,
verses: Just as I am, without one plea,
But that Thy blood was shed for me,
And that Thou bid'st me come to Thee,
O Lamb of God, I come, I come.
Just as I am, though tossed about
O Lamb of God, I come, I come.
Just as I am, poor, wretched, blind;
Sight, riches, healing of the mind,
Yea, all I need in Thee to find,
O Lamb of God, I come, I come.
Just as I am, Thou wilt receive
O Lamb of God, I come, I come.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

verses: K'anyi jenu nara, k'anyi jenu nara nu
Kristi nri n'enye ndu Ahu dinwenu no n'udi achicha na
mmanya n'k'onyere umuazu ya na oriri ikpe azu (mgbe ogwara ha si)

rie nu ahu n'obara Kristi Ahu n'obara Kristi di omimi esekpuoro mgi ahu
