title: Keep In Mind Jesus Christ
firstLine: Keep in mind that Jesus Christ has died for us and is risen from the dead.
verses: Keep in mind that Jesus Christ has died for us and is risen from the dead.
He is Joy for all ages.
If we endure with the Lord,
We shall reign with the Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Keep Step With The Master
firstLine: Keep step with the Master, whatever betide;
verses: Keep step with the Master, whatever betide;
While foes are alluring, and danger is near;
Daily walk... with Christ your Saviour,
He will lead you all the journey through.
Keep step with the Master, wherever you go;
The light of His presence your path will illumine,
And make all the desert a garden of bloom.
Keep step with the Master, nor halt by the way;
Whate'er He commands you, oh, haste to obey!
Arise at His bidding, press on in His might,
While walking with Jesus, you're sure to be right.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: 4/4 Kele Chineke Jaa Ya Mma
firstLine: Kele Chineke jaa ya mma,
verses: Ekele idi mma eh - onye ker'uwa Ony'oma ony'oma, ony'oma. DC
Soronum tobe ya, tobenu Chineke Ekele diri gi nna, imela,
K'anyi ja ya mma, bunye y'onyinye Anyi ekele gi nna imela.
Anyi g'eji gini kele gi, Ezebube k'ibu Otito na nsopuru diri gi Eze daa luo
Ihu n'anya Chineke kasi akasi Oliseburuwa Chineke daa luo imela.
Nara onyinye umu gi n'enye gi Nara aja, anyi n'achunyere gi.
Onyinye nwanyi ajadu, k'ayi n'ebunye gi Nara ya gozie ya Chukwu onye ebube.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: King Of Glory King Of Peace
firstLine: King of glory, King of peace, I will love you, And that love may never cease, I will love you, Thou hast granted my request, Thou hast heard me.
verses: King of glory, King of peace, I will love you, And that love may never cease, I will love you, Thou hast granted my request, Thou hast heard me.
Thou didst note my working breast, Thou hast spared me.
Wherefore with my utmost art, I will sing you, And the dream of all my heart, I will sing you, Though my sins against me cried, Thou didst clear me, And alone when they replied, You did hear me.
Seven whole days, not one in seven I will praise you,
In my heart though not in heaven, I can praise you, Small it is in this poor sort, to enclose you, Even eternity's too short to extol you.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Kwebe N'ukwe 'N'etinu Nkpu Onu
firstLine: Key Ab: 6/8
verses: Kwebe n'ukwe 'n'etinu nkpu onu, N'ihi na onye no n'etiti unu bu nnukwu onye di nso nke Isreal
N'ezie chineke bu onye nzoputa m of enwere m nchekwube egwu agaghi atum N'ihina dinwenu bu ikem , ukwe m O bu onye nzoputa m unu g'eji onu kuru mmiri n'umi nke nzoputa
Na enye nu dinwenu Ekene n'etonu aha ya, mee nu ka oluaka ya rute madu nile nti, n'akowaputanu ka - aha ya si dika mkpa.
Buoro nu dinwenu abu oma Nihi na oruru olu oma, meenu Kanu ya n'uwa nile, ndi nke zion kwebenu ukwe, n'etinu mkpu onu Nihi na onye no n'etiti unu bu onye di nso nke Isreal.
Alaeze nke Isreal kwebenu ukwe otito nye dinwenu, Gosinu na ike n'ebubu nke chukwu di nala Isreal nile, n'ihi nkea werenu opi ike n'ubo akwara, buoro dinwenu abu.
Otito diri nna na nwa na muo nso etu odi na mbigo, ugbu a gadigide kwotua rue mgbe ebighiebi - Amen.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Kyrie
firstLine: Kyrie eleison,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lauda Sion Salvatorem
firstLine: Lauda, Sion, Salvatorem,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lead Kindly Light
firstLine: Lead, kindly light, amid the encircling gloom,
verses: Lead, kindly light, amid the encircling gloom,
The night is dark, and I am far from home,
I do not ask to see
I was not ever thus, nor prayed that
I loved to choose and see my path;
So long Thy power hath blest me, sure it still
O'er moor and fen, o'er crag and torrent,
And with the morn those Angel faces smile,
Which I have loved long since,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lead Us Heavenly Father
firstLine: Lead us heavenly Father, lead us,
verses: Lead us heavenly Father, lead us,
Guard us, guide us, keep us, feed us,
For we have no help but Thee.
If our God and Father be.
All our weakness Thou dost know,
Thou did thread this before us,
Thou didst feel its keenest woe;
Through the desert Thou didst go.
Fill our hearts with heavenly joy,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Let All Mortal Flesh Keep Silence
firstLine: " Let all mortal flesh keep silence,
verses: " Let all mortal flesh keep silence,
And with fear and trembling stand,
For with blessing in his hand
Christ, our God, to earth descendeth,
King of Kings, yet born of Mary,
As of old on earth he stood,
Lord of Lords in human vesture,
In the Body and the Blood
He will give to all the faithful
His own self for heav'nly food.
Rank on rank the host of heaven
Spreads its vanguard on the way,
As the Light of light descendeth
That the pow'rs of hell may vanish
At his feet the six-winged seraph;
Veil their faces to the Presence,
As with ceaseless voice they cry:
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Let The Heavens Be Glad
firstLine: Let the heavens be glad and the earth rejoice,
verses: Let the heavens be glad and the earth rejoice,
Before the Lord, for he comes, for he comes, for he comes.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Let Thy Priest O Lord
firstLine: Let Thy Priest O Lord be clothed with justice,
verses: Let Thy Priest O Lord be clothed with justice,
Turn not away the face of Thy Anointed.
O sing to the Lord, sing to the Lord,
Make known his deeds among the people.
God has set his people, people free,
(After verse 3 please repeat chorus two times)
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Let Us Break Bread Together At The Altar
firstLine: Let us break bread together at the altar,
verses: Let us break bread together at the altar,
Break bread together we are one;
For when we are together Christ is with us,
Break bread together for we are one.
Let us break bread together at the altar,
Break bread together we are one;
Let us drink wine together at the altar,
Let us offer together gifts at the altar,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Let Us Break Bread Together On Our Knees
firstLine: Let us break bread together on our knees;
verses: Let us break bread together on our knees;
Let us break bread together on our knees;
When I fall down on my knees with my face
O Lord, have mercy on me.
When I fall down on my knees with my face
O Lord, have mercy on me.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Let Us All Come And Bow
firstLine: Let us come to him with song,
verses: Let us come to him with song,
Sing the glory of his name,
For the Lord God is Almighty,
For His powers are greater than all,
Let us come to him with song.
Let us all come and bow,
Let us all come and kneel,
For His mercies bide with us.
Let us come and sing to the Lord,
Let us make a joyful noise,
For the Lord God is Almighty,
All the earth is in his hands,
Heaven and the mountain are His,
And the sea that he had made,
And the whole earth are his hand made.
Let us all come and bow down,
Let us all come and kneel down,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Líbera Me Dómine
firstLine: Líbera me, Dómine,
verses: Quando cœli movéndi sunt et terra
Dum véneris iudicáre sæculum per ignem.
Tremens factus sum ego, et tímeo, dum discussio vénerit, atque ventúra ira.
Dies illa, dies iræ, calamitátis et misériæ, dies magna et amára valde.
Dum véneris iudicáre sæculum per ignem.
Réquiem ætérnam dona eis, et lux perpétua lúceat eis.
Líbera me, Dómine, de morte ætérna, in die illa tremenda. Quando coeli movendi sunt et terra, Dum véneris iudicáre sæculum per ignem.
Et. clamor meus ad te veniat.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Light Of Christ (Psalm 27)
firstLine: Light of Christ, light our way,
verses: Light of Christ, light our way,
Light our way as we go.
The Lord is my light and my salvation,
The Lord is the stronghold of my life,
of whom shall I be afraid?
He will hide me under the cover of His wings.
He will set me high on a rock.
And I shall dwell in the house of the Lord,
All the days of my life.
The Lord is a lamp unto my feet.
He is a light to my path.
His light shall break forth like the dawn.
Arise for the light has come.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Like A Sunflower
firstLine: Like a sunflower
verses: That follows every movement of the sun,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Like Olive Branches (Psalm 128)
firstLine: Like olive branches around the table of the Lord, so God's children in the Church.
verses: Like olive branches around the table of the Lord, so God's children in the Church.
May the God of Israel join you in one;
In joy and in sorrow, the Lord is your way.
Blessed those who fear the Lord,
For they shall find favour in his sight.
Blessed be the toil of hands,
In joy and in gladness shall you work.
With good fortune in your home shall you be blessed;
Your wife shall be like a fruitful vine.
Blessed shall your children be around your board;
Like strong olive branches shall they stand.
Blest indeed, shall be the man who serves the Lord;
For he shall prevail throughout his life.
May the Lord who dwells on high give joy to you;
And may he protect you all your life.
May the sight of many sons delight your heart;
May God grant his peace to Israel.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Like The Deer That Yearns (Psalm 42)
firstLine: "My soul is thirsting for the Lord:
verses: "My soul is thirsting for the Lord:
When shall I see Him face to face?
I shall go to the house of God with songs of joy.
My soul is thirsting for God,
When can I enter and see
My tears have become my bread
As I hear it said all day long:
Why are you cast down my soul,
Hope in God, I will praise Him still
Praise the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit,
The God who is, who was and who will be,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Little King So Fair And Sweet
firstLine: Little King, so fair and sweet,
verses: Little King, so fair and sweet,
See us gathered round Thy feet;
Be Thou Monarch of our School,
We will be Thy subjects true,
Brave to suffer, brave to do,
All our hearts to Thee we bring
Take them, keep them little King.
Raise Thy little Hand to bless
Bless our sorrow and our pain,
That each cross may be our gain,
By Thine own sweet childhood, Lord
Set Thy seal on everything, Which we do,
Be our Teacher when we learn,
All the hard to easy turn;
Be our Playmate when we play,
So we shall indeed be gay,
Keep us happy, keep us pure,
All its days to Thee we bring,
Bless them, guard them, little King.
Be our Leader in the fight,
In the darkness be our Light,
O'er the rough and o'er the smooth,
We will try to follow Thee,
To Thy mantle we will cling,
Help us, save us, little King.
Little King, so dear and sweet,
Here we cast before Thy feet
All we are or yet may be,
All our body, all our soul
Let them all Thy praises sing,
Let us in the noisy world,
In an age of ease and pride,
In an age which seeks its way,
While Thy simple Truth shall ring,
In word and act, O little King.
Call Thy children to Thy home,
In that gentle Voice of Thine, Which we know,
sweet child Divine, At the gate,
O! meet us thus, As we loved Thee -
To Thine own, O little King.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Mabuza Mabuza
firstLine: Key Ab: 3/4
verses: E E nku mu wa bi leko o yam ba
E E E mabuza mabuza tata oyo mba a
to-bon-ze-liyo ba papa bonso tata oyo mba
To -bon-ze -liyo ba mama bonso tata oyamba
To -bonze- liyo bile-nye bonso tata oyamba.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Mary's Boy Child
firstLine: Long time ago in Bethlehem
verses: And man will live for evermore,
While shepherds watched their flocks by night,
They saw a bright new shining star,
The music seemed to come from afar.
Now Joseph and his wife Mary,
She found no place to bear her child,
Not a open room was in sight.
By and by they found a little nook,
And in a manger cold and dark,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Look Beyond The Bread You Eat
firstLine: Look beyond the bread you eat,
verses: Look beyond the bread you eat,
See Your Saviour and your Lord;
Look beyond the cup you drink,
See His love poured out as blood.
Give us a sign that we might believe in You,
Moses gave us manna from the sky.
He who eats this bread will never die.
The bread I give you will be my very flesh
My blood will truly be your drink.
who can listen to His word,
We shall no longer go with Him.
Lord, to whom shall we go?
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lord Accept The Gifts We Offer
firstLine: Lord, accept the gifts we offer,
verses: Lord, accept the gifts we offer,
Bread and Wine to be transformed now
Through the action of your priest (priests)
Take us too, O Lord, transform us
May your grace in us increase.
May our souls be pure and spotless.
As the host of wheat so fine,
May all stain of sin be crushed out,
Like the grape that forms the wine,
Which we give through Christ our Saviour
And our faith and love renew.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lord Dismiss Us
firstLine: Lord, dismiss us with your blessing;
verses: Lord, dismiss us with your blessing;
Fill our hearts with joy and peace;
Let us each, your love possessing,
O refresh us, O refresh us,
As we work to keep a-pace.
May the fruits of this Communion
In our hearts and lives abound:
To the truth may we be found.
So that when your love shall call us,
Let no fear of death upset us,
May we ever, may we ever,
In you loving presence stay. Amen.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lord For Tomorrow And Its Needs
firstLine: Lord, for tomorrow and its needs,
verses: Lord, for tomorrow and its needs,
Keep me, my God, from stain of sin,
Let me be slow to do my will,
Help me to mortify my flesh,
Let me be kind in word and deed,
Let me no wrong or idle word,
Set thou a seal upon my lips,
Let me in season, Lord, be grave,
Let me be faithful to thy grace, Just for today.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lord Have Mercy
firstLine: Key F: 3/4
verses: Lord have mercy, Lord have mercy
Oh-Lord have mercy on us D/S
Oh Christ have mercy on us D/S
Oh-Lord have mercy on us D/S
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lord Have Mercy 2
firstLine: Key Eb: 2/4
verses: Lord Lord have mercy Lord, Lord have mercy
Lord - Lord have mercy on us.
Christ, Christ have mercy on us
Lord - Lord have mercy on us.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lord We Pray For Golden Peace
firstLine: Lord we pray for golden peace,
verses: Lord we pray for golden peace,
May all men dwell in liberty,
Let no man live in vain,
Let no man live in vain.
Wipe away all war and strife,
Let goodness rule the hearts of men,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Loving Mother Of The Redeemer
firstLine: Loving mother of the Redeemer,
verses: gate of heaven, star of the sea,
assist your people who have fallen yet
To the wonderment of nature you bore your Creator,
yet remained a virgin after as before,
You who received Gabriel's joyful greeting,
have pity on us poor sinners.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Low In The Grave He Lay
firstLine: Low in the grave He lay,
verses: Low in the grave He lay,
Up from the grave He arose,
With a mighty triumph o'er His foes;
He arose a victor from the dark domain,
And He lives forever with His Saints to reign
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Lux Aeterna
firstLine: Lux aeterna luceat eis, Domine,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Magnificat: God Fills Me With Joy
firstLine: God fills me with joy, alleluia!
verses: God fills me with joy, alleluia!
His holy presence is my robe, alleluia!
The Lord who is my Saviour.
That God has shown me favour?
The world shall call me blest
In me is manifest God's greatness and His glory.
For those who are His friends,
And keep His laws as holy,
But by His power the great,
The kings who sit in state,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Magnificat: My Soul Is Filled With Joy
firstLine: My soul is filled with joy;
verses: My soul is filled with joy;
As I sing to God my saviour;
He has looked upon His servant;
To the people He has chosen;
I am lowly as a child;
But I know from this day forward;
That my name will be remembered;
For all men will call me blessed:
I proclaim the power of God;
He does marvels for His servants;
Though He scatters the proud hearted;
And destroys the might of princes;
To the hungry He gives food;
In His mercy He is mindful;
Of the people He has chosen;
In His love He now fulfills;
What He promised I will praise the Lord, my saviour;
Praise the Father, Son and Spirit;
As it was in the beginning;
Is now and ever shall be;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Magnificat: Soul Now Glorifies
firstLine: My soul now glorifies;
verses: The Lord who is my saviour;
That God has shown me favour
For those who are His friends;
And keep His laws as holy;
But by His power the great;
The kings who sit in state;
The Lord who is my saviour;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Magnificat: My Soul Now Glorifies The Lord My God
firstLine: My spirit sings His praise,
verses: from now all ages Will call me blessed,
For those who keep His laws,
He puts His arms in strength
and down He brings the proud,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Magnificat: My Soul Proclaims The Lord My God
firstLine: My soul proclaims the Lord my God;
verses: My soul proclaims the Lord my God;
All nations now will share my joy;
His little one He has made great
His strong right arm puts down the proud;
He fills the hungry with good things;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Canticle Of The Blessed Virgin (Magnificat)
firstLine: The Lord has done marvels for me,
verses: The Lord has done marvels for me,
My Spirit rejoices in God, my Saviour.
He looks on his servant in her nothingness;
Henceforth all ages will call me blessed.
The Almighty works marvels for me.
His mercy is from age to age,
He puts forth his arm in strength
He casts the mighty from their thrones
He fills the starving with good things,
The mercy promised to our fathers,
For Abraham and his sons forever.
Praise the Father, the Son and Holy Spirit,
Both now and forever, world without end.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Magnify And Glorify
firstLine: Magnify, glorify, extol and exalt the Lord;
verses: Magnify, glorify, extol and exalt the Lord;
Magnify, glorify His holy name for evermore;
Magnify, glorify, extol and exalt the Lord;
Exalt and glorify, magnify His name for ever more
Our feet shall stand within thy gates, O Jerusalem!
Rejoice in thy salvation, Our feet shall stand..
shall stand within thy gates, O Jerusalem!
Magnify, glorify, extol and exalt the Lord:
Magnify, glorify His holy Name forever more;
Magnify glorify, extol and exalt the Lord;
Exalt and glorify, Magnify His Name for ever more.
Extol Him that rideth upon the heavens, By His Name, JAH!
Extol Him that rideth upon the heavens, By His name, JAH!
We will rejoice in your salvation, And in your Name will we lift up our banners
And come again with singing unto Zion,
We will rejoice in your salvation,
And in Your Name will we set up our banners,
And come again with singing unto Zion,
And obtain gladness and joy Halleluia! Amen,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Man Is Lonely By Birth
firstLine: Man is lonely by birth, Man is only a pilgrim on earth,
verses: Man is lonely by birth, Man is only a pilgrim on earth,
Born to be king, time is but a temporary thing,
Only on loan while on earth.
Like the wind in the tree,
Man has been rather reckless and free,
Thrown far and wide, he longs to settle down
Beside the stream flowing through eternity.
Like the grass on the lawn,
He will pass by the way and be gone,
A lesson to learn, we walk but once there's no return,
For a song and a place in the sun,
For home up above where every day is lived in love
For rest when the journey is done.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Man Of Sorrows Wrapt In Grief
firstLine: Man of sorrows wrapt in grief,
verses: Man of sorrows wrapt in grief,
Bow thine ear to our relief;
Thou for us the path hast trod
Of the dreadful wrath of God;
Thou the cup of fire hast drained
Lamb of love! we look to thee:
By the garden, fraught with woe,
Whither thou full oft wouldst go;
By the dire and deep distress
Of that mystery fathomless Lord, our tears in mercy see:
By those lips which fain would pray
That it might but pass away;
By the heart which drank it dry,
Lest a rebel race should die
Be thy, pity, Lord, our plea:
Man of sorrows! let thy grief
bow thine ear, Slow to anger, swift to hear;
Lead us to the throne of God.
There for aye to sing to thee
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Mary Had A Baby
firstLine: Mary had a baby, yes, my Lord,
verses: Mary had a baby, yes, my Lord,
Mary had a baby, yes, my Lord;
And the angels in the heavens are singing.
What did she name him, yes, my Lord, What did she name him Lord?
What did she name him, yes, my Lord;
And the angels in the heavens are singing.
Named him King Jesus, yes, my Lord,
Named him King Jesus, yes, my Lord;
And the angels in the heavens are singing.
Where was he born, yes, my Lord,
Where was he born, yes, my Lord;
And the angels in the heavens are singing.
Born in a manger, yes, my Lord,
Born in a manger, yes, my Lord;
And the angels in the heavens are singing.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Mary Let Perpetual Succour
firstLine: Mary, let Perpetual Succour,
verses: Be the answer to our prayer;
For thy Son of all the wretched,
Let thy children feel it now.
Of our passions we are weary,
We ary of the yoke of sin;
Yet though longing to be holy,
Faint of heart, we ne'er begin.
Though we try to rise, yet ever,
For our Mother 's help we call.
Let us feel thy help in sorrow,
Mourners look for joy to thee;
Succour all, both Priests and people,
Who to thee their homage pay;
To iling men and praying women,
Help them on thy festal day.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: May Angels Guide You And Bring You Into Paradise (In Paradisum)
firstLine: May angels guide you and bring you into paradise:
verses: May angels guide you and bring you into paradise:
and may all the martyrs come forth to welcome you home;
and may they lead you into the holy city, Jerusalem.
May the angel choirs sing to welcome you,
and like Lazarus, forgotten and poor,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Most Ancient Of All Mysteries
firstLine: Most ancient of all mysteries,
verses: When heaven and earth were yet unmade,
Thou, in thy bliss and majesty,
there was no fountFrom which thy Being flowed,
There is no end which thou can'tst reach:
The work that thou didst bless;
And oh, what then must thou be like,
Still at thy throne we lie;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Mother At Your Feet Is Kneeling
firstLine: Mother at your feet is kneeling,
verses: Mother at your feet is kneeling,
One who love you, 'tis your child
Who has sighed so oft to see you.
Bless me Mother bless your child
Mother when my Jesus calls me
From this world so dark and drear
From the wily snares of Satan,
How I love Him fond and true
Tell Him I belong to you.
Plead for me when Jesus judges,
Answer for me when He asks
How I spent so many moments
May it e'er to me be giv'n
As on earth I fondly love Thee
So, to love Thee still in Heaven.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Mother Dearest Mother Fairest
firstLine: Mother dearest, Mother fairest,
verses: Help of all, who call on thee.
Help us, help, we cry to thee.
Mary, help us, help we pray.
Mary, help us, help we pray.
Help us in all care and sorrow.
Mary, help us, help we pray.
Lady, help in pain and sorrow, S
oothe those rack'd on beds of pain,
May the golden light of morrow,
Bring them health and joy again.
Lady, help the absent loved ones.
How we miss their presence here.
May the hand of Thy protection
Guide and guard them far and near.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Mother Dear O Pray For Me
firstLine: Mother dear, O pray for me!
verses: Mother dear, O pray for me!
Whilst far from heav'n and thee.
I wander in a fragile bark.
Protect they child and cheer my path
With thy sweet smile of love.
Thy love and bliss I share.
Mother dear, O pray for me!
E'er tempt thy child to wander far
When thorns beset life's devious way,
Then Mary aid thy weeping child,
Mother dear, oh pray for me,
When all seems full of cheer,
That I may all my danger see,
A Mother 's help I, then, most need,
That paints with gold the flow'ry mead
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: "Must I Go - And Empty Handed?"
firstLine: "Must I go, and empty handed?
verses: Not one day of service give Him,
Lay no trophy at His feet?
Not one soul with which to greet Him?
Not a death I shrink or falter,
For my Saviour saves me now,
Thought of that now clouds my brow.
Oh, the years of singing wasted,
Could I but recall them now,
I would give them to my Saviour,
To His will, I'd gladly bow.
Up, ye saints, arouse, be earnest;
Up and work while yet 'tis day;
Ere the night of death o'ertake you,
Strive for souls while yet you may.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My Faith Has Found A Resting Place ( I Need No Other Argument)
firstLine: My faith has found a resting place,
verses: My faith has found a resting place,
His wounds for me shall plead.
It is enough that Jesus died,
And that He died for me.
Enough for me that Jesus saves,
This ends my fear and doubt;
A sinful soul I come to Him,
My heart is leaning on the Word,
My great Physician heals the sick,
The lost He came to save;
For me His precious blood He shed,
For me His life He gave.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My God Accept My Heart This Day
firstLine: My God, accept my heart this day,
verses: My God, accept my heart this day,
That I from thee no more may stray,
Before the Cross of him who died,
And Christ be all in all.
Anoint me with thy heavenly grace,
And seal me for thine own,
That I may see thy glorious face,
Let every thought and work and word
Then life shall be thy service, Lord,
And death the gate of heaven.
All glory to the Father be,
All glory to Holy Ghost, to thee,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My God How Wonderful Thou Art
firstLine: My God, how wonderful thou art,
verses: My God, how wonderful thou art,
How dread are thine eternal years,
By prostrate spirits day and night
The sight of thee must be,
Oh, how I fear thee, living God,
And worship thee with trembling hope,
Yet I may love thee too,
O Lord, Almighty as thou art,
For thou hast stooped to ask of me
The love of my poor heart.
No earthly father loves like thee,
Bears and forbears as thou hast done,
Prostrate before thy throne to lie,
And gaze and gaze on thee.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My God I Love You
firstLine: My God, I love you though there were
verses: My God, I love you though there were
No heaven for me to win,
No hell to punish those who dare
Upon the Cross your wide embrace
Made me, dear Lord, your own;
The nails, the spear, the long disgrace
That night of fear, those hours of pain,
That death itself was borne, to gain
And shall the fear of hell below
Be all the reason heart can know
The love that asks not anything,
Love like your own love free,
Jesus I give, who are my king,
Who are my God, to you.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My God Loves Me
firstLine: My God loves me,
verses: And all the wonders I see,
Are all small signs of the love,
To Him no one else is the same,
I'm just not lost in the crowd,
Because of this I can see,
In spite of all my fault,
My God loves me, O what a mystery,
That God should share his love,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My Hope Is Built On Nothing Less
firstLine: My hope is built on nothing less,
verses: My hope is built on nothing less,
I dare not trust the sweetest frame,
But wholly lean on Jesus' name.
On Christ the Solid Rock, is sinking sand,
All other ground is sinking sand,
All other ground is sinking sand,
When darkness veils His Lovely face,
I rest on His unchanging grace;
In every high and stormy gale,
My anchor holds within the veil.
His oath, His covenant, His blood,
Support me in the whelming flood;
When all around my soul gives way,
He then is all my hope and stay.
When He shall come with trumpet sound,
Oh, may I then in Him be found;
Faultless to stand before the throne.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Easter Song (My Lord He Died For A Kingdom)
firstLine: My Lord, He died for a Kingdom,
verses: My Lord, He died for a Kingdom,
To redeem the hearts of men,
Now my people, don't you weep,
He has risen from His sleep,
Sing alleluia, the Lord is risen,
My Lord came forth like the morning
with the splendour of the sun,
from the darkness of the tomb,
Now the seasons and the sea
that had waited late and long,
Now we all with one accord
live and love the Risen Lord,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ezigbo Nwaturu
firstLine: Key C: 2/4
verses: Ezigbo nwaturu, achicha nke ndi oma Onye si n'igwe bia anyi esekpuoro gi.
Eze nke ndi muo ma, eze nke si n'igwe, anyi bu ndi no n'uwa Nacho gi O Chukwu.
Na mbosi baptisma, k'imere anyi umugi Chukwu nwa nke di ebube bata n'obi an any
K'inoriri n'uwa, igwara umu Isreal A bum achicha ndu, nke si n'igwe bia.
Noyere anyi n'uwa, ezigbo dinwenu k'anyi wee hu gi n'anya Naleze nke eligwe.
I bu nna anyi ezie, onye nche aturu Onye nche nke umu ogbenye chukwu di afo -oma
I bu Eze n'k'igwe, Ibu Eze ndi oma Anyi bu ndi no n'uwa n'achu gi o Chukwu.
Umu chukwu nile k'anyi n'urianu Anyi ncha bu umu nna, na kristi Dinwenu.
A bum, abum uzo, abum, abum ndu Onye obula riri achicha g'enwe ndu okpu.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My Prayer Rise Like Incense(psalm 140)
firstLine: ANTHIPHON:
verses: my hands like an evening offering.
I call to you, Lord, help me now,
listen to me when I call to you.
Lord I say to you, hear me,
listen to me, Lord, I am invoking you.
Lord place a guard over my mouth,
a sentry at the door of my lips,
keep my heart from inclining to evil.
Save me from all wicked deeds and desires,
May the righteous reprove in kindness,
but I will not let the wicked anoint my hand.
I will always resist their evil deeds.
Lord God, my eyes are turned to you,
Guard me from the traps they have set for me.
From the snare of those who do evil.
Glory to the Father and to the Son and to the Holy Spirit.
As it was in the beginning,
is now, and shall be for ever.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My Soul Give Thanks To The Lord (Psalm 102)
firstLine: My soul, give thanks to the Lord,
verses: My soul, give thanks to the Lord,
My soul, give thanks to the Lord,
All my being bless His holy name.
My soul give thanks to the Lord,
And never forget all his blessings.
It is he who forgives all your guilt,
Who heals every one of your ills,
Who redeems your life from the grave,
Who crowns you with love and compassion.
Who fills your life with good things,
Renewing your youth like an eagle's.
The Lord does deeds of justice,
Give judgement for all who are oppressed.
He made known His ways to Moses
The Lord is compassion and love,
Slow to anger and rich in mercy.
His wrath will come to an end,
He will not be angry for ever.
He does not treat us according to our sins
Nor repay us according to our faults.
For as the heavens are high above the earth,
So strong is His love for those who fear him.
As far as the east is from the west,
So far does He remove our sins.
As a father has compassion on his sons,
The Lord has pity on those who fear Him.
For He knows of what we are made,
He remembers that we are dust.
As for man, his days are like grass,
He flowers like the flower of the field;
The wind blows and he is gone,
And his place never sees him again.
Give praise to the Father Almighty,
To His Son, Jesus Christ, the Lord,
To the Spirit who dwells in our hearts,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My Soul Is Longing For Your Peace (Psalm 131)
firstLine: My soul is longing for your peace,
verses: My soul is longing for your peace,
Lord, you know that my heart is not proud,
And my eyes are not lifted from the earth.
Lofty thoughts have never filled my mind,
Far beyond my sight all ambitious deeds.
In you peace I have maintained my soul,
I have kept my heart in your quiet peace.
As a child rests on his mother's knee,
So I place my soul in your loving care.
Israel, put all your hope in God,
Place your trust in him, now and evermore.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: My Times Are In Thy Hand
firstLine: My times are in Thy Hands,
verses: My times are in Thy Hands,
My God, I wish them there;
My life, my friends, my soul I leave
My times are in Thy Hand,
Pleasing or painful, dark or bright,
As best may seem to Thee.
My times are in Thy Hand,
Why should I doubt or fear?
A Father's Hand will never cause
My times are in Thy Hand,
The Hand my cruel sins hand pierced
Is now my guard and guide.
My times are in Thy Hand,
And after death at Thy right Hand
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: N'oriri Di Aso Eligwe N'uwa N'eme Korita
firstLine: Key Ab/G: 2/4
verses: N'oriri di aso, eligwe n'uwa n'eme korita, Chukwu na madu aburu otu ndi no n'eligwe na ndi no n'uwa emekoria bie oma buru otu n'ime muo (oru obube)
oru itu n'anya, ya n'ihe omimi
Chineke bu amamihe n'onwe ya ihu n'anya ya ebuka kwa, umu madu Kelenu chukwu, doo nna ekele diri gi Chukwu.
Bia naranum rie bia nara num nuo (Jesu gwara anyi nile) onye riri ahum nuobara m, g'ebiri n'ime m munwa biri n'ime ya
A-bu m uzo buru ndu na eziokwu (Jesu gwara anyi nile) Onye sobe mu ga enwe ndu okpu ngwanu k'anyi sobe ya.
Allelula, Alleluia, were nu onu too chukwu.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Nearer My God To Thee
firstLine: Nearer, my God, to Thee,
verses: Nearer to Thee, Even though it be a cross
Still all my song shall be,
Nearer, my God, to Thee, Nearer to Thee.
Thou that hast bled for me,
Still through my tears I'll see
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: New Praises Be Given
firstLine: New praises be given to Christ newly crowned,
verses: New praises be given to Christ newly crowned,
Who back to his heaven a new way hath found,
God's blessedness sharing before us he goes
What mansions preparing, what endless repose.
His glory still praising on thrice holy ground,
The apostles stood gazing, his mother around,
They watched while their Master ascended above.
Eternity knows him, your conquering head
Those high habitations he leaves not again,
Till judging nations, on earth he shall reign.
And cry, looking thither, you portals let down
For him who rides hither, in peace and renown.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Now Thank We All Our God
firstLine: Now thank we all our God,
verses: Now thank we all our God,
With heart and hands and voices,
Has blessed us on our way,
God Through all our life be near us,
And blessed peace to cheer us,
And keep us in his grace,
And free us from all ill,
In this world and the next.
All praise and thanks to God
The Son, and him who reigns
For thus it was, is now,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Now That The Day-star Glimmers
firstLine: Now that the day-star glimmers bright,
verses: Now that the day-star glimmers bright,
May guide us on our way.
No sinful word, nor deed of wrong,
But simple truth be on our tongue,
And in our hearts be love.
And, while the hours in order flow,
Our gates, beleaguer'd by the foe: -
And grant that to thine honour,
Lord, Our daily toil may tend;
That we begin it at thy word,
And, lest the flesh in its excess
Should loard it o'er the soul,
To God the Father glory be,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Now The Day Is Over
firstLine: "Now the day is over,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O All You Works Of The Lord Bless The Lord
firstLine: "O all you works of the Lord, bless the Lord:
verses: "O all you works of the Lord, bless the Lord:
To him be highest glory and praise forever.
And you, angels of the Lord, bless the Lord:
And you, heavens of the Lord, bless the Lord:
And you, sun and moon, bless the Lord:
And you, stars of heavens, bless the Lord:
And you, showers and rain, bless the Lord:
And you, all breezes and winds, bless the Lord:
And you, cold and heat, bless the Lord:
And you, night-time and day, bless the Lord:
And you, mountains and hills, bless the Lord:
And you, all plants of the earth, bless the Lord:
And you, rivers and seas, bless the Lord:
And you, creatures of the sea, bless the Lord:
And you, every bird in the sky, bless the Lord:
And you, wild beasts and tame, bless the Lord:
And you, children of men, bless the Lord:
And you, Priests of the Lord, bless the Lord:
And you, servants of the Lord, bless the Lord:
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: (4/4) O N'e Mere M Nma
firstLine: O n'e mere m nma, O n'emere m nma nma kedu ihem g'e nye ezi nna mo- onye obi oma A gam'aja ya nma, a ga m aturu ya nma nma,
verses: O n'e mere m nma, O n'emere m nma nma kedu ihem g'e nye ezi nna mo- onye obi oma A gam'aja ya nma, a ga m aturu ya nma nma,
Chin'emere m nma n'eziokwu (Chi n'emere mo) Imana Chineke nna bu ya bu chi n'emere mihe oma.
Obu chi- I n'aju m'onye nyere mu Ndu mo-ndu nke idi onye nyere gi ee.
I naju m'onye nyeremu ahu ike ahu ike nk'inwere onye nyere gi ee oh.
O bu chi- I n'eje eje i n'a bata
O bu onye n'eche be gi n'ihem berede
n' onya nile nke ekwensu n'agba, O bu onye n'echebe gi n'ihe mbere de-oh.
O bu chi-obu onye kere anyanwu na onwa Ma kee kpakpa ndo nile no n'eligwe, onyere madu echiche na ihe nile n'uju, nwannem Sorom kele Chineke chi mo n'emerem ihe oma.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Blessed Saint Joseph How Great Was Thy Worth
firstLine: O blessed Saint Joseph how great was thy worth,
verses: O blessed Saint Joseph how great was thy worth,
The one chosen shadow of God up on earth
Called Father of Jesus ah, then wilt thou be,
Sweet Spouse of Our Lady! a father to me?
For thou to the pilgrim art father and guide,
And Jesus and Mary felt safe by thy side;
Ah blessed Saint Joseph how safe should I be,
When the treasures of God were unsheltered on earth,
Safe keeping was found for them both in thy worth;
O Father of Jesus, be father to me,
Sweet Spouse of Our Lady, and I will love thee.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Bread Of Heaven
firstLine: "O bread of heaven, beneath this veil
verses: "O bread of heaven, beneath this veil
Thou dost my very God conceal,
hail I love thee and adoring kneel:
Each loving soul by thee is fed
With thy own self in form of bread.
I live; no 'tis not I that live.
God gives me life, God lives in me.
He feeds my soul, he guides my ways,
And every grief with joy repays.
O bond of love, that dost unite
The servant to his loving Lord;
Could I dare live, and not requiteSuch love -
Then death were meet reward I cannot live unless to prove
Some love for such unmeasured love.
To gaze on thee with changeless love;
Yes, thus, I hope thus shall it be,
For how can He deny me heaven
Who here on earth Himself hath given?
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Brothers Of Christ Jesus Come
firstLine: O brothers of Christ Jesus
verses: To offer your presents to God*
Give us this day our daily bread,
We ask from Thee through Jesus Christ.
We give to Thee today on earth.
We give to Thee today on earth.
Sins of the world, had made us fall
The wicked ones, are against us
O Father, take us from their hands,
And make us strong, to overcome them.
O Holy Spirit, make our hearts pure,
To be good followers of Christ,
Kindle in us, thy blessed light,
Spirit of God, remain with us.
Praises to God, in Heaven above.
Praises to Christ, His only Son,
All these we ask, from Thee O God.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Come All Ye Faithful
firstLine: O come all ye faithful,
verses: O come ye, O come ye to Bethlehem;
O come, let us adore Him,
O come, let us adore Him,
O come, let us adore Him,
Sing, all ye citizens of heaven above,
Jesus, to you be all glory given;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Come And Mourn With Me Awhile
firstLine: O come and mourn with me a while,
verses: O come and mourn with me a while,
See Mary calls us to her side;
O come and let us mourn with her,
Have we no tears to shed for Him;
While soldiers scoff and Jews deride?
Ah! look how patiently He hangs;
How fast His feet and hands are nailed;
His blessed tongue with thirst is tied;
His failing eyes are blind with blood;
Seven times He spoke, seven words of love,
And all three hours His silence cried
For Mercy on the souls of men;
O break, O break hard heart of mine;
Thy weak self-love and guilty pride
His Pilate and His Judas were;
A broken heart, a fount of tears,
Ask and they will not be denied;
A broken heart love's cradle is;
O love of God! O sin of man!
In this dread act your strength is tried
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Come Thou Wisdom Whose Decree
firstLine: O come, thou wisdom whose decree,
verses: O come, thou wisdom whose decree,
The way of prudence here below,
And life hereafter deign to show.
Is born to save thee, Israel.
Who once by Israel's host adored,
O Rod of Jesse, mystic bough,
From Satan's cruel snares do thou,
From death's grim dungeon, we implore
And hell's abyss thine own restore.
O come, thou Key of David's store,
Unlock the heavenly gates once more;
Safe journey to thy courts bestow,
And shut the way that leads below.
O come, thou Daystar seen on high,
With healing for our hearts draw nigh;
Do thou the mists of night dispel,
O come, of Gentile hearts the King,
A world that needs thee ransoming,
And save thy servants, who confess
That doth in exile homeless mourn,
Until her Saviour Christ be born.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Come To The Throne Of Grace
firstLine: O come to the throne of grace,
verses: O come to the throne of grace,
O come to the heart most pure;
To Mary our hope of life,
O pray for us, help us to-day
Thou hope of the human race.
The present, its pain and joy
The future, all it may be.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Emmanuel
firstLine: O come we are waiting, (3x)Emmanuel.
verses: On a land of deep shadow,
A sign God will give us,
And He shall be called Emmanuel.
The peace He will bring us,
With the poor and the meek,
With the breath from his lips
With the lamb they shall lie,
Shall be led by a boy,
On the nest to the viper,
For the earth shall be filled
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Esca Viatorum
firstLine: O esca viatorum,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Food Of Angels
firstLine: O food of angels, now food for men
verses: O food of angels, now food for men
Communion, Christ Himself, not mere a symbol
And the lowly, the humble, eat the Lord.
And the lowly; the humble eat the Lord
The poor, the servant; the humble eat the Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Give Thanks To The Lord (Psalm 136)
firstLine: "O give thanks to the Lord for He is good,
verses: "O give thanks to the Lord for He is good,
Great is His love, love without end,
Give thanks to the God of gods,
Great is His love, love without end,
Give thanks to the Lord of lords.
Who alone has wrought marvellous works,
Great is His love, love without end,
Whose wisdom it was made the skies,
Great is His love, love without end,
Who spread the earth on the seas.
It was He who made the great lights,
Great is His love, love without end
The sun to rule in the day,
Great is His love, love without end,
The moon and stars in the night.
The first-born of the Egyptians he smote,
Great is His love, love without end,
Brought Israel out from their midst,
Great is His love, love without end,
Arm outstretched, with power in His hand.
He divided the Red Sea in two,
Great is His love, love without end,
Made Israel pass through the midst,
Great is His love, love without end,
Flung Pharaoh and his force in the sea.
Through the desert His people He led,
Great is His love, love without end,
Nations in their greatness He struck,
Great is His love, love without end,
King in their splendour He slew.
He let Israel inherit their land,
Great is His love, love without end,
On His servant their land He bestowed,
Great is His love, love without end,
He remembered us in our distress.
And he snatched us away from our foes,
Great is His love, love without end,
He gives food to all living things,
Great is His is love, love without end,
To the God of heaven give thanks.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O God Of Love
firstLine: O God of love, O King of Peace,
verses: O God of love, O King of Peace,
Make wars throughout the world cease,
The plans of sinful men restrain,
Give peace O Lord, give peace again.
Remember Lord, your works of old,
The wonders that our fathers told,
Give peace O Lord, give peace again.
Where saints and angels dwell above,
All hearts are one in holy love,
May we to that high state attain,
Give peace O Lord, give peace again.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O God Our Help In Ages Past
firstLine: O God, our help, in ages past,
verses: O God, our help, in ages past,
Our hope for years to come,
Our shelter from the stormy blast,
Beneath the shadow of thy throne,
Before the hills in order stood,
A thousand ages in thy sight,
Short as the watch that ends the night,
They fly forgotten as a dream
O God, our help in ages past,
Our hope for years to come,
Be thou our guard while troubles last.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O God Thy People Gather
firstLine: O God, Thy people gather,
verses: To praise Thy name, O Lord,
Thou art our loving Father, Thou art our holiest Lord,
But we have sinned against Thee, By thought and deed and word,
Before the court of heaven We stand and humbly pray,
Our sins may be forgiven Our faults be washed away.
To turn and make us live,
That so we may adore thee
And may the prayers and voices
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O God You Are My Strength (Psalm 43:3-5)
firstLine: O God, you are my strength, why have you rejected me?
verses: O God, you are my strength, why have you rejected me?
Send forth your light and your truth, They will guide me to your holy hill.
I will go to the altar of God, and I will glorify him in song.
Why are you depressed, my soul?
Hope in God, trust in his goodness.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Godhead Hid
firstLine: " Godhead hid, devoutly I adore Thee,
verses: " Godhead hid, devoutly I adore Thee,
Who truly art within the forms before me;
To Thee my heart I bow with bended knee,
As failing quite in contemplating Thee.
Sight, touch, and taste in Thee are each deceived;
The ear alone most safely is believed;
I believe all the Son of God has spoken,
Than Truth's own word there is truer token.
God only on the Cross lay hid from view;
But here lies hid at once the Manhood too;
And I, in both professing my belief,
Make the same prayer as the repentant thief.
Thy wounds as Thomas saw, I do not see;
Yet Thee confess my Lord and God to be;
Make me believe Thee ever more and more;
In Thee my hope, in Thee my love to store.
O Thou Memorial of our Lord's own dying!
O Bread that living art and vivifying!
Make ever Thou my soul on Thee to live;
Ever a taste of Heavenly sweetness give.
O loving Pelican! O Jesus, Lord!
Unclean I am, but cleanse me in Thy Blood;
Of which a open drop, for sinners spilt,
Is ransom for a world's entire guilt.
Jesus! whom for the present veil'd I see,
What I so thirst for, Oh, vouchsafe to me:
That I may see Thy countenance unfolding,
And may be blest Thy glory in beholding.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Happy Day
firstLine: O happy day, that fixed my choice,
verses: O happy day, that fixed my choice,
On Thee, my Saviour and my God!
We ll may this glowing heart rejoice,
And tell its raptures all abroad.
When Jesus washed my sins away!
He taught me how to watch and pray
When Jesus washed my sins away!
I am my Lord's and He is mine;
He drew me, and I followed on,
Charmed to confess the voice Divine.
Fixed on this blissful centre, rest;
Nor ever from thy Lord depart,
With Him of every good possessed.
High Heaven, that heard the solemn vow
That vow renewed shall daily hear,
Till in life's latest hour I bow,
And bless in death a bond so dear.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Holy Lord
firstLine: O holy Lord, by all adored,
verses: O holy Lord, by all adored,
To you this day your children pray,
Accept O King, the gifts we bring,
And grant us Lord, your blessing.
To God on high be thanks and praise,
Who deigns our bonds to sever,
His care shall guide us all our days,
And harm shall reach us never,
On Him we rest with faith assured:
Of all that live, He is the Lord,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Holy Night
firstLine: O holy night! the stars are brightly shining,
verses: O holy night! the stars are brightly shining,
It is the night of the dear Saviour's birth!
Long lay the world in sin and error pining,
Till He appeared and the soul felt its worth.
A thrill of hope, the weary world rejoices,
For yonder breaks a new and glorious morn!
O night when Christ was born!
O night divine, O night, O night divine.
Led by the light of faith serenely beaming,
With glowing hearts by His cradle we stand.
So led by light of a star sweetly gleaming,
Here came the wisemen from Orient land.
The King of kings lay thus in lowly manger,
In all our trials born to be our Friend;
He knows our need, to our weakness is no stranger.
Behold your King, before Him lowly bend!/2ce
Truly He taught us to love one another;
His law is love and gospel is peace:
Chains shall He break, for the slave is our brother,
And in His name all oppression shall cease.
Sweet hymns of joy in grateful chorus raise we,
Christ is the Lord! O, praise His name forever!
His power and glory evermore proclaim!/2ce
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Jesus Christ Remember
firstLine: O Jesus Christ, remember, when you will come again,
verses: O Jesus Christ, remember, when you will come again,
Upon the clouds of heaven, with all your shining train;
When every eye shall see you in deity reveal'd,
Who now upon this altar in silence are concealed.
Remember then, O Saviour, I supplicate of you,
That here I bow'd before you upon my bended knee,
That here I owned your presence, and did not you deny,
And glorified your greatness though hid from human eye.
Accept, divine Redeemer, the homage of my praise;
O be the light and honour and glory of my days,
O be my consolation when death is drawing nigh,
O be my only treasure through all eternity.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Jesus We Adore Thee
firstLine: "O Jesus, we adore thee,
verses: In forms of bread and wine
And with the saints and angels,
Come, live in us, we pray,
That all our thoughts and actions,
Come, eat this bread from heaven,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O King Of Kings
firstLine: O King of Kings,
verses: In splendour of glory throned on high,
Art known from age to age.
Now and in time to be,
The gift of peace to shower,
On those who trust in Thee.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O King Of Might And Splendour
firstLine: "O King of might and splendour,
verses: As now with love most tender
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: What Did He Do
firstLine: "O listen to a wondrous story,
verses: Yes, one come down from heaven's glory,
Who saved us from eternal loss?
Who but God's Son upon the cross,
He died for us, Where is he now?
Believe it thee in heaven interceding.
No angel could His place have taken
Highest of the high though he,
The loved One on the cross forsaken
Was one of the Godhead Three.
Will you surrender to this Saviour?
You too shall come to know his favour,
He will save you, save you now.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Little Town Of Bethlehem
firstLine: O little town of Bethlehem,
verses: How still we see thee lie!
Above thy deep and dreamless sleep
Yet in thy dark streets shineth
The hopes and fears of all the years,
For Christ is born of Mary;
While mortals sleep, the angels keep
And praises sing to God the King,
And peace to men on earth.
So God imparts to human hearts
No ear may hear His coming,
But in this world of sin;
Where meek souls will receive Him, still
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Lord I Am Not Worthy
firstLine: "O Lord, I am not worthy,
verses: That Thou shouldst come to me.
But speak the word of comfort,
No more by sin to grieve Thee,
O Lord, Thou art all holy,
But when Thou soon wilt enter,
Then heal me, be my shelter,
O Lord, how can I thank Thee
For such a gift as this?
I praise Thee, I extol Thee,
I love Thee, O my Sire,
Till once in joy and glory,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Lord My God
firstLine: 0 Lord, my God,
verses: Thy power throughout the universe displayed.
When through the woods and forest glades
I wander And hear the birds sing sweetly in the trees.
When I look down from lofty mountain grandeur,
And hear the brook and feel the breeze.
And when I think that God,
I scarce can take it in.
That on the Cross, my burden gladly bearing,
He bled and died to take away my sin.
When Christ shall come with shouts of acclamation
what joy shall fill my heart!
Then I shall bow in humble adoration,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Maid Conceived Without A Stain
firstLine: O Maid conceived without a stain,
verses: O Maid conceived without a stain,
Come thou within our hearts to reign
O make thy children chaste and mild,
And turn their hearts to thee.
O Mother of all mothers best
In thee the weary find their rest,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Paradise O Paradise!
firstLine: O Paradise!
verses: Who doth not crave for rest?
Who would not seek the happy land,
Where they that loved are blest?
Bright death that is the welcome dawn
I long to be where Jesus is,
To feel, to see Him near.
I want to sin no more;
I want to be as pure on earth,
Patience: I almost think I hear
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Perfect Love
firstLine: O perfect Love, all human thought
verses: O perfect Love, all human thought
stranscending, Lowly we kneel in prayer before thy throne,
That theirs may be the love
Whom thou for evermore dost join in one.
Of tender charity and steadfast faith,
With child-like trust that fears nor pain nor death.
Grant them joy which brightens earthly sorrow,
Grant them the peace which claims all earthly strife;
And to life's day the glorious unknown morrow
That dawns upon eternal love and life.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Purest Of Creatures
firstLine: O purest of creatures,
verses: Dark night hath come down on us,
Mother , and weLook out for thy shining,
All her eyes are on thee,
, And He blest thy clear shining,
And God found a home where
He was won by thy shining
And he shone in thy shining
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Queen Of The Holy Rosary
firstLine: O Queen of the Holy Rosary!
verses: O Queen of the Holy Rosary!
Oh! bless us as we pray,
Buds white and red and gold.
O Queen of the Holy Rosary!
Thy soul was His fair garden,
And lay them round thy footstool,
God's Son was fain to be
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Redeemer Hear This Anthem (O Redemptor Sume Carmen)
firstLine: O Redeemer, hear this anthem
verses: As we sing with one accord.
That a fruitful tree once bore
When we bear it in procession
Consecrate, O King of heaven, Olive oil, a living sign;
Let it thwart the demons' power,
Lend to it your strength divine.
Are renewed as they are sealed,
And our glory, once so wounded,
Now at last through grace is healed.
When the sacred font has taken
All the stains of sin away,
Send your gifts, O Lord, we pray.
Lord, who came from God the
Father In the Virgin's womb to rest,
Grant us light, and let not perish
Those who share this Chrism blest.
May this day be always festive
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Redemptor Sumen Carmen
firstLine: Redemptor, sume carmen
verses: Hoc olivum, signum vivum, lura contra daemonum.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Sacrament Most Holy
firstLine: "O Sacrament most holy,
verses: Now come, all you who labour
Come, eat this bread from heaven;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Sacred Heart
firstLine: O Sacred heart,
verses: Our home lies deep in thee;
On earth thou art an exile's rest,
In heaven the glory of the blest,
New life to sinners, they bestow,
May our Lord's sons in truth e'er stand,
With faith's bright banner still in hand,
Our trust is all in thee;
For though earth's night be dark and drear,
Thou breathest rest where thou art near,
When shades of death shall fall,
And save us from the tempter's snare,
Where we may ever rest near thee,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Salutaris Hostia
firstLine: O Salutaris hostia,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Sanctissima O Piissima.
firstLine: O sanctissima, O piissima.
verses: Quid quid optamus, per te speramus
In te speramus, ad te clamamus,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O What A Wonderful Wonderful Day
firstLine: O what a wonderful, wonderful day,
verses: O what a wonderful, wonderful day,
After I'd wandered in darkness away,
O what a tender, compassionate friend
He met the need of my heart;
Shadows dispelling, with joy I am telling,
He made all the darkness depart!
Heaven came down and glory filled my soul,
When at the cross my Saviour made me whole;
And my night was turned to day,
Heaven came down and glory filled my soul.
Born of the Spirit with life from above,
O what a standing is mine,
And the transaction so quickly was made,
When as a sinner I came,
Took of the offer of grace He did proffer,
He saved me, oh praise His dear name!
Now I've a hope that will surely endure
I have a future in heaven for sure,
And it's because of thar wonderful day,
When at the cross I believed.
From His precious hand I received!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Of How Much More Value
firstLine: Of how much more value are you than these!
verses: Of how much more value are you than these!
Of how much more value are you than these!
Look at the sparrow: he neither sows nor reaps;
he has no barn, no storehouse,
Look at the lily: she neither toils nor spins;
yet not even Solomon in all his glory
was arrayed like one of them.
Look at the green grass: see how it
So, don't worry, don't be anxious, saying,
"What shall we eat?" or "What shall we drink?
"Your Father knows you need these things.
So, give all, and be empty,
then be filled with all good things.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Oh Lord My God When I In Awesome Wonder
firstLine: Oh Lord my God
verses: And when I think that God,
I scarce can take it in;
What joy shall fill my heart.
My God, how great Thou art.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Oh Sing Praises
firstLine: Oh! sing praise, Oh! sing praises,
verses: Oh! sing praise, Oh! sing praises,
O sing praises to God most high.
Oh! sing praises, Oh! sing praises,
Sing praises, sing praises, sing praises,
For His good He made us
For He is love, He loves us,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Oh The Love Of My Lord
firstLine: Oh, the love of my Lord is the essence
verses: Oh, the love of my Lord is the essence
of all that I love here on earth.
All the beauty I see he has given to me
and his giving is gentle as silence.
Every day, every hour, every moment
have been blessed by the strength of his love,
At the turn of each tide, he is there at my side,
and his touch is as gentle as silence.
There've been times when I've turned from his presence,
And I've walked other paths, other ways.
But I've called on his name in the dark of my shame,
And his mercy was gentle as silence.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Old Rugged Cross
firstLine: On a hill far away, stood an old rugged Cross,
verses: On a hill far away, stood an old rugged Cross,
The emblem of suff'ring and shame;
And I love that old Cross
For a world of lost sinners was slain.
So I'll cherish the old rugged Cross,
Till my trophies at last I lay down;
I will cling to the old rugged Cross,
And exchange it someday for a crown.
Has a wondrous attraction for me;
For the dear Lamb of God,
To bear it to dark Calvary.
Its shame and reproach gladly bear;
Where His glory forever I'll share.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: O Yes In The Presence Of The Lord
firstLine: O yes, in the presence of the Lord,
verses: O yes, in the presence of the Lord,
I will bring my gifts, In
thanksgiving and love There is joy in my
heart. It is flowing like a river I will
Please accept this gift we offer,
Take our bread upon Your altar
And our wine in the chalice,
With thankful hearts and joyful songs,
We return what You have given,
To You Father, now we offer,
With the host and with the chalice,
All we have and all our being
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Future Lies Before Me
firstLine: Oh! I often sit and ponder,
verses: Oh! I often sit and ponder,
When the sun is sinking low,
Where shall yonder future find me
Does but God in Heaven know,
Shall I be among the living,
Shall I mingle with the free,
Saviour, keep my heart with Thee.
Oh! the future lies before me,
And I know not where I'll be,
But where'er my path be leading,
Saviour, keep my heart with Thee.
Shall I be at work with Jesus,
Whilst He Leads me by the hand,
And to those around be saying,
Come and join this happy band
Come for all things now are ready
Oh where'er my path be leading,
Saviour, keep my heart with Thee.
But perhaps my work for Jesus,
Soon in future may be done,
And my crown in heaven won,
Chanting nile to Him who bought me,
With His Blood shed on the Tree.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: On A Hill Far Away Stood The Old Rugged Cross
firstLine: On a hill far away stood the old rugged cross
verses: On a hill far away stood the old rugged cross
The emblem of suff'ring and shame;
And I love that old cross Where the dearest and blest
For a world of lost sinners was slain.
So I'll cherish the old rugged cross
Till my trophies at last I lay down;
I will cling to the old rugged cross
And exchange it some day for a crown.
Has a wondrous attraction for me;
For the dear Lamb of God left His glory above,
To bear it to dark Calvary.
In the old rugged cross, stained with blood so divine,
To the old rugged cross I will ever be true,
Its shame and reproach gladly bear,
Then He'll call me some day to my home faraway,
Where His glory forever I'll share.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: On Christ The Solid Rock
firstLine: My hope is built on nothing less
verses: My hope is built on nothing less
I dare not trust the sweetest frame,
But wholly trust in Jesus' name.
On Christ the solid rock I stand,
All other ground is sinking sand;
All other ground is sinking sand.
When darkness seems to hide His face,
I rest on His unchanging grace.
In every high and stormy gale,
My anchor holds within the veil.
His oath, His covenant, His blood,
Support me in the whelming flood.
When all around my soul gives way,
He then is all my hope and stay.
When He shall come with trumpet sound,
Oh may I then in Him be found.
Faultless to stand before the throne.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: On This Day O Beautiful Mother
firstLine: On the day, O beautiful Mother ,
verses: On the day, O beautiful Mother ,
On this day we give Thee our love.
Near they, Madonna, fondly we hover,
Trusting thy gentle care to prove.
On this day we ask to share,
Aid us ere our feet astray
Queen of angels, deign to hear
Young hearts gain, O Virgin pure,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Once In Royal David's City
firstLine: Once in royal David's city,
verses: Where a mother laid her Baby,
In a manger for His bed.
He came down to earth from heaven,
Who is God and Lord of all;
And His shelter was a stable,
And His cradle was a stall,
With the poor and mean and lowly,
Lived on earth, our Saviour holy.
And our eyes at last shall see Him,
For that Child so dear and gentle,
Is our Lord in Heaven above;
And He leads His children on,
To the place where He is gone.
Not in that poor lowly stable,
We shall see Him but in heaven,
Set at God's right hand on high.
When like star His children crowned,
All in white shall wait around.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Once More My Soul
firstLine: "Once more, my soul, thy Saviour through thy word.
verses: "Once more, my soul, thy Saviour through thy word.
And now, O Lord, I must,
I must decide: shall I accept of thee?
I will, God helping me I will,
Thy precious blood was shed to purchase me.
By grace I will thy mercy now receive.
Thy love my heart hath won,
I will, I will, believe and trust in thee alone.
Thou knowest Lord how very weak I am,
And now I fear to stray for strength to serve,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: One Thought Only Have We
firstLine: One thought only have we
verses: Though we gain the whole world,
Keep and guard and guide me,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx
