title: Open Your Hearts
firstLine: Open up your hearts and let the Lord come in,
verses: Open up your hearts and let the Lord come in,
Open up your hearts and let the Lord come in,
Open up your hearts and let the Lord come in,
God is our Father so come on in,
God is our Father so come on in,
God is our Father so come on in,
Christ is our brother so take him in,
Christ is our brother so take him in,
Christ is our brother so take him in,
Sing his praises, come on in,
Sing his praises, come on in,
Sing his praises, come on in,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Our Father Who Art In Heaven
firstLine: Our Father, who art in Heaven,
verses: Our Father, who art in Heaven,
As in heaven, so on the earth,
Give us this day our daily bread,
And forgive us, all our trespasses,
As we forgive those who trespass against us,
And lead us not into temptation,
But deliver us from all that is evil,
For ever and for ever and ever
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Our Lord Is Risen
firstLine: Our Lord is risen from the dead,
verses: Our Lord is risen from the dead,
Our Jesus is gone up on high,
The powers of hell are captive led,
Dragged to the portals of the sky.
Who is the King of glory ?
The Lord that all his foes overcame,
The world, sin, death and hell overthrew,
And Jesus is the conqueror's name,
And Jesus is the conqueror's name.
And angels chant the solemn lay,
Lift up your heads ye heavenly gates,
Loose all your hearts in mass light,
And wide unfold the ethereal scene,
He claims these mansions as his right,
Receive the King of glory in.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Pange Lingua Gloriosi
firstLine: Pange lingua gloriosi
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Panis Angelicus (O Food Of Angels)
firstLine: Panis Angelicus fit panis hominum;
verses: Sic nos tu visita sicut te colimus:
Per tuas semitas duc nos quo tendimus
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Parce Domine
firstLine: Parce Domine, parce populo tuo;
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Pass Me Not O Gentle Saviour
firstLine: "Pass me not, O gentle Saviour;
verses: While on others Thou art calling,
Saviour, Saviour, hear my humble cry;
And while on others Thou art calling,
Let me at thy throne of mercy
Thou the Spring of all my comfort,
Whom have I on earth beside Thee?
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Peace Perfect Peace
firstLine: Peace, perfect peace!
verses: In this dark world of sin?
The blood of Jesus whispers peace within.
To do the will of Jesus this is rest.
On Jesus' bossom naught but calm is found.
In Jesus' keeping we are safe, and they.
Jesus we know, and He is on the throne.
Jesus has vanquished death and all its powers.
And Jesus calls us up to heaven's perfect peace.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Pilate Said I've Found No Fault In Him
firstLine: Pilate said I've found no fault in Him.
verses: Pilate said I've found no fault in Him.
The people said we found a fault in Him
But He went and journeyed to the cross.
Praise the Lord for Jesus died for you, died for me.
Praise the Lord for God so loved the world
Praise the Lord He gave His only Son
Praise the Lord to carry sins of men
Praise the Lord for Jesus died for you, died for me.
It is finished said saviour on the cross,
It is finished the half on heaven melt,
It is finished for Jesus died for you,
Praise the Lord for Jesus died for you, died for you.
Jesus hung between heaven and earth,
Jesus bled and died upon the cross,
Just to save the soul of men from death,
Praise the Lord for Jesus died for you, died for me.
He has promised, to give eternal life,
Only to those who believe in His name,
If you believe, you'll have eternal life,
Praise the Lord for Jesus died for you, died for me.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise Be To You God Of All Creation
firstLine: Praise be to You, God of all creation
verses: Praise be to You, God of all creation
For giving us this Bread we offer You today;
Fruit of the vine and the work of human hands,
It will become the bread of life
Man of faith, man of hope
Sent to heal and sent to serve,
Man of faith, man of hope,
Sent to bring Your love to man,
And to love, love, love and serve,
Praise be to You, God of all creation,
Fruit of the vine and the work of human hands
It will become the drink of life,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise God Of Abraham
firstLine: Praise God of Abraham
verses: The heavenly King who with love abideth in eternity,
My hymn O Lord is high,
I will always exalt Thy Holy Name.
Praise God of Abraham, giver of all blessings
Who to seek joy at His right hand,
Hath exalted man: I loath all worldly cares,
That may draw me away from thee:
Will be my key steps to God.
O God who lives on high,
Saying holy, holy, holy, almighty King:
Thou who sitteth on the throne,
My nile of praise is great
All the heavenly powers glorify and praise thee,
The Father, Son and Holy Spirit for ever more:
I will thank my Lord and God:
Are thine now and ever more. Amen.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise Him In The Morning
firstLine: Praise Him, praise Him
verses: Love Him, love Him, Love Him in the morning
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise My Soul The King Of Heaven
firstLine: Praise my soul the King of Heaven:
verses: Praise my soul the King of Heaven:
To His feet thy tribute bring;
Who like thee His praise shall sing?
Praise Him for His grace and favour
Praise Him, still the same as ever,
Slow to chide, and swift to bless:
Praise Him! Praise Him! Praise Him!
Praise Him! Glorious in His faithfulness!
Father- like He tends and spares us, We ll our feeble frame He knows;
In His hands He gently bears us, Rescues us from all our foes:
Praise Him, Praise Him, Praise Him, Praise Him, Widely as His mercy flows.
Angel, help us to adore Him, Ye behold Him face to face
Sun and moon, bow down before Him
Dwellers all in time and space:
Praise Him! Praise Him! Praise Him!
Praise with us the God of grace!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise The Lord In Heaven (Psalm 150)
firstLine: Praise the Lord in Heaven
verses: praise Him in His holy place,
praise Him for His powerful deeds.
praise Him with a thousand strings,
praise and glory, Three in One.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise The Lord You Heavens
firstLine: Praise the Lord; you heavens adore Him;
verses: Praise the Lord; you heavens adore Him;
Praise Him, angels in the height;
Sun and moon rejoice before Him,
Praise Him all you stars and light,
Praise the Lord for He has spoken;
Laws which never shall be broken
for their guidance He has made.
Praise the Lord, for He is glorious:
God has made His Saints victorious;
Sin and death shall not prevail,
Praise the God of our salvation,
Hosts on high his power proclaim;
Heaven and earth and all creation,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise To The Holiest
firstLine: Praise to the holiest in the height
verses: Praise to the holiest in the height
And in the depth be praised,
In all his words most wonderful,
Most sure in all his ways.
O loving wisdom of our God!
When all was sin and shame,
A second Adam to the fight
O wisest love! that flesh and blood
Should strive afresh against their foe,
And that a higher gift than grace
God's presence and his very self,
O generous love! that he who smote
In man for man the foe,
And on the cross on high,
Should teach his brethren, and inspire
Praise to the holiest in the height
And in the depth be praise,
In all his words most wonderful,
Most sure in all his ways.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise To The Lord
firstLine: Praise to the Lord, the Almighty
verses: Praise to the Lord, the Almighty
O my soul, praise him for he is
All you who hear now to his presence draw near
Praise to the Lord, let us offer our gifts at the altar
Let not our sins and offences now cause us to falter
Christ, the High Priest, bids us all join in his feast
Victims with him on the altar.
Praise to the Lord, O let all that is in us adore him,
All that has life and breath comes now rejoicing before him,
Let the Amen sound from his people again
As we here worship before him.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Praise We Our God With Joy
firstLine: Praise we our God with joy,
verses: Praise we our God with joy,
The bonds of sin doth break,
In words of peace doth speak.
From that pure fount are welling,
Where, in our heart of hearts,
Our God hath set his dwelling,
Our law, our life, his will.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Precious Promise
firstLine: Precious promise God hath given
verses: On the way from earth to heaven,
" I will guide thee with Mine eye."
I will guide thee, I will guide thee,
I will guide thee with Mine eye;
On the way from earth to heaven,
I will guide thee with Mine eye.
Let this promise ring within thee:
" I will guide thee with Mine eye."
When thy secret hopes have perished
In the grave of years gone by,
Let this promise still be cherished
" I will guide thee with Mine eye."
When the shades of life are falling,
And the hour has come to die,
" I will guide thee with Mine eye."
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Priestly People
firstLine: Key Bb: 4/4
verses: Priestly people, kingly people Holy people,
God's chosen people Sing praise to the Lord.
We sing to you oh Christ beloved son of the father,
we give you praise O wisdom everlasting and word of God.
We sing to you, O son, born of Mary the virgin,
we give you praise, our brother born, to heal us our saving Lord.
We sing to you, o brightness of splendor and glory,
we give you praise, O morning star announcing the coming day.
We sing to you, o light bringing men out of darkness,
we give you Praise, o guiding light, who show us the way to heaven.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Queen Of Heaven
firstLine: Queen of heaven, rejoice,
verses: For the Lord whom you were worthy to bear,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Regina Caeli
firstLine: Regina caeli, laetare, Alleluia!
verses: Gaude et laetare Virgo Maria, Alleluia!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Requiem
firstLine: Requiem aeternam dona eis, Domine,
verses: Te decet hymnus, Deus in Sion,
et tibi reddetur votum in Jerusalem:
exaudi orationem meam, ad te omnis caro veniet.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Rock Of Ages
firstLine: "Rock of ages, cleft for me,
verses: Let me hide myself in Thee
Let the Water and the Blood,
From Thy riven side which flowed,
Be of sin the double cure,
Save me from its guilt and power.
Not the labour of my hands
Could my zeal no respite know,
Could my tears for ever flow,
All for sin could not atone:
Thou must save, and Thou alone.
Nothing in my hand I bring,
Simply to The Cross I cling!
Naked, come to Thee for dress;
Helpless, look to Thee for grace;
Foul, I to the fountain fly;
Wash me, Saviour, or I die.
While I draw this fleeting breath,
When my eyelids close in death,
When I soar to worlds unknown,
See Thee on Thy judgment throne,
Rock of ages, cleft for me,
Let me hide myself in Thee.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Rorate Caeli
firstLine: Antiphon:
verses: Ecce civitas Sancti facta est deserta:
Domus sanctificationis tuae et gloriae tuae,
Et iniquitates nostrae Quasi ventus abstulerunt nos:
Et allisisti nos in manu iniquitatis nostrae.
De Petra deserti ad montem filliae Sion:
Ut auferat ipse jugum captivitis nostrae.
Quia innovavit te dolor? Salvabo te, noli timere.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Now What Will You Do With Your Ointment So Rich And So Rare
firstLine: Said Judas to Mary, "Now what will you do
verses: Said Judas to Mary, "Now what will you do
with your ointment so rich and so rare?"
"I'll pour it all over the feet of the Lord
and I'll wipe it away with my hair," she said,
"Oh Mary, Oh Mary, oh think of the poor --
this ointment, it could have been sold,
and think of the blankets and think of the bread
you could buy with the silver and gold," he said,
"Tomorrow, tomorrow I'll think of the poor
for dearer than all of the poor in the world
is my love who is going away," she said,
Said Jesus to Mary, "Your love is so deep
today you may do as you will.
Tomorrow you say I am going away,
but my body I leave with you still," he said,
"my body I leave with you still."
"The poor of the world are my body,' he said,
"to the end of the world they shall be,
the bread and the blankets you give to the poor
you'll know you have given to me," he said,
"you'll know you have given to me."
"My body will hang on the cross of the world
and Martha and Mary will find me again
and wash all the sorrow away," he said,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sachanum Chineke N'ime Njo Mo
firstLine: Key F: 4/4
verses: Sachanum chineke nna, n'abum nwa gi
chineke nna mo(ngi kam nario) sachanu mo
Dinwenu bata - bata, bata n'obi mo
so ngi n'enye mu ndu biko bata Chineke di mma.
A guu gi na-agu mkpuru obi mo
A guu gi n'agu mkpuru obi mo dika nri
Onyekere uwa sachanumo, (sacham na njo mo)
Onye ker'uwa sachanumo, n'agbu nke njo
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Salve Mater Misericordiae
firstLine: Salve Mater misericodiae,
verses: Mater plena sanctae laetitiae, O Maria.
Et altius sedes in superis, O Maria.
Nam qui sedet in Patris dextera,
Intra tua se clausit viscera, O Maria.
Tu es facta tota divinitus, O Maria.
Tibi numquam fecit consimilem, O Maria.
Et nos tandem post hoc exsilium,
Laetos iunge choris caelestium, O Maria.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Salve Regina
firstLine: Salve, Regina, Mater misericordiae;
verses: Vita, dulcedo, et spes nostra, salve.
Ad te clamamus, exsules filii havae,
Et Iesum, benedictum fructum ventris tui,
O clemens, O pia, dulcis Virgo Maria.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sanctus
firstLine: Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus Dominus Deus Sabaoth.
verses: Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus Dominus Deus Sabaoth.
Pleni sunt caeli et terra gloria tua.
Benedictus qui venit in nomine Domini.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: See Amid The Winter's Snow
firstLine: Hail thou ever-blessed morn,
verses: Born for us on earth below,
He, who built the starry skies,
He, who throned in height sublime,
Wherefore have ye left your sheep,
As we watched at dead of night,
Lo, we saw a wondrous light,
To ld us of the Saviour's birth."
What a tender love was thine,
Thus to come from highest bliss,
Down to such a world as this.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: See The Church
firstLine: See the Church assembled here,
verses: See the Church in common prayer,
See the Church in common prayer,
Christ the Lord, our common meal,
Food for every one of us.
Lord, make us one with him
Lord make us one with him.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: See The Loving Shepherd Of Thy Sheep
firstLine: See the loving shepherd of thy sheep,
verses: See the loving shepherd of thy sheep,
The holy, the holy bread of angels,
From heaven, from heaven He came to earth,
He is our true Father all the time,
The shepherd, the shepherd of thy sheep,
In Him, in Him we place our soul's redemption,
We are victims of our wretchedness,
O Lord, O Lord, our Saviour,
Have mercy, have mercy on us all,
Stay with us, stay with us in this world,
So that we, so that we may be with you,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: See The Paraclete Descending
firstLine: See the Paraclete descending,
verses: Grace and truth on Him attending,
Show themselves in Christ new born.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Send Forth By God's Blessing
firstLine: Sent forth by God's blessing,
verses: The People of God from his dwelling take leave,
God's sacrifice ended, O now be extended;
The fruit of this Mass in all hearts who believe,
Shall blossom in action for God and for man.
To further his kingdom by God's holy plan.
The tasks of our everyday life we embrace.
Our faith ever sharing, in love ever caring;
We claim as our brothers all men of each race.
One Bread that has fed us,
one light that has led us,
Unite us as one in His life that we share
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Shout From The Highest Mountain
firstLine: Shout from the highest mountain
verses: Let all men rejoice in him.
Let all men know the wonders of our God.
For all the good things the Lord has done for
us let us join now in song.
For all the birds in the trees and the sky and all nature as well.
For all the children who play in the fields:
For all the people who follow his ways, those
And for our country that keeps us in peace:
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Silent Night Holy Night
firstLine: Silent night, holy night,
verses: All is calm, all is bright,
Holy infant, so tender and mild,
Son of God, love's pure light,
Radiant beams from your holy face,
With the dawn of redeeming grace,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sing A New Song (Psalm 96)
firstLine: Sing a new song to the Lord of the world,
verses: Sing a new song to the Lord of the world,
Speak of his glory and power.
Go and proclaim all the wonders of God
Tell of his deeds to all men.
Sing a new song to the Lord of the world,
God is alone to be praised,
God the great maker of heaven and earth
Sing a new song to the Lord of the world,
Praise to the name of the Lord.
Enter his temple to offer your gifts
Sing a new song to the Lord of the world,
Ocean and earth sing His praise,
God will protect all mankind with His love,
Sing a new song to the Lord of the world,
Praise to the name of the Lord,
Father and Son and the Spirit of Light,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sing Praise To The Living God
firstLine: Sing praise to the living God, Glory
verses: Sing praise to the living God, Glory
Come adore the living God, Glory Halleluia,
Though sun and moon may pass away, His word will ever stay
His power is forevermore, Glory Halleluia.
Glory to the Trinity, the undivided unity.
The Father; Son and Spirit One,
From whom all life and goodness come.
To the living God we sing, Glory,
Let our love and praises ring, Glory, Halleluia.
To His sons, He always gives His mercy and His love.
Praise Him now forevermore, Glory, Halleluia.
To the God who cannot die, Glory, Halleluia
To the living God we cry, Glory, Halleluia
He promised to be with us and He lives in everyone
Love Him now forevermore, Glory, Halleluia.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sing Them Over Again To Me
firstLine: Sing them over again to me,
verses: Sing them over again to me,
Let me more of their beauty see,
Christ, the blessed one, gives to all
Sinner, list to the loving call,
Offer pardon and peace to all,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sing To God All People (Psalm 100)
firstLine: Sing to God all people,
verses: Praise him, serve him, bless him,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sing With Joy The Saviour's Glory
firstLine: Men and angels, sing in chorus
verses: Men and angels, sing in chorus
Praise our God whom here before us
In the sacred host we adore.
Sing with joy the Saviour's glory
Of his flesh the myst'ry sing;
Of the blood all price exceeding,
On the night of his last supper,
He as food to all his brethren
Gave himself with his own hand.
Let our hearts his gift revere;
Faith its aid to vision lending,
Tell that he unseen is here.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sleep Holy Babe
firstLine: Sleep Holy Babe,
verses: Great Lord of earth and sea and sky,
How sweet it is to see Thee lie
In such a place of rest.
All bending low with folded wings,
Before th' Incarnate King of Kings,
In joy upon that face awhile,
To o quickly will thy slumbers break
And thou to lengthened pains awake,
O Lady blest, Sweet Virgin hear my cry;
Forgive the wrong that I have done
To Thee, in causing thy dear Son
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: So Gi Bu Onye M Nwere
firstLine: Key A: 4/4
verses: So gi bu Onye m nwere,
Chineke nna lezie nwa gi anya,
Chukwu m'ekwela k'uwa merie mo. DC
We-re muo nso gi kpalite obi mo.
n'amalite Chukwu meriri onwu na ekwensu bia
Chetakwa n'ebili mmiri n'kuwa ka n'etiriri
Chukwu dajuru ebili mmiri o bia o.
Abu m nwa gi Chineke chebe mo
Igbara pumo ogini ka m g'eme.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sole Hope Of All The World
firstLine: Sole hope of all the world and Lord,
verses: Sole hope of all the world and Lord,
Receive the prayers thy servants raise
Mixed with psalms and chants of praise.
And though our conscience doth proclaim,
Our deep transgressions and our shame,
Cleanse us, O God, we humbly plead,
From sins of thought and word and deed.
Our sins remember thou no more,
So take upon thee Lord, our care,
That pure in heart we make our prayer.
Therefore accept, O Lord, this tide
Of fact which thou hast sanctified,
That we may reach by mystic ways,
May he who is the threefold Lord
On us confer this high reward,
In whom so long as world abide
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sons Of God Hear His Holy Word
firstLine: Sons of God, hear his holy word!
verses: Sons of God, hear his holy word!
Gather round the table of the Lord!
Eat His Body, drink His Blood,
And we'll sing a song of love:
And our life has just begun;
In the Spirit we are young:
That we love our fellow man
Till we reach the promised land,
If we want to live with him,
We must also die with him,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Soul Of Jesus Sanctify Me
firstLine: (Anima Christi)
verses: Water from the side of Jesus, wash me.
Suffer me not to be separated from Thee.
From the malignant enemy defend me,
In the hour of my death call me,
And bid me come unto Thee,
That with Thy saints I may praise Thee
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Soul Of My Saviour
firstLine: "Soul of my Saviour, sanctify my breast;
verses: "Soul of my Saviour, sanctify my breast;
Body of Christ, be Thou my saving Guest;
Blood of my saviour, bathe me in thy tide;
Wash me with waters, flowing from thy side.
Strength and protection, may thy passion be,
O Blessed Jesus, hear and answer me.
Deep in thy wounds Lord, hide and shelter me,
So shall I never, never part from Thee.
Guard and defend me, from the foe malign;
In death's dread moments, make me only thine;
Call me and bid me, come to Thee on high.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Bringing In The Sheaves
firstLine: Sowing in the morning, sowing seeds of kindness
verses: Sowing in the morning, sowing seeds of kindness
Sowing in the noontide and the dewy eves,
Waiting for the harvest and the time of reaping,
We shall come rejoicing, bringing in the sheaves.
Bringing in the sheaves, bringing in the sheaves,
We shall come rejoicing, bringing in the sheaves.
Bringing in the sheaves, bringing in the sheaves,
We shall come rejoicing, bringing in the sheaves.
Sowing in the sunshine, sowing in the shadows,
Fearing neither clouds nor winter's chilling breeze,
By and by the harvest and the labour ended,
We shall come rejoicing, bringing in the sheaves.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Spirit Of God In The Clear Running Water
firstLine: Spirit of God in the clear running water,
verses: Spirit of God in the clear running water,
Blowing to greatness the trees on the hill,
Spirit of God in the finger of morning.
Fill the earth, bring it to birth and blow where you will,
Blow, blow, blow, till I be but the breath of the Spirit blowing in me.
I saw the scar of a year that lay dying,
Hear the lament of a lone whip-poor-will,
Spirit of God, see that cloud crying,
Fill the earth, bring it to birth and blow where you will,
Blow, blow, blow, till I be but the breath of the Spirit blowing in me.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Stand Up And Sing (Psalm 148)
firstLine: Stand up and sing to the Lord, we're gathered to worship
verses: Stand up and sing to the Lord, we're gathered to worship
Lift up your heart and be not afraid.
We 've come together to share his love.
Stand up and sing to the Lord.
Praise the Lord from the heavens, praise him in the heights:
Praise him all you his angels, praise him all you his hosts:
Praise him sun and moon; Praise him all you shining stars.
Praise him you highest heavens, and you waters above the heavens
Let them praise the name of the Lord
He established them forever and ever;
He gave them a duty which shall not pass away.
Praise the Lord from the earth;
You sea monster and all depths; fire and hail,
snow and mist, storm winds that fulfil his word;
You mountains and all you hills, you fruit trees and all you cedars;
You wild beast and all tame animals, you creeping things and you winged fowl.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Stand Up My Friends Let's Go And Offer
firstLine: Stand up my friends let's go and offer,
verses: Stand up my friends let's go and offer,
Offer our gifts to the Father above
Father, take our offerings, take our offerings, take our offerings
Father, take the poor offerings which we bring
Do not mind, do not mind
Do not mind as our gifts are so small
We offer them, we offer them
We offer them together with our hearts
Receive this bread, receive this bread
Receive this bread which we have given
Receive this wine, receive this wine.
Receive the wine which we have given.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Stand Up Stand Up For Jesus!
firstLine: Stand up! stand up for Jesus!
verses: Stand up! stand up for Jesus!
Stand up! stand up for Jesus!
Ye that are men, now serve Him
Stand up! stand up for Jesus!
The arm of flesh will fail you
Stand up! stand up for Jesus!
The strife will not be long,
This day the noise of battle,
A crown of life shall be;
He with the King of glory
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Standing On The Promises Of God
firstLine: Standing on the promises of Christ my King,
verses: Standing on the promises of Christ my King,
Through eternal ages let His praises ring;
Glory in the highest, I will shout and sing,
Standing on the promises of God.
Standing on the promises of God my Saviour;
I'm standing on the promises of God.
Standing on the promises cannot fail,
When the howling storms of doubt and fear assail,
By the living Word of God I shall prevail,
Standing on the promises of God.
Standing on the promises I now can see
Prefect, present cleansing in the blood for me;
Standing in the liberty where Christ makes free,
Standing on the promises of God.
Standing on the promises of Christ the Lord,
Bound to Him eternally by love's strong word
Overcoming daily with the Spirit's sword,
Standing on the promises of God.
Standing on the promises I cannot fall,
Listening every moment to the Spirit's call,
Resting in my Saviour as my all in all,
Standing on the promises of God.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sweet Heart Of Jesus
firstLine: Sweet Heart of Jesus,
verses: Sweet heart of Jesus we implore,
Oh, make us love Thee more and more.
To follow close The print of
Thy dear footsteps, And when we fall,
Sweet Heart, oh love us still.
Bless all hearts that love Thee,
And bless the friends we cherish,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sweet Sacrament Divine
firstLine: "Sweet Sacrament divine,
verses: Jesus, to you our voice we raise,
In songs of love and heartfelt praise,
There in your ear, all trustfully
We tell our tale of misery
Soon may we reach the shore,
Save us for still the tempest raves;
Save, lest we sink beneath the waves;
In thy far depths doth shine
Sweet light, so shine on us, we pray
That earthly joys may fade away,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Sweet Saviour Bless Us Ere We Go
firstLine: Sweet Saviour bless us ere we go,
verses: Sweet Saviour bless us ere we go,
Thy word into our minds instill,
And make our lukewarm hearts to glow
With lowly love and fervent will.
Through life's long day and death's dark night,
O gentle Jesus, be our light.
The day is done, its hours have run,
And thou hast taken count of all,
The scanty triumphs grace has won
The broken vow, the frequent fall.
Grant us, dear Lord, from evil ways
And bless us more than in past days
Do more than pardon, give us joy,
That only long to be like thee.
Labour is sweet, for thou hast toiled,
And care is light for thou hast cared,
Let not our words with self be soiled,
For all we love - the poor, the sad,
The sinful - unto these we call,
Oh let thy mercy make us glad,
Thou art our Jesus and our all.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Take And Sanctify For Your Ho-nour
firstLine: Take and sanctify
verses: All that I am, all that I do
Take my life and take my all
All that I am, all that I do
Take my life and take my all
Every thing I will ever have.
All that I dream, all that I crave
Take my life and take my all
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Take Me Deeper In Love With You
firstLine: Take Deeper in Love with you Jesus
verses: Take Deeper in Love with you Jesus
Hold me Closer in Love with you Jesus
Lord there is a longing only you can fill
A raging tempest only you can still
My souls is thirsting for you my God
Yes from sunrise to sunrise I seek your face
From day to day I long for your touch
To hold my hand Lord lest I fall
To draw me nearer, closer to thee….
In your love is found great faithfulness
Your love is filled with hope and peace
Your love is filled with compassion and joy
O teach me Lord to love you back……..
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Take My Hands And Make Them As Your Own
firstLine: Take my hands and make them as your own,
verses: Take my hands and make them as your own,
and use them for your kingdom here on earth,
Anoint them for your service where
you may need your gospel to be sown.
Take my hands, they speak now for my heart
and by their actions they will show their love,
Guard them on their daily course,
Be their strength and guiding force
to ever serve the Trinity above.
Take my hands, I give them to you, Lord,
Prepare them for the service of your name,
And by their love they'll sow your seed,
so all may know the love and hope you gave.
Take my hands, take my hands, O Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Take Our Bread We Ask You
firstLine: Take our bread, we ask you,
verses: Take our bread, we ask you,
Take our hearts, we love you;
Yours as we stand at the table you set;
Yours as we eat the bread our hearts can't forget,
We are a sign of your life with us yet,
We are yours, we are yours.
Your holy people standing washed in your blood,
Spirit filled yet hungry we await your food,
We are poor, but we've brought ourselves the best we could;
We are yours, we are yours.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Tantum Ergo Sacramentum
firstLine: Tantum ergo Sacramentum,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Taste And See That The Lord Is Good!
firstLine: Taste and see that the Lord is good!
verses: Taste and see that the Lord is good!
Happy is the man who trusts in Him!
Sing to the Lord with me,
In chorus let us praise his name,
Look to him and be filled with joy,
For the Lord's blessings come over his people.
If you would have eternal life,
Turn from evil, seek joy, and peace,
For the Lord looks with favour on all just men.
Call on the Lord in grief,
For he is near the crushed of heart,
But the Lord saves his people from all evil.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Te Deum Laudamus
firstLine: Te Deum laudamus,
verses: venerandum tuum verum et unicum Filium;
Te ergo quaesumus, tuis famulis subveni,
Et rege eos, et extolle illos
Fiat misericordia tua, Domine, super nos,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ten Unclean And Nowhere To Go
firstLine: Ten unclean and nowhere to go,
verses: Ten unclean and nowhere to go,
Ten men cleansed as clean as snow,
One returned to give God thanks,
Ten men, lepers in a Hebrew town,
Ten crying: "Lord, won't you please come down"
No hope near, till one fine day,
Jesus of Nazareth passed that way.
See, how the whole world passed us by,
No man's home will take us in!
"Then Christ bent to touch their skin.
Like a tree when its buds come true,
Or a patch of spring that is fresh and new,
Gave them the flesh of a new-born child.
God gives gifts to us every day,
Favours His people in every way;
Do you ever give thanks for a gift received?
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Thank You For Giving Me The Morning
firstLine: Thank you for giving me the morning,
verses: Thank you for giving me the morning,
Thank you for every day that 's new,
Thank you that I can know my worries
Thank you for all my brothers and sisters,
Thank you for all the men that live,
Thank you for even greatest enemies,
Thank you I have my occupation,
Thank you for every pleasure small,
Thank you for music, light and gladness,
Thank you for many little sorrows,
Thank you for every kindly word,
Thank you that everywhere your guidance
Thank you I know your Spirit here,
Thank you because you love all people,
Thank you, O Lord, you spoke unto us,
Thank you that for our words you care,
Thank you, O Lord, you came among us,
Thank you, O Lord, your love is boundless,
Thank you that I am full of you,
Thank you, you made me feel so glad,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Church Is Where Men Are
firstLine: " The Church is where men are,
verses: " The Church is where men are,
He's hidden behind every face we behold.
We must be on guard because
through our actions, the truth will unfold.
Let our light shine for all to see,
Since He died for us, let us live for Him
The Church is where Christ is,
And folks every where are made by the Lord,
What we do to others we do to Him,
By judging our neighbour, we spell our reward.
The Church is where men are,
It is from the fruit one discovers the tree.
We are silent witnesses, living the Mass,
By taking him with us wherever we be.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Coming Of Our God
firstLine: The coming of our God Our thought must now employ,
verses: The coming of our God Our thought must now employ,
Then let us meet him on the road
A servant's form Christ put on
Nor let thy stubborn heart despise
And summon all that are his own
For unto sin 'tis ours to die
To Christ, that set us free,
Like tribute to the Father bring,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Day Of Wrath That Day (Dies Iræ Dies Illa)
firstLine: The day of wrath, that day
verses: The day of wrath, that day
Will dissolve the world in ashes
As foretold by David and the Sibyl!
How much tremor there will be,
The trumpet, scattering a wondrous sound
through the sepulchres of the regions,
will summon all before the Throne.
The written book will be brought forth,
from which the world shall be judged.
When therefore the Judge will sit,
What am I, miserable, then to say?
when [even] the just may [only] hardly be sure?
Who freely savest those that have to be saved,
That I am the cause of Thy way:
Lest Thou lose me in that day.
Thou redeemedst [me] having suffered the Cross:
let not so much hardship be lost.
I sigh, like the guilty one:
lest I be burned up by eternal fire.
Grant me a place among the sheep,
and take me out from among the goats,
setting me on the right side.
Once the cursed have been rebuked,
Call Thou me with the blessed.
[my] heart is as crushed as the ashes:
perform the healing of mine end.
on which from the ash arises
the guilty man who is to be judged.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Earth O Lord Rejoices
firstLine: The earth, O Lord, rejoices,
verses: And bless the Lord that o*r us
Our every thought and action we offer to thee
From folly and distraction, we beg thee keep us free.
Let no profane example, no censure, no applause,
Lead us this day to trample O Lord, upon thy laws.
It pleased thee, Lord, to make us, that we may serve thee here
Let not thy grace forsake us, but keep us in thy fear.
Preserve our life, O Father, that we may serve thee still.
But let us lose it, rather, than disobey thy will.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The First Noel
firstLine: The first Noel, the angel did say,
verses: The first Noel, the angel did say,
In fields where they lay keeping their sheep,
Born is the King of Israel.
They look'd above and there saw a star,
That shone in the east beyond them afar,
And which to earth did give great light,
And so it continued by day and night.
And by the light of that same Star,
Three wise men came from country far;
To seek for a King was their intent,
And to follow the star wherever it went.
This star drew near to the north-west,
At length over Bethlehem seemed to rest,
And there it stayed by night and by day,
Right over the place where Jesus lay.
Then entered in those wise men three
And offered there in His presence,
Both gold and myrrh with frankincense.
Then let us all with one accord,
Sing praises to our heavenly Lord,
That made both heaven and earth of nought,
And with His blood mankind has bought.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Heavens Proclaim (Psalm 19)
firstLine: The works of your hands declare your glory, O God.
verses: The works of your hands declare your glory, O God.
Lord, open my lips and my
The heavens proclaim the glory of God,
and the firmament shows forth the work of his hands.
Day unto day takes up the story,
And night unto night makes known the message.
No speech, no word, no voice is heard,
Yet their b goes forth through all the earth.
Their words to the utmost bounds of the world.
There he has placed the tent for the sun;
It comes forth like a bride-groom coming from his tent
Rejoices like a champion to run its course.
At the end of the sky is the rising of the sun;
To the furthest end of the sky is its course;
There is nothing concealed from its burning heat.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Joy Of The Lord
firstLine: Key G: CHORUS 1:
verses: The joy of the Lord, our rampart and power!
Blessed are you, O merciful Lord
Praised by all men for ages to come.
O Lord on high, your glorious name,
Let all the earth proclaim without end.
Glory and power to Father and Son,
And to the Spirit, joy of our life.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The King Of Glory
firstLine: The King of glory comes, the nation rejoices.
verses: The King of glory comes, the nation rejoices.
Open the gates before him, lift up your voices.
Who is the King of glory ;
He is Emmanuel, the promised of ages.
In all of Galilee, in city or village,
He goes among his people curing their illness.
Sing then of David's Son, our Saviour and brother;
In all of Galilee was never another.
He gave his life for us, the pledge of salvation;
He took upon himself the sins of the nation.
He conquered sin and death; He truly has risen,
And he will share with us his heavenly vision.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Lord Is My Shepherd (Psalm 23)
firstLine: His goodness shall follow me always
verses: His goodness shall follow me always
to the end of my days.
He leads me by safe paths,
There is nothing I shall want.
Fresh and green are the pastures
Near restful waters he leads me
He guides me along the right path,
He is true to his name.
If I should walk in the valley of darkness,
You are there with your crook and your staff:
With these you give me comfort.
You have prepared a banquet for me
In the sight of my foes.
My head you have anointed with oil,
Surely goodness and kindness shall follow me
All the days of my life.
In the Lord's own house shall I dwell,
To the Father and Son give glory,
To God who is, who was, and who will be
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Lord’s Is The Earth (Psalm 24)
firstLine: (First 3 Verses) Seek the face of the Lord, and yearn for Him.
verses: (First 3 Verses) Seek the face of the Lord, and yearn for Him.
Open wide O you gates eternal
and let the King of glory enter.
The Lord's is the earth and its fullness,
The world and all its peoples,
It is He who set it on the sea;
On the waters He made it firm.
Who shall climb the mountain of the Lord?
Who shall stand in His holy place?
The man with clean hands and pure heart,
He shall receive blessings from the Lord
And reward from the God who saves him.
Such are the men who seek him,
Seek the face of the God of Jacob.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Lord's My Shepherd
firstLine: " The Lord's my Shepherd, I'll not want,
verses: " The Lord's my Shepherd, I'll not want,
He makes me down to lie
he leadeth me the quiet waters by.
My soul he doth restore again
And me to walk doth make
E'en for his own name sake.
Yea, though I walk through death's dark vale,
Yet will I fear no ill;
For thou art with me, and thy rod
In the presence of my foes:
My head thou dost with oil anoint
Goodness and mercy all my life
And in God's house for evermore
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Mass Is Ended
firstLine: The Mass is ended, all go in peace,
verses: The Mass is ended, all go in peace,
We must diminish and Christ increase,
We take him with us where'r we go,
That through our action his life may show.
We witness his love to everyone,
By our communion with Christ his Son,
We take the Mass to where men may be,
So Christ may shine forth for all to see.
Thanks to the Father who shows the way,
His life is with us throughout each day,
Let all our living and loving be
To praise and honour the Trinity.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Spirit Lives To Set Us Free
firstLine: The spirit lives to set us free,
verses: The spirit lives to set us free,
walking in the light of the Lord
The dead were wakened by His call,
He died in pain on Calvary,
To save the lost like you and me,
We know His death is not the end,
you and me, Walk, walk in the light.
By Jesus death our wounds are
The spirit lives in you and me
Walk, in the light His light will shine for all to see,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: The Virgin Mary Had A Baby Boy
firstLine: The Virgin Mary had a baby boy,
verses: The Virgin Mary had a baby boy,
The Virgin Mary had a baby boy,
The Virgin Mary had a baby boy,
And they say that His name was Jesus.
He come from the glorious kingdom.
He come from the glorious kingdom.
Oh yes, believer! Oh yes, believer!
He come from the glorious kingdom.
The angels sang when the baby born,
The angels sang when the baby born,
The angels sang when the baby born,
And proclaimed Him the Saviour Jesus.
The shepherds came where the baby born,
The shepherds came where the baby born,
The shepherds came where the baby born,
And they say that His name was Jesus.
The Wise Men saw where the baby born,
The Wise Men saw where the baby born,
The Wise Men saw where the baby born,
And they say that His name was Jesus.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: There Is A Home Eternal
firstLine: There is a home eternal,
verses: When, oh when shall I see thee
How they swell the glad Anthem
When, oh when shall I see thee
Soon shall I join the ransomed
Why should I fear to die?
Soon my eyes shall behold him
Then, oh then shall I see thee
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: There Shall Be Showers Of Blessing
firstLine: There shall be showers of blessing:
verses: There shall be showers of blessing:
This is the promise of love;
But for the showers we plead.
There shall be showers of blessing—
Over the hills and the valleys,
There shall be showers of blessing;
Send them upon us, O Lord!
Grant to us now a refreshing;
Come, and now honor Thy Word.
There shall be showers of blessing;
O that today they might fall,
Now as to God we’re confessing,
Now as on Jesus we call!
There shall be showers of blessing,
If we but trust and obey;
If we let God have His way.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: There Was A Man In Jerico Called Zaccheus
firstLine: There was a man in Jerico called Zaccheus,
verses: There was a man in Jerico called Zaccheus,
There was a man in Jerico called Zaccheus,
Now the Hebrews, they were tall,
The Lord went walking one day
And the people began to gather
So he climbed a sycamore tree,
And the Lord looked up and said:
I am dining with you today,
Zaccheus, I come to your house,
But the people began to sneer,
Does the Lord seek lodging here?"
Now Zaccheus was small of stature,
That a man who is stout of heart can grow and grow:
"If I have cheated young and old,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: There's A Call Comes Ringing
firstLine: "There's a call comes ringing o'r the restless wave
verses: "There's a call comes ringing o'r the restless wave
Send the light, send the light!...
Send the light... send the light!...
Send the light, the blessed gospel light!
Let it shine, from shore to shore!
Send the light... the blessed gospel light!
We have heard the Macedonia call today,
Send the light... send the light!
And our grateful offerings at the cross we lay
Send the light... send the light!
May the grace of Jesus unto all abound,
Send the light... send the light!
And a Christ-like Spirit everywhere be found
Send the light... send the light!
Let us not grow weary, in the work of love
Send the light, send the light!
While we gather jewels for our crown above
Send the light... send the light!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: There's Not A Friend Like The Lowly Jesus
firstLine: There's not a Friend like the lowly Jesus:
verses: There's not a Friend like the lowly Jesus:
No, not one! no, not one!
None else could heal all our souls' diseases:
No, not one! no, not one!
Jesus knows all about our struggles;
He will guide till the day is done:
There's not a Friend like the lowly Jesus:
No, not one! no, not one!
No friend like Him is so high and holy,
No, not one! no, not one!
And yet no friend is so meek and lowly,
No, not one! no, not one!
There's not an hour that He is not near us,
No, not one! no, not one!
No night so dark, but His love can cheer us,
No, not one! no, not one!
Did ever saint find this Friend forsake him?
No, not one! no, not one!
Or sinner find that He would not take him?
No, not one! no, not one!
Was e'er a gift like the Savior given?
No, not one! no, not one!
Will He refuse us a home in heaven?
No, not one! no, not one!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: This Is My Body Given For Your Freedom
firstLine: This is my Body, given for your freedom.
verses: This is my Body, given for your freedom.
This is my Blood which was shed for all mankind.
Take, all, and eat, till the day of my return.
Put your sorrows in my hands.
As I once led you to freedom, Come now to my Father's house.
On your journey to the kingdom,
You will find the strength you need,
Of my rising from the dead.
Hear, my flock, now hear me calling
For I know you all by name.
Come with me the Blessed Shepherd,
When the whole world shakes with terror,
When the sky bursts into flame,
On the clouds shall I appear,
And you shall know my hour has come.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: This Is My Will My One Command
firstLine: This is my will, my one command,
verses: This is my will, my one command,
That love should dwell among you all,
This is my will that you should love,
As I have shown that I love you.
No greater love a man can have
Than that he die to save his friends
You are my friends if you obey,
All I command that you should do.
I call you now no longer slaves,
No slave knows all his master does
I call you friends, for all I hear,
My Father say you hear from me.
You choose not me, but I chose you
That you should go and bear much fruit
I called you out that in me,
Should bear much fruit that will abide.
All that you ask My Father dear,
For my name's sake you shall receive
This is my will my one command
That love should dwell in each in all.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: This Is The Day
firstLine: This is the day that the Lord has made;
verses: This is the day that the Lord has made;
Let us be glad and rejoice in it!
Let us be glad and rejoice!
This is the day that the Lord has made.CHORUS: 1:
Alleluia is our song of joy!
Sing the song we will sing forever:
This is the day when the Lord arose;
Let us be glad and rejoice in it!
Let us be glad and rejoice!
This is the day when the Lord arose.
This is the day when we rise with Him;
Let us be glad and rejoice in it!
Let us be glad and rejoice!
This is the day when we rise with Him!
This is the day all the world is new;
Let us be glad and rejoice in it!
Let us be glad and rejoice!
This is the day all the world is new!
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: This World Is Not My Home I'm Just A Passing Through
firstLine: This world is not my home I'm just a passing through,
verses: This world is not my home I'm just a passing through,
My treasures are laid up somewhere beyond the blue,
The angels beckon me from heaven's open door,
And I can't feel at home in this world anymore.
Oh lord you know I have no friend like you,
If heaven's not my home, then Lord what will I do.
The angels beckon me from heaven's open door,
And I can't feel at home in this world anymore.
I have a loving mother just over in glory land,
And I don't expect to stop until I shake her hand.
She's waiting now for me in heaven's open door,
And I can't feel at home in this world anymore.
Just over in glory land, we'll live eternally;
The saints on every hand are shouting victory.
Their songs of sweetest praise, drift back from heaven's shore;
And I can't feel at home In this world anymore.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Thy Kingdom Come Yea Bid It Come
firstLine: Thy kingdom come, yea, bid it come,
verses: Thy kingdom come, yea, bid it come,
But, when thy kingdom first began
On earth, thy kingdom was a home,
A child, a woman, and a man.
The child was in the midst thereof,
The center and the fount of love,
Wherever on this earth shall be
A child, a woman, and a man,
And o'r that Trinity of love
Send down, as in thy appointed day,
The brooding spirit of thy Dove.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: To Christ The Prince Of Peace
firstLine: To Christ, the prince of Peace,
verses: To Christ, the prince of Peace,
And Son of God most high,
The Father of the world to come
Deep in his heart for us
The wound of love he bore;
That love where with he still in flames
Could these constrain to open thus
Hide us in thy dear heart,
There seek thy grace through life,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: To God Be The Glory Great Things He Hath Done
firstLine: To God be the glory great things He hath done;
verses: To God be the glory great things He hath done;
So loved Him the world that He gave us His Son,
Who yielded His life an atonement for sin,
And opened the life-gate that all may go in.
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord,
Let the earth hear His voice!
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord,
O come to the Father through Jesus the Son
And give Him the glory great things He hath done!
O Perfect redemption, the purchase of blood,
To every believer the promise of God;
The vilest offender who truly repents,
That moment from Jesus a pardon receives.
Great things He hath taught us great things He hath done,
And great our rejoicing through Jesus the Son,
But purer, and higher, and greater will be
Our wonder, our transport, Jesus we see.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: To Jesus' Heart All Burning With Fervent Love For All
firstLine: To Jesus' heart, all burning
verses: O Heart, for me on fire
With love no man can speak,
God gives me for thy sake.
To o true, I have forsaken
Yet now let me be taken
As thou art meek and lowly,
So may my heart be wholly
And earth's false glare is done;
I'll say I'm all thine own.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: To Thee, O Heart Of Jesus
firstLine: To thee, O Heart of Jesus,
verses: To thee, O Heart of Jesus,
To Thee our hearts we give,
Help, help us all to love Thee,
And serve Thee while we live.
Yes, yes till life is o'er,
No heart can be so tender,
No heart can love like Thee:
Thy life's blood all, O Jesus,
Was shed to set us free.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: To Win My Heart With Visions Bright And Fair
firstLine: To win my heart with visions bright and fair,
verses: To win my heart with visions bright and fair,
In vain the world with all its craft has tried;
Harmless and weak its dazzling weapons are,
I nothing fear with Jesus at my side.
And heavenly hosts, that swell the glad refrain,
In that Mary land, that Mary land.
And mother Mary, Queen of heaven shall reign,
And mother Mary, Queen of heaven shall reign.
Come, all ye proud ones of the earth,
Array your gathering hosts around me far and wide:
My heart is calm amid the loud affray,
I do not fear with Jesus at my side.
Death hath for me no fear; its bitter pains
Shall never from my King my heart divide;
Faithful to him till death my will remains,
I do not fear with Jesus at my side.
Though all the terrors of the last dread day,
With death and hell together were allied,
Though Heaven and earth before me fled away,
I do not fear with Jesus at my side.
Jesus my Lord, my only hope and shield,
No power of ill before Thee can abide;
I trust in Thee upon the battle field,
I do not fear with Jesus at my side.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Today He's Risen From The Dead
firstLine: To day He's risen, death no more shall bind him to the grave,
verses: To day He's risen, death no more shall bind him to the grave,
No more can hell or sin's foul power o'er him dominion have,
He likened to our sinful form, Once doom'd himself to die,
That he by death might death o'ercome,
O death: where's now your moral sting,
Taught us to triumph in our turn,
I know that my Redeemer lives,
He will revive my dust again,
Then clothed in my own glorious flesh,
That sweet hope in my bosom glows,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Turn Up Christians Come Offer Your Gifts
firstLine: Turn up Christians
verses: Come offer your gifts to God's altar, (I want to go to heaven in the morn-morning)
Bring your gifts to God's altar
Offer Him gifts of bread and wine
Take this bread and take this wine
Change them to Your risen self
Bring your gifts to God's altar
Offer Him your gifts and your hearts.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Unto Us Is Born A Son
firstLine: Unto us is born a Son,
verses: Unto us is born a Son,
See on earth His life begun,
Ox and ass their Owner know
So he gave the word to slay,
Of his love and mercy mild
And O that Mary's gentle Child
Might lead us up to glory !
O and A and A and O.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Veni, Creator Spiritus
firstLine: Veni, Creator Spiritus,
verses: V. Emitte spiritum tuum et creabuntur.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Ven I Jesu Amor Mi
firstLine: Veni Jesu Amor Mi,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Vexilla Regis
firstLine: Vexilla Regis prodeunt:
verses: Ornata Regis purpura, Electa digno stipite
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Vidi Aquam Egredientem De Témplo
firstLine: Vidi áquam egredientem de témplo,
verses: et ómnes, ad quos pervénít áqua ísta,
Glória Pátri, et Fílio, et Spirítui Sáncto:
Sicut érat in princípio, et nunc, et semper,
et ómnes, ad quos pervénit áqua ísta,
Et salutáre túum da nobis. Allelúia.
Et clamor méus ad te véniat.
atque deféndat omnes habitántes in hoc habitáculo.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Are Marching To The High Altar
firstLine: We are marching to the high altar,
verses: We are marching to the high altar,
To offer our bread and wine,
To atone for the sins of the whole world,
What a beautiful day, a wonderful day,
We offer our gifts to the Lord,
There will be no more pain and no more sorrow
Let us break bread at the high altar.
For the grace of peace and unity
Let us drink wine at the high altar,
For the love of our dear brethren,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Are One In Jesus
firstLine: We are one in Jesus, all of us are one,
verses: We are one in Jesus, all of us are one,
We are one in Jesus, all of us are one,
We are one, we are one, Jesus Christ and we are one,
We are one, we are one, Jesus Christ and we are one.
We are one in Jesus, all of us are one,
All of us are brothers, brothers of our Lord,
All of us are brothers, brothers of our Lord,
We are one, we are one, brothers of our risen Lord,
We are one, we are on in the Spirit of our Lord,
All of us are brothers, brothers of our Lord.
Jesus is our brother, Christ and we are one,
Jesus is our brother, Christ and we are one,
One in mind, one in heart, in the Spirit from on high,
With our brother in our midst, never more to die,
Jesus is our brother, Christ and we are one,
We are one in Jesus, all of us are one,
All of us are one, all of us are one.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Are Your Bread Now
firstLine: We are your bread now,
verses: The bread of love are we,
The bread of love are we.
With one heart and one voice,
We sing to him we know,
In the breaking of the bread
A song of love with heart
Give us this day, O Lord,
The bread of all we do,
And all this day we are to meet,
To every table that You set,
My body and all we do,
And the thrill of a heart
That is happy just to know,
We stand here rich and poor,
I am yours and you are mine,
And we all are Christ's the Lord,
And He, O joy is God.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Bring Our Gifts, Lord
firstLine: We bring our gifts Lord, bread and wine,
verses: We bring our gifts Lord, bread and wine,
We bring our gifts Lord, bread and wine,
We bring ourselves, Lord, heart and mind,
Make us one, Lord, as we prayed,
Make us one, Lord, as we prayed,
Grant us peace, Lord, hear our prayer,
Let us love, Lord, as you loved,
Let us love, Lord, as you loved,
In this banquet, Lord, we find strength,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Fly To Thy Protection
firstLine: We fly to thy protection
verses: But from all dangers deliver us
Oh ever glorious and Blessed Virgin.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Gather Together
firstLine: We gather together,
verses: That all may grow in knowledge,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Long For You, O Lord
firstLine: " We long for you, O Lord,
verses: " We long for you, O Lord,
We long for you, O Lord,
Come, make us one with you in love,
We long for you, O Lord.
O Lord, Come, Lamb of God, and give us peace,
We hunger for you, O Lord,
We hunger for you, O Lord,
Come, feed us now with living bread,
Your flesh is strength, O Lord,
Your flesh is strength, O Lord,
Come, holy strong One, make us strong,
Your flesh is strength, O Lord.
You live in us, O Lord,
You live in us, O Lord,
To be our Way and Truth and Life,
You live in us, O Lord.
Our love of them is love of you,
You love through us, O Lord,
You love through us, O Lord,
We are your heart and hands and voice,
You love through us, O Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Long To See Thee So
firstLine: We long to see Thee so,
verses: We long to see Thee so,
The sands of time run slow.
O come, O come, O come,
O come, O come, O come,
We have no King but thee.
We long to see Thee so,
We long to see Thee so,
No other joys can please us,
The sands of time run slow.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Offer Thee The Holy Mass
firstLine: We offer thee the Holy Mass,
verses: We offer thee the Holy Mass,
To thank thee for thy gracious gifts,
And praise thy Name for evermore. Amen.
Now Jesus Christ, thy flesh and blood,
The same in Mass as on the Cross,
Though under forms of Bread and Wine.
We pray for pardon and for grace
To change the lives that we have led,
And beg thee for Thy Son's dear sake,
To bless the living and the dead.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Offer You, O Father Above
firstLine: We offer you, O Father above,
verses: We offer you, O Father above,
These earthly gifts of bread and wine,
We give ourselves within this sing,
The Lamb of God was offered for us,
For men, He gave His Body and Blood,
We offer now this gift of love,
We know we are not worthy alone
To worship at so holy a throne,
And so with Jesus Christ we pray,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Plough The Fields
firstLine: " We plough the fields, and scatter the good seed on the land,
verses: " We plough the fields, and scatter the good seed on the land,
But it is fed and water'd by God's Almighty hand;
He sends the snow in winter,
The warmth to swell the grain,
Then thank the Lord, O thank the Lord,
He only is the Maker of all things near and far;
The winds and waves obey Him,
By Him the birds are fed;
Much more to us, His Children,
We thank Thee then, O then, O Father,
For all things bright and good,
The seed time and the harvest,
Our life, our health, our food;
Accept the gifts we offer for all Thy love imparts.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Praise Thee, O God, For The Son Of Thy Love
firstLine: " We praise thee, O God,
verses: For the Son of thy love,
Who has shown us our Saviour,
To the Lamb that was slain,
Fill each soul with thy love,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Praise Thee O God (Te Deum Laudamus)
firstLine: We praise Thee, O God:
verses: we acknowledge Thee to be the Lord.
All the earth doth worship Thee
to Thee the heavens and all the Powers therein.
To Thee the Cherubim and Seraphim cry with unceasing voice:
Holy, Holy, Holy: Lord God of Hosts.
The heavens and the earth are full of the majesty of Thy glory.
Thee the glorious choir of the Apostles.
Thee the admirable company of the Prophets.
Thee the white-robed army of Martyrs praise.
doth acknowledge. The Father of infinite Majesty.
Thine adorable, true and only Son Also the Holy Ghost
Thou art the King of Glory, O Christ.
Thou art the everlasting Son of the Father.
Thou having taken upon Thee to deliver man didst not abhor the Virgin's womb.
Thou having overcome the sting of death didst open to believers the kingdom of heaven. Thou sittest at the right hand of God in the glory of the Father.
We believe that Thou shalt come to be our Judge.
We beseech Thee, therefore, help Thy servants: whom Thou has redeemed with Thy precious Blood.
Make them to be numbered with Thy Saints in glory everlasting.
and bless Thine inheritance. Govern them and lift them up forever.
Day by day we bless Thee.
And we praise Thy name forever:
Vouchsafe, O Lord, this day to keep us without sin.
Have mercy on us, O Lord: have mercy on us.
Let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us:
as we have hoped in Thee. O Lord, in Thee have I hoped: let me never be confounded.

title: We Shall Go Up Into God's House
firstLine: We shall go up into God's house
verses: We shall go up into God's house
We shall go into house of the LordBASS:
I rejoiced when I heard them say:
" Let us go to God*s house, SOPRANO:
And now our feet are standing
Jerusalem is built as a city
We shall go up into God's house with shouts of joy
We shall go into house of the Lord
I rejoiced when I heard them say:
" Let us go to God's house, "
And now our feet are standing
Jerusalem is built as a city
It is there that the tribes go up,
There to praise the Lord's name,
There were set the thrones of judgment,
For the peace of Jerusalem, pray:
May peace reign in your walls,
For love of my brethren and friends,
" For love of the house of the Lord,
I will ask for your good.
Praise the Father, the Son and Holy Spirit
The God who is, who was and is to come
At the end of the ages.

title: We Stand For God
firstLine: We stand for God, and for his glory
verses: We stand for God, and for his glory
The Lord Supreme and God of all;
Against his foes we raise his standard,
Around the Cross we hear his call.
Guard us when danger is nigh;
To you we pledge our lives and service
Strong in a trust that ne'r shall die.
To you we pledge our lives and service,
Strong in a trust that ne'r shall die.
We stand for God, let us be loyal,
Our love proclaim with every breath,
To Christ the King and Lord of Lords,
We shall be faithful unto death.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We Three Kings Of Orient
firstLine: We three kings of Orient are,
verses: We three kings of Orient are,
Field and fountain, moor and mountain,
O star of wonder, star of night,
Guide us to thy perfect light.
Born a King on Bethlehem's plain,
Gold I bring to crown Him again,
Prayer and praising, all men raising,
Myrrh is mine, its bitter perfume,
Breathes a life of gathering gloom,
Alleluia, alleluia! Earth to the heaven replies.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: We're Going With Jesus
firstLine: We 're going with Jesus, walking together,
verses: We 're going with Jesus, walking together,
Over the path he has trod;
And up every mountain, down every valley
We 're working for the Lord on earth,
The good you do will all come back
He heard his word, we know it true,
He came to save the world;
Then tell the people, all the people
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: What A Blessed Hope Is Mine
firstLine: What a blessed hope is mine,
verses: What a blessed hope is mine,
I am heir of love Divine
He will keep me to the end,
For the blessed hope of Christ,
I lay strife of this world;
Did come down to us from the Lord.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: What A Friend We Have In Jesus
firstLine: " What a Friend we have in Jesus,
verses: " What a Friend we have in Jesus,
All our sins and griefs to bear!
Oh, what peace we often forfeit,
Oh, what needless pain we bear -
All because we do not carry
Take it to the Lord in prayer.
Can we find a friend so faithful
Who will all our sorrows share?
Take it to the Lord in Prayer!
Cumbered with a load of care?
Take it to the Lord in prayer.
Do your friends despise, forsake you?
Take it to the Lord in prayer.
In His arms He'll take and shield you
You will find a solace there.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: What Can We Bring To Offer
firstLine: What can we bring to offer as our gift to You,
verses: What can we bring to offer as our gift to You,
O Lord, O God, as our gift to You?
You are full of kindness For Your sinful sons,
O Lord, O God, For Your sinful sons.
So, we offer You Jesus as our Holy Gift,
O Lord, O God, as our Holy Gift.
Take them, all of our labour, Suffering and pain,
O Lord, O God, Suffering and pain.
Take then, beauty and gladness, Heart and mind and will,
O Lord, O God, Heart and mind and will.
Bread and wine will be given, Humble food and drink,
O Lord, O God, Humble food and drink.
Symbols of human labours, Symbols of ourselves,
O Lord, O God, Symbols of ourselves.
But our Jesus will change them To His risen self,
O Lord, O God, To His risen self.
He will share with His brothers,
O Lord, O God, Life and truth and love.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: What Happiness Can Equal Mine
firstLine: " What happiness can equal mine?
verses: I have found the object of my love,
My Saviour and my Lord Divine;
Is come to me from Heaven above.
He makes my heart His own abode;
His flesh becomes my daily bread,
He pours on me His healing Blood,
And with His life my soul is fed.
My Lord is mine and I am His
In me He dwells; in Him I live
Where could I taste a purer bliss?
What greater boom could Jesus give?
Dear Jesus now my heart is Thine,
Oh, may it never from Thee flee;
My God be Thou forever mine,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: What Have We To Offer Him?
firstLine: What have we to offer him,
verses: What have we to offer him,
Who is God and Lord of all?
From the Father came the Son,
All true men are joined in him,
Who for love has saved the world.
Joined in one as God made man,
take the bread and take the wine,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Whatsoever You Do
firstLine: Whatsoever you do to the least of my brothers
verses: Whatsoever you do to the least of my brothers
When I was hungry you gave me to eat,
When I was thirsty you gave me to drink,
Now enter into the home of my Father.
When I was homeless you opened your door,
When I was naked you gave me your coat,
Now enter into the home of my Father.
When I was weary you helped me find rest,
When I was anxious you calmed all my fears,
Now enter into the home of my Father.
When I was restless, you listened and cared,
When I was lonely you gave me your love,
Now enter into the home of my Father.
When in a prison you came to my cell,
When on a sick bed, you cared for my needs,
Now enter into the home of my Father.
In a strange country you made me at home,
Seeking employment you found me a job,
Now enter into the home of my Father.
Hurt in the battle, you bound up my wounds,
Searching for kindness you held out your hand,
Now enter into the joy of my Father.
When I was laughed at, you stood at my side,
When I was happy, you shared in my joy,
Now enter into the home of my Father.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: When He Comes Back
firstLine: When he comes back, (2X)
verses: Our lamps will be burning to welcome Him
The Master has promised that He will return
On a night when there's no one expecting to see him at all,
Keep oil in the lamps so they're ready to burn
On the night of his secret, when those who are waiting he'll call.
Look not for the Master in heaven's dark space,
By the light of our living on earthwe'll discover his face,
The face of the Master is always at hand:
In the face of the stranger, the poor, in the face of a man.
This stranger will search for his home in the night;
And then how will he find it unless all the windows are bright?
But if we are waiting - why then he'll come in
And there'll be a homecoming with dancing and singing within.
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

title: Wonderful Is Your Hand I Work (Psalm 8: 1 - 4)
firstLine: When I look at the work of your hands,
verses: When I look at the work of your hands,
Lord, our God, glorious is your name,
Even the infants your power proclaim
When I look at the heavens above,
The moon and the stars you have set,
What is man that you show him love
Your dominion you let him share,
Of creatures of field and stall.
Fish of the sea and birds of the air,
source: Daily mass readings, hymns and prayers.docx

firstLine: When I remember
verses: My heart leaps for joy for
I will give You all I have,
For always Lord You know that
I have given all I had.
There was a time in life
lost Lord I am a living
of woes to joy Lord I
